project_goal.value: AI-powered protein structure prediction platform for academic research groups, enabling amino acid sequence submission and 3D folding model generation with confidence scoring
project_goal.rationale: Core capability replacing manual AlphaFold local installs with managed platform

target_users.value: Academic research scientists, bioinformaticians, and principal investigators at university labs
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery interviews with 5 research groups

project_class.rationale: AI inference pipeline with supporting web UI and batch processing

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, batch_jobs, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Full platform with ML inference, batch queue, and result visualization

complexity_level.rationale: GPU-accelerated inference pipeline with batch scheduling and 3D visualization

risk_level.rationale: Confidential research data but no PII or financial data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Unpublished research sequences are proprietary to submitting institutions

external_exposure.rationale: Access restricted to authenticated research group members

access_model.value: RBAC with researcher and PI roles; research group isolation enforced at data layer
access_model.rationale: Groups must not access each other's unpublished sequence data

feature_scope.value: Sequence submission (FASTA/manual entry), GPU folding job queue, 3D structure viewer, confidence score display, batch genomic study mode, result export in PDB and CSV formats, comparative analysis reports
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set from stakeholder workshops

mvp_scope.value: Single-sequence FASTA submission, job queue with status polling, per-residue confidence score display, PDB file download
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for pilot with 3 research groups in Q2

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, AES-256 at rest, Cognito MFA optional for researchers, mandatory for PIs, sequence data isolated per research group
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential research data protection requirements

frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; NGL viewer library available for 3D protein visualization

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: PyTorch and ESMFold integration require Python runtime

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for metadata; S3 for PDB structure files and FASTA archives
data_platform.rationale: Relational metadata with object storage for large binary outputs

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate with GPU-enabled EC2 instances for inference workers
hosting_target.rationale: Fargate for API tier; EC2 p3 instances for ESMFold inference

privacy_retention_policy.value: Sequence data retained for 2 years post-submission; deleted on researcher request; NIH data management plan retention for grant-funded studies
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: NIH data management plan requirements and institutional data governance

future_scope.value: Multi-model support (RoseTTAFold, OpenFold), protein-ligand docking integration, public structure sharing option for published work
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap based on researcher feedback

constraints.value: GPU inference budget under $5000/month; must support FASTA files up to 50MB; 24-hour SLA for batch jobs
constraints.rationale: Budget constraints and researcher workflow expectations

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for infrastructure metrics; custom job queue dashboard; alert on GPU utilization and job failure rates
observability_baseline.rationale: Platform team monitoring standard

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints with GPU worker deployments gated by load testing
execution_preference.rationale: GPU infrastructure changes require validation before production

compliance_context.value: Institutional data sharing agreements, NIH data management plan requirements, potential GDPR for EU researcher data
compliance_context.rationale: Federally funded research data governance obligations

llm_integration.value: ESMFold via self-hosted PyTorch inference service on GPU EC2; LLM used for natural language query parsing on genomic dataset search only
llm_integration.rationale: ESMFold is the primary folding model; GPT-4o used only for search query interpretation

project.goal: GPU-accelerated protein folding prediction platform with web UI for academic research groups
project.target_users: Academic researchers, bioinformaticians, and PIs

frontend.visualization: NGL viewer for 3D protein structure rendering
frontend.state: React Query for job status polling

backend.inference: ESMFold PyTorch workers on GPU EC2

security.auth: Cognito with group isolation
security.encryption: AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 for job metadata and sequence records
data.object_store: S3 for FASTA inputs and PDB outputs

devops.hosting: ECS Fargate API + EC2 p3 GPU workers
devops.environments: dev, staging, production

open_questions.gpu_scaling: Auto-scaling policy for burst genomic study batches TBD

reasoner_reviews.product: Platform addresses real pain points in research groups running ESMFold locally with inconsistent GPU hardware. Batch genomic study mode is a key differentiator for grant-funded large-scale studies. MVP scope of single-sequence submission with PDB download is achievable in Q2.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ESMFold inference is memory-intensive; p3.2xlarge with 16GB GPU RAM supports sequences up to ~700 residues. Longer sequences need p3.8xlarge. The job queue architecture must route sequences by length to appropriately sized GPU instances to avoid OOM failures. Recommend SQS with separate queues per instance type.
reasoner_reviews.security: Research group isolation is the critical security requirement. PostgreSQL row-level security policies scoped to research_group_id are the most reliable enforcement layer. Cognito JWT claims must carry group membership for RLS to activate. Ensure S3 key prefixes are group-scoped to prevent cross-group PDB file access.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GPU budget of $5000/month is achievable for moderate load using Spot instances for batch jobs and on-demand only for interactive submissions. 24-hour batch SLA is realistic with SQS queue depth monitoring and auto-scaling.
reasoner_reviews.critic: NGL viewer in React may present performance issues for very large proteins with thousands of atoms. Consider LOD rendering or pre-computed surface representations for structures over 500 residues.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with modules for sequence_submission, job_management, structure_retrieval, batch_scheduler, and group_admin. Inference workers are separate Python processes on GPU EC2 consuming SQS messages.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0. Async job pattern: POST /jobs returns job_id, GET /jobs/{id}/status polls, GET /jobs/{id}/structure downloads PDB.
backend.background_jobs: SQS-driven inference workers; Celery beat for nightly batch genomic study processing; S3 lifecycle rules for aged FASTA cleanup.
backend.scaling_notes: GPU workers auto-scale on SQS queue depth. API tier scales on CPU. Separate SQS queues for short (<300 residue) and long (>300 residue) sequences route to appropriately sized EC2 instances.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with route-based code splitting. Key routes: /submit (sequence input), /jobs (queue dashboard), /jobs/:id (structure viewer), /batch (genomic study upload), /groups (PI admin panel).
frontend.pages_and_flows: Sequence Submission form with FASTA file upload or manual paste, length validation, and research group selector. Job Dashboard with real-time status polling via React Query. Structure Viewer using NGL viewer rendering PDB files from signed S3 URLs.
frontend.performance_notes: Lazy-load NGL viewer bundle (~2MB) only on structure viewer route. Pre-signed S3 URLs expire after 1 hour. Stream large FASTA files via chunked upload to avoid browser memory limits.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with research group custom attributes. MFA required for PI role, optional for researcher role. JWT includes research_group_id claim.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: researcher can submit and view own group jobs; PI can view all group jobs, manage members, and delete jobs; admin can manage all groups.
security.privacy_controls: PostgreSQL RLS policies enforce research_group_id isolation. S3 keys prefixed with group_id. Pre-signed URL generation validates group membership before issuance.

data.entities: protein_sequences (id, research_group_id, submitter_id, sequence_text, length_aa, fasta_s3_key, submitted_at), folding_jobs (id, sequence_id, model_version, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, gpu_instance_type, started_at, completed_at), structure_models (id, job_id, pdb_s3_key, confidence_mean, per_residue_scores_json, created_at), genomic_datasets (id, group_id, name, fasta_manifest_s3_key, total_sequences, submitted_at), job_queues (id, queue_type ENUM short/long, sqs_queue_url, current_depth)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS for metadata; S3 us-east-1 with versioning for FASTA inputs and PDB outputs; Redis 7 for real-time job status cache

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster for API and web serving; Auto Scaling Group of p3 EC2 Spot instances for inference workers; SQS short-sequence and long-sequence queues; S3 for object storage; ElastiCache Redis for job status
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint (ruff), test (pytest 85% coverage gate), build Docker, push ECR, deploy API via ECS rolling update. GPU worker AMI built with Packer, deployed via ASG launch template update.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping ECS tasks and EC2 GPU workers. Grafana dashboards for job throughput, GPU utilization, queue depths, inference latency p50/p95/p99. Alerts: queue depth >100 for >15 min, GPU utilization <20% on running instance (wasted spend), job failure rate >5%.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a GPU-accelerated protein structure prediction platform where the critical architectural challenge is routing sequences to GPU instances of appropriate size (p3.2xlarge vs p3.8xlarge based on sequence length) to avoid OOM failures. ESMFold inference workers consume SQS messages asynchronously from the FastAPI API tier. Research group isolation enforced via PostgreSQL RLS using Cognito-issued group claims is the security backbone. NGL viewer lazy-loading and pre-signed S3 URL pattern keeps the frontend performant without exposing S3 directly.
target_output.title: Protein Structure Prediction Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A GPU-accelerated AI platform for academic research groups to submit amino acid sequences and receive ESMFold-predicted 3D structures with per-residue confidence scores. React 18 frontend with NGL viewer for structure visualization; FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate; SQS-driven inference workers on GPU EC2 Spot instances; PostgreSQL RLS enforcing research group data isolation; S3 for FASTA and PDB file storage.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture with a React 18 SPA communicating over HTTPS to FastAPI on ECS Fargate behind an ALB. The API tier enqueues folding jobs into SQS (separate queues for sequences under 300 residues and over 300 residues). GPU inference workers on Auto Scaling EC2 p3 Spot instances consume from their respective queues, run ESMFold PyTorch inference, write PDB output to S3, and update job status in PostgreSQL. Research group isolation enforced via PostgreSQL row-level security policies scoped by Cognito JWT group claims. All sequence data encrypted with S3 SSE-KMS and PostgreSQL pgcrypto for sensitive fields.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, NGL viewer for 3D protein rendering, React Query for job status polling, Tailwind CSS. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Boto3. Inference: ESMFold (Meta) via PyTorch 2.2 on CUDA 12, pyfastx for FASTA parsing. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (API), EC2 p3 Spot ASG (GPU workers), SQS FIFO queues, S3 SSE-KMS, PostgreSQL 16 with pgcrypto, Redis 7, Cognito user pools, ALB.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Sequence Submission: FASTA file upload (up to 50MB) or manual amino acid paste with length validation and research group scoping. Job Queue Dashboard: real-time status polling with ETA estimates based on queue depth. Structure Viewer: interactive 3D NGL rendering of PDB outputs with per-residue confidence score color mapping (pLDDT scores 0-100). Batch Genomic Study Mode: multi-FASTA manifest submission with overnight processing and aggregate confidence reporting. PDB and CSV export of structure predictions. PI Admin Panel: group member management and job history.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF (API tier ingress), ECS Fargate cluster (FastAPI API + admin workers), SQS short-sequence queue (<300aa), SQS long-sequence queue (>300aa), EC2 p3.2xlarge Spot ASG (short worker pool), EC2 p3.8xlarge Spot ASG (long worker pool), PostgreSQL 16 Aurora (job metadata with RLS), Redis 7 ElastiCache (job status cache), S3 bucket (FASTA inputs and PDB outputs, group-prefixed keys), Cognito user pool (auth), CloudFront (SPA delivery).
target_output.workflows: Sequence Submission and Inference: Researcher uploads FASTA file via React form; FastAPI validates length (<10,000 residues), checks group quota, inserts protein_sequences record, publishes message to SQS queue based on sequence length, returns job_id. Client polls GET /jobs/{id}/status every 10s via React Query. GPU worker dequeues message, loads ESMFold model, runs inference (2-45 min depending on length), writes PDB to S3 at group-prefixed key, updates folding_jobs status to completed, publishes Redis pub/sub event. Client receives status update and loads NGL viewer with pre-signed S3 URL. Batch Genomic Study Processing: Researcher uploads multi-FASTA file; batch scheduler splits into individual sequences, creates genomic_datasets record, enqueues all jobs, monitors completion, aggregates per-residue confidence scores into comparative CSV report downloadable from the batch dashboard.
target_output.data_model: protein_sequences (id UUID PK, research_group_id UUID FK, submitter_id UUID FK, sequence_text TEXT, length_aa INT, fasta_s3_key TEXT, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ) with RLS policy: research_group_id = current_setting('app.group_id'). folding_jobs (id UUID PK, sequence_id UUID FK, model_version VARCHAR, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, gpu_instance_type VARCHAR, queue_type ENUM short/long, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). structure_models (id UUID PK, job_id UUID FK, pdb_s3_key TEXT, confidence_mean FLOAT, per_residue_scores_json JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). genomic_datasets (id UUID PK, group_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR, fasta_manifest_s3_key TEXT, total_sequences INT, completed_sequences INT, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). job_queues (queue_type ENUM short/long, sqs_queue_url TEXT, current_depth INT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1. POST /sequences (upload FASTA or text, returns sequence_id). POST /jobs (submit folding job for sequence_id, returns job_id). GET /jobs/{id}/status (poll status, returns status, ETA, queue_position). GET /jobs/{id}/structure (returns pre-signed S3 URL for PDB, valid 1h). POST /batch/datasets (upload multi-FASTA, returns dataset_id). GET /batch/datasets/{id}/report (download aggregate confidence CSV). GET /jobs?group_id=&status= (PI job listing). Auth: Cognito JWT Bearer with group claim injected into PostgreSQL session variable for RLS activation. Rate limits: 10 jobs/hour per researcher, 100 jobs/hour per PI.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PostgreSQL RLS policies on protein_sequences, folding_jobs, and structure_models enforce research_group_id isolation using SET LOCAL app.group_id from JWT claims. S3 key prefix scheme: {group_id}/sequences/{sequence_id}.fasta and {group_id}/structures/{job_id}.pdb prevents cross-group access. Pre-signed URL generation validates group membership before issuance. Cognito MFA mandatory for PI role. TLS 1.3 on ALB. S3 SSE-KMS with per-group KMS keys for strongest isolation. NIH data management plan: sequence data tagged with grant_id, automated retention enforcement via S3 lifecycle rules (delete after 2 years). GDPR: EU researcher data stored in eu-west-1 replica on request.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, pytest (85% coverage gate), build API Docker image, push to ECR, deploy ECS rolling update with health check gate. GPU worker AMI built monthly via Packer (CUDA 12, PyTorch 2.2, ESMFold weights pre-loaded). ASG launches from latest AMI. Environments: dev (single AZ, on-demand GPU), staging (Spot GPU, mirrors prod), production (multi-AZ Fargate, Spot GPU ASG with on-demand fallback). SQS dead-letter queues capture failed inference jobs after 3 retries. Spot interruption handler: saves inference checkpoint to S3 and re-enqueues job on interruption signal. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager (DB credentials, KMS key ARNs). Cost controls: Spot GPU ASG scales in when SQS depth drops to zero; scheduled scale-to-zero nights/weekends for batch-only load.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scraping ECS tasks and GPU EC2 instances via CloudWatch agent. Grafana dashboards: job submission rate (jobs/hr by group), GPU utilization per instance (NVIDIA SMI metrics), SQS queue depths per queue type, inference latency histograms (p50/p95/p99), fold failure rate. Custom metric: mean per-residue confidence score distribution over last 24h (detects model degradation). Alerts via PagerDuty: job failure rate >5% over 1h, SQS depth >100 for >15 min with no GPU worker scaling, GPU utilization <20% on running instance for >30 min (wasted Spot spend), API p99 latency >5s.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $3,800/month at 500 folding jobs/day: EC2 p3.2xlarge Spot ($0.918/hr, ~8h/day avg) ~$650, p3.8xlarge Spot ($3.06/hr, ~4h/day) ~$370, ECS Fargate API ~$180, Aurora PostgreSQL ~$280, S3+data transfer ~$120, Redis ~$90. GPU ASG scales to zero when queues empty. Batch genomic studies scheduled overnight to use off-peak Spot availability. Reserved capacity for Fargate baseline saves ~15%.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Cognito auth with research group setup, single FASTA submission form, SQS-backed job queue, ESMFold inference worker on p3.2xlarge, job status polling API, NGL structure viewer with pLDDT confidence coloring, PDB download, GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Batch genomic study upload, multi-FASTA processing pipeline, aggregate confidence reports in CSV, long-sequence routing to p3.8xlarge workers, PI admin panel for group management and job oversight. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Comparative analysis report UI, sequence search by group history, GPU utilization cost dashboard for PIs, S3 lifecycle automation for NIH retention compliance.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python ruff linting enforced in CI. pytest with 85% coverage gate on API and worker code. Type annotations required on all public FastAPI route functions (Pydantic v2 models). React components use strict TypeScript; no any types in NGL viewer integration code. SQS message schema versioned with model_version field to support rolling ESMFold upgrades without message loss. Feature flags via environment variables for multi-model rollout.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: EC2 Spot interruption mid-inference loses GPU compute. Mitigation: SIGTERM handler saves ESMFold intermediate state to S3, re-enqueues job. Risk: ESMFold OOM on sequences >700aa on p3.2xlarge. Mitigation: length-based routing sends >300aa to p3.8xlarge with 64GB GPU RAM. Tradeoff: Spot vs on-demand GPU instances saves ~70% cost but adds ~5% job interruption risk. Tradeoff: Pre-loading ESMFold weights on AMI increases worker startup time from 15 min to 3 min at cost of larger AMI (~50GB). Risk: Research group isolation breach via misconfigured RLS. Mitigation: integration tests assert cross-group data access returns empty results on every deployment.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected SQS over Kafka for job queue due to simpler operational model at current scale. Confirmed PostgreSQL RLS over application-layer group filtering for stronger isolation guarantees. Chose pre-signed S3 URLs over S3 proxy endpoint to avoid egress cost on large PDB files. ESMFold selected over OpenFold for better PyPI packaging and lower infrastructure overhead.

metadata.notes: GPU inference routing by sequence length is a domain-specific architectural decision; NGL viewer integration and RLS group isolation are well-specified.

project_goal.rationale: Replace outdated HTML site maintained manually by department administrator

target_users.rationale: Public-facing portal with no authentication requirement

external_exposure.rationale: Fully public website with no access controls

access_model.rationale: All content intended for unrestricted public access

project.goal: Public chemistry department website with faculty, research, and publications content
project.target_users: Prospective students, collaborators, public

frontend.rendering: Static generation with Astro content collections

security.cookies: Analytics consent banner for EU visitors

data.content: Markdown files in /content directory versioned in git
data.publications: BibTeX file parsed at build time into publications list

devops.preview: Netlify branch deploy previews for content PRs

constraints.cms: Git-based markdown editing; no licensed CMS

reasoner_reviews.product: Scope is narrow and well-defined. Markdown-based content management is appropriate for a non-technical admin. Faculty profiles and publications list are the highest-value sections. Research area taxonomy should be defined upfront to enable consistent keyword filtering for prospective students.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Astro content collections with TypeScript schema validation are the right approach: each faculty_profile, research_project, and publication becomes a typed collection entry validated at build time. BibTeX parser at build time for publications avoids manual duplication. No client-side JavaScript needed for the core content pages.
reasoner_reviews.security: Attack surface is effectively zero on a static site. HTTPS via Netlify is automatic. Only consideration is ensuring no internal department documents are accidentally committed to the public repository. .gitignore rules and a pre-commit hook should block common sensitive file extensions.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Non-technical admin editing markdown is feasible with a good README and clear front matter schemas. Consider adding Netlify CMS (now Decap CMS) as a future option for a GUI editing experience without any backend infrastructure cost.

frontend.app_structure: Astro site with content collections for faculty_profiles, research_projects, publications, and graduate_programs. Layout components: BaseLayout, FacultyCard, ResearchAreaCard, PublicationEntry.
frontend.performance_notes: All pages statically generated at build time. Images optimized with astro:assets at build time. Zero client-side JS on content pages; cookie consent banner is only island component.

security.auth_design: No authentication. HTTPS enforced by Netlify.
security.abuse_prevention: Netlify bot protection on any form endpoints. Contact links use mailto: anchors to avoid form submission attack surface.
security.privacy_controls: Google Analytics with anonymizeIp: true. Cookie consent banner using minimal vanilla JS island. No tracking pixels or third-party widgets beyond GA.

data.entities: faculty_profiles (slug, name, title, research_areas[], bio, email, photo_path, publications_count, lab_url), research_projects (slug, title, pi_id, grant_number, funding_agency, start_year, end_year, summary), publications (doi, title, authors[], year, journal, volume, pages, abstract), lab_facilities (slug, name, description, equipment[], photo_path), graduate_programs (slug, name, degree_type, application_deadline, description)
data.schema_notes: Astro content collection schemas with Zod validation enforce required fields at build time. Build fails if a faculty profile is missing required fields.

devops.deployment_topology: Netlify CDN global edge. GitHub repository as source of truth. Netlify auto-deploys on push to main. Branch deploy previews for content PRs.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: TypeScript type check, Astro build validation, broken link check via lychee. Netlify auto-deploy on successful CI. Netlify deploy previews linked in PR comments.
devops.observability_stack: Google Analytics 4 for page views, faculty profile engagement, research area clicks. Netlify build notifications to department Slack channel on build failure.

backend.service_design: No backend service. BibTeX parsing via bibtex-parser npm package at build time. No server-side runtime.
backend.api_patterns: No API. All data baked into static HTML at build time.

target_output.thinking_summary: Static university department site with no server-side runtime. Key decisions: Astro content collections with Zod schema validation for type-safe markdown content management, BibTeX parsing at build time for publications to avoid manual entry, Netlify CDN for zero-ops hosting. The only interactive element is a cookie consent banner for GDPR compliance implemented as a minimal Astro island.
target_output.title: University Chemistry Department Static Website Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A fully static Astro 4 site with TypeScript content collections for the university chemistry department. Faculty profiles, research projects, publications (parsed from BibTeX), and lab facilities rendered as static HTML at build time and served from Netlify CDN globally. Non-technical administrator manages all content through git-committed markdown files with build-time schema validation catching errors before deployment.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure static architecture with Astro 4 content collections as the data layer. Each content type (faculty_profiles, research_projects, publications, lab_facilities, graduate_programs) is a typed Astro collection with Zod schema validation enforced at build time. BibTeX publication files are parsed by bibtex-parser during the build to generate the publications list without manual entry. Netlify CI/CD triggers a full static rebuild on every push to main. No server-side runtime, no database, no API layer. Only client-side JavaScript is a minimal cookie consent island and the Google Analytics 4 snippet.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, astro:assets for image optimization, astro-sitemap for sitemap generation. Content: Markdown with YAML frontmatter, BibTeX for publications. Build tooling: bibtex-parser npm package, lychee for broken link checking. Hosting: Netlify CDN with edge caching, Netlify Deploy Previews. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with anonymized IP. CI: GitHub Actions for type check and build validation.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Faculty Directory: searchable grid of faculty cards with research area tags linking to individual profile pages with bio, publications count, and lab link. Research Areas: taxonomy-organized project listings with PI attribution and funding agency tags. Publications List: chronologically sorted list parsed from BibTeX with DOI links and optional abstract toggle. Lab Facilities: image gallery of departmental instruments and shared equipment. Graduate Programs: admissions information with application deadlines and contact links. Department News: markdown-driven news feed with dated entries.
target_output.system_components: GitHub repository (content and code source of truth), Netlify Build (Astro SSG with content collection validation), Netlify CDN (global static asset delivery), Google Analytics 4 (anonymized traffic tracking), astro-sitemap (automatic sitemap.xml generation), lychee link checker (CI step validating no broken DOI or internal links).
target_output.workflows: Content Update by Administrator: Admin edits a faculty profile markdown file in /content/faculty/, commits to a feature branch, opens a GitHub pull request. GitHub Actions runs TypeScript type check and Astro build validation. Netlify generates a deploy preview URL linked in the PR for visual review. Maintainer merges PR; Netlify deploys to production within 90 seconds. BibTeX Publication Update: Researcher emails admin a BibTeX entry; admin appends to /content/publications.bib, commits to main. Build parses BibTeX, regenerates publications page with new entry sorted by year. Prospective Student Browsing: Visitor lands on /research, filters by research area keyword, navigates to a specific project page, finds PI contact email, clicks mailto: link to reach out directly.
target_output.data_model: faculty_profiles collection: slug (string), name (string), title (string), research_areas (string array), bio (string), email (string), photo_path (image), lab_url (string optional), joined_year (number). research_projects collection: slug (string), title (string), pi_slug (reference to faculty), grant_number (string optional), funding_agency (string), start_year (number), summary (string). publications: parsed from BibTeX fields author, title, year, journal, volume, pages, doi into typed objects at build time. lab_facilities collection: slug, name, description, equipment_list (string array), photos (image array). graduate_programs collection: slug, name, degree (BS/MS/PhD), application_deadline (string), contact_email (string).
target_output.api_design: No API endpoints. All data is statically generated. Sitemap at /sitemap.xml. RSS feed at /news/rss.xml for department news feed. Contact links use mailto: anchors pointing to faculty email addresses. No form submission endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced automatically by Netlify with auto-renewing Let's Encrypt certificate. No user accounts, no form data collection, no cookies beyond analytics consent. Cookie consent banner implemented as Astro island injecting GA4 script only after user accepts. Google Analytics configured with anonymize_ip: true for GDPR compliance. Repository .gitignore and pre-commit hook block accidental commit of .env, .pdf, .docx, and credential files. Netlify bot protection enabled.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: astro check (TypeScript validation), astro build (full static build), lychee --offline (broken internal link check). On main branch merge: Netlify auto-triggers production deploy from build output. Branch pushes generate Netlify Deploy Preview URLs. Build time approximately 45 seconds including BibTeX parsing and image optimization. Rollback: Netlify one-click rollback to any previous deploy from dashboard. No secrets required; no environment variables in production.
target_output.observability: Google Analytics 4 dashboard for page views, top faculty profile pages, research area section engagement, geographic visitor distribution. Netlify build logs for build failures with email notification to department admin. GitHub Actions build status badges in repository README. No infrastructure metrics needed for static hosting.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $0/month on Netlify free tier (100GB bandwidth/month, sufficient for department traffic). If bandwidth exceeded: Netlify Pro at $19/month. No compute costs, no database costs. Scales automatically with CDN edge nodes globally. No scaling decisions required.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (4 weeks): Astro project setup with Tailwind, content collection schemas for all five types, faculty profiles and research areas pages, publications BibTeX parser integration, responsive mobile layout, Google Analytics integration, cookie consent island, GitHub Actions CI, Netlify deploy pipeline. Phase 2 Enhancements (3 weeks): Lab facilities gallery with image optimization, graduate programs pages, department news feed with RSS, sitemap generation, broken link CI check. Phase 3 Optional (future): Decap CMS integration for GUI-based content editing by admin without git knowledge.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode enforced; all content collection schemas define required and optional fields with Zod. Astro content collection query functions typed with CollectionEntry generics. Images committed to /public/images/faculty/ in WebP format at 800x800 max resolution. BibTeX file validated by bibtex-parser during CI build; malformed entries fail the build. Broken link check runs in CI to catch dead DOI links before deployment.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Non-technical admin introduces markdown syntax errors breaking the build. Mitigation: Clear README with content editing guide, build error notifications with line number context. Risk: Faculty publication lists become stale if BibTeX updates require developer involvement. Mitigation: Provide admin with simple BibTeX entry template and document the process. Tradeoff: Markdown-based CMS over Decap CMS reduces setup complexity but requires admin git familiarity. Tradeoff: No search functionality in MVP; prospective students rely on browser Ctrl+F or research area taxonomy navigation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Astro over Next.js because no server-side features are needed and Astro produces smaller HTML output for content sites. Chose Netlify over GitHub Pages for deploy preview functionality used in content review workflow. BibTeX parsing at build time selected over manual markdown publications entries to reduce admin burden on publication-heavy department.

metadata.notes: Intentionally thin plan reflecting weak quality tier; static site with minimal operational complexity but missing deeper content architecture details.

project.goal: Multi-tenant LIMS for CRO with sample management, results, and billing
project.target_users: CRO analysts, supervisors, client managers
project.timeline: MVP in 12 weeks; full platform 6 months

frontend.state: Zustand for UI state, TanStack Query for server state

security.auth: Keycloak with MFA and tenant claim
security.transport: mTLS for partner API, TLS 1.3 everywhere

data.cache: Redis 7 for sessions and job queue

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

reasoner_reviews.product: Prior round addressed basic sample intake but lacked multi-tenant billing and QC automation. Chain-of-custody audit log is the critical differentiator for ISO 17025 accreditation. Client portal for report access is a key retention driver for repeat business.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: PostgreSQL RLS with tenant_id is the right isolation pattern. Go 1.22 with Gin handles the 5000 samples/day throughput comfortably with connection pooling via pgxpool. Barcode scanning integration via REST API from mobile scanners is the simplest integration pattern without requiring a dedicated mobile app in phase 1.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS for the partner-facing API is non-negotiable given confidential client data. Keycloak client scopes map to tenant_id claims injected into Go context and then into PostgreSQL SET LOCAL for RLS. Need to ensure chain_of_custody_events table is append-only (INSERT only, no UPDATE/DELETE) for immutable audit trail required by ISO 17025.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 5000 samples/day per tenant at 10 tenants is 50,000 samples/day total. PostgreSQL with pgxpool (pool size 20) and Redis for sample status caching handles this comfortably. GKE Autopilot auto-scales on CPU; batch billing jobs run off-peak to avoid interference with interactive workloads.

backend.service_design: Go monolith with domain packages: sample_intake, chain_of_custody, test_management, result_processing, report_generation, billing, client_portal_api. Domain packages communicate via internal interfaces, not HTTP, for MVP; split into services if scale requires.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful at /api/v1 and /partner/v1. /partner/v1 requires mTLS client certificate. Versioned with Gin route groups. OpenAPI 3.1 spec generated from Go struct annotations.
backend.background_jobs: Redis-backed job queue (asynq library) for: report PDF generation, billing batch runs, QC check evaluation, email notifications. Separate worker pool for each job type.
backend.failure_handling: Idempotency keys on sample registration to prevent duplicate records from scanner retries. Dead letter queue for failed report generation jobs with Datadog alert.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router with tenant-scoped layouts. Route groups: (analyst) for lab workflows, (client) for partner portal, (admin) for CRO administration.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Sample Intake: barcode scan → sample registration form → test panel selector → chain-of-custody receipt. Analyst Worklist: pending samples sorted by priority and SLA deadline. Result Entry: instrument result upload form with QC threshold validation. Client Portal: read-only report browser and PDF download. Admin: tenant management, pricing config, user administration.
frontend.state_management: TanStack Query for server state with 30s polling on analyst worklist. Zustand for in-session barcode scan state on intake workflow.

security.auth_design: Keycloak realm per environment. Tenants are Keycloak groups. tenant_id injected as JWT claim. mTLS termination at GKE Ingress for /partner/v1 routes.
security.authorization_model: CRO roles: analyst (sample intake, result entry), supervisor (QC override, report approval), admin (full CRO access). Client roles: viewer (read-only reports), manager (report download, user management for their org).
security.audit_and_logging_controls: chain_of_custody_events INSERT-only table (ROW SECURITY prevents UPDATE/DELETE). Datadog log forwarding for all API requests with tenant_id in structured log context.

data.entities: samples (id, tenant_id, barcode, client_id, received_at, status ENUM received/assigned/testing/complete/rejected), test_panels (id, tenant_id, name, included_tests[], turnaround_hours), chain_of_custody_events (id, sample_id, event_type ENUM received/transferred/assigned/resulted/approved/rejected, actor_id, timestamp, location, notes), analyst_assignments (id, sample_id, analyst_id, assigned_at, completed_at), result_reports (id, sample_id, report_pdf_gcs_key, generated_at, approved_by), invoices (id, client_id, billing_period, line_items_json, total_amount, status ENUM draft/sent/paid), instruments (id, tenant_id, name, model, calibration_due_date)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with RLS; Redis 7 for sample status cache (5-min TTL); GCS for PDF reports with signed URLs; BigQuery for analytics exports

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster with Namespaces: lims-api, lims-workers, lims-monitoring. Ingress-NGINX with mTLS for /partner routes. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with high availability. Memorystore Redis 7.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: golint, go test (90% coverage gate), docker build, push to Artifact Registry, helm upgrade --install to GKE. Schema migrations via golang-migrate in CI migration job before app deploy. Feature branch → staging; tag → production.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM tracing on all Go handlers. Custom metrics: samples_ingested_per_hour per tenant, qc_failure_rate, report_generation_latency_p99, invoice_generation_duration. PagerDuty: sample intake API p99 >1s, qc_failure_rate >10%, billing job failure.

ISS-001.title: Chain-of-custody table allows UPDATE operations breaking immutability requirement
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan did not specify INSERT-only constraint on chain_of_custody_events. ISO 17025 requires tamper-evident custody records. Current schema has no protection against UPDATE or DELETE.

ISS-002.title: Multi-tenant billing batch job lacks idempotency controls
ISS-002.detail: Monthly billing aggregation job could double-charge clients if re-run after partial failure. No idempotency key or processed_billing_period tracking in invoice table.

focus_issues.title: Chain-of-custody table allows UPDATE operations breaking immutability requirement
focus_issues.detail: ISO 17025 requires tamper-evident custody records. Round 1 plan lacks INSERT-only enforcement.

focus_issues.title: Multi-tenant billing batch job lacks idempotency controls
focus_issues.detail: Billing job can double-invoice clients on re-run after partial failure.

latest_recommendations: Add INSERT-only PostgreSQL trigger and ROW SECURITY policy on chain_of_custody_events to prevent UPDATE and DELETE
Add billing_period_locks table with unique constraint to prevent duplicate billing run for same tenant/period pair

convergence_state.reason: Two unresolved issues preventing accreditation compliance and billing integrity

previous_audits.summary: Solid architecture for LIMS scale requirements but two critical gaps in immutability enforcement and billing idempotency block accreditation and financial integrity.
strengths: PostgreSQL RLS for multi-tenant isolation is well-specified
Go backend with pgxpool appropriate for 5000 samples/day throughput
mTLS for partner-facing API correctly specified
concerns: chain_of_custody_events has no immutability protection
billing batch job has no idempotency mechanism
blocking_issues: chain_of_custody_events must be INSERT-only for ISO 17025 compliance
billing job must prevent double-invoicing on re-run
recommendations: Add PostgreSQL trigger BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE ON chain_of_custody_events to raise exception
Add billing_period_locks table with (tenant_id, billing_period) unique constraint; billing job acquires advisory lock before running

issue_updates.title: Chain-of-custody immutability not enforced
issue_updates.detail: No trigger or RLS policy preventing UPDATE/DELETE on chain_of_custody_events

issue_updates.title: Billing batch idempotency missing
issue_updates.detail: Invoice generation job can re-run without detecting already-processed billing periods

previous_plan.title: CRO LIMS Platform Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Multi-tenant LIMS built on Go/Gin backend with Next.js 14 frontend deployed on GKE Autopilot. PostgreSQL 16 with RLS for tenant isolation. Supports sample intake, chain-of-custody tracking, test assignment, and client reporting.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Next.js 14 frontend communicating with Go/Gin REST API on GKE. PostgreSQL 16 with RLS enforcing tenant isolation. Redis for caching and job queue. GCS for report PDFs.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Keycloak MFA, mTLS on partner API, PostgreSQL RLS with tenant_id. ISO 17025 compliance mentioned but chain-of-custody immutability not enforced at database level.
previous_plan.data_model: samples, chain_of_custody_events, test_panels, analyst_assignments, result_reports, invoices tables present but without immutability constraints on custody events or idempotency on invoice generation.

target_output.thinking_summary: Revision round targeting two specific issues: ISS-001 (chain-of-custody immutability) resolved by adding a PostgreSQL BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger raising an exception on chain_of_custody_events, plus an INSERT-only RLS policy. ISS-002 (billing idempotency) resolved by introducing a billing_period_locks table with unique (tenant_id, billing_period) constraint and advisory lock acquisition before billing job execution, with idempotent invoice upsert logic.

fix_report.action_taken: Added BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger on chain_of_custody_events that raises exception 'custody record is immutable'. Added PostgreSQL RLS USING policy restricting all operations to INSERT. Documented this in data_model and security_and_compliance sections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISO 17025 tamper-evident chain-of-custody requirement met; no UPDATE or DELETE possible on custody events at database level

fix_report.action_taken: Added billing_period_locks table with UNIQUE(tenant_id, billing_period) and processed_at timestamp. Billing job now acquires PostgreSQL advisory lock keyed on tenant_id+billing_period before executing, checks billing_period_locks for existing record, and inserts lock record only on successful completion. Idempotent invoice upsert uses ON CONFLICT(client_id, billing_period) DO UPDATE.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Billing job re-runs after partial failure will detect existing billing_period_locks record and skip without double-charging clients

target_output.title: CRO LIMS Platform Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: Multi-tenant LIMS on GKE Autopilot with Go 1.22/Gin backend and Next.js 14 frontend. PostgreSQL 16 with multi-tenant RLS, immutable chain-of-custody event log enforced by database trigger, and idempotent monthly billing batch jobs. Supports 5,000 samples/day per tenant across 10 client organizations with ISO 17025, CAP, and SOC 2 Type II compliance controls.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier multi-tenant architecture: Next.js 14 SSR frontend served via GKE Ingress, Go/Gin API microservice behind Ingress-NGINX with mTLS termination for /partner/v1 routes, PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL with row-level security enforcing tenant_id isolation. Keycloak injects tenant_id JWT claim; Go middleware sets PostgreSQL session variable for RLS activation. Redis 7 via Memorystore for sample status cache and asynq job queue. GCS for PDF report storage with signed URLs. Chain-of-custody events are append-only at database level via trigger. Billing batch jobs use advisory locks and period-lock table for idempotency.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, TanStack Query, Zustand. Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, GORM, pgxpool, asynq (Redis-backed job queue), golang-migrate. Database: PostgreSQL 16 (Cloud SQL HA), Redis 7 (Memorystore). Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, Ingress-NGINX with mTLS, GCS, Artifact Registry, Keycloak. Observability: Datadog APM, PagerDuty. CI: GitLab CI with Helm.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Sample Intake Module: barcode scan via REST endpoint, registration form with client account selector and test panel assignment. Chain-of-Custody Tracker: immutable append-only event log for each sample transfer, assignment, and result event viewable as timeline. Analyst Worklist: prioritized queue of assigned samples with SLA countdown timers. Result Entry: manual entry or CSV upload with automated QC threshold evaluation. Report Generation: async PDF generation via asynq worker, GCS storage, client portal signed URL delivery. Billing Engine: monthly aggregation of completed test counts per client, contract pricing tier application, invoice generation with idempotency guard. Client Portal: read-only access to own reports and invoices. Admin Dashboard: tenant onboarding, pricing configuration, accreditation audit log export.
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot cluster (lims-api Deployment, lims-worker Deployment), Ingress-NGINX (TLS termination + mTLS on /partner routes), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA (primary + read replica), Memorystore Redis 7 (session cache + asynq queue), GCS bucket (PDF reports with signed URLs), Keycloak (Deployment in lims-auth namespace), Datadog agents (DaemonSet), PagerDuty integration, BigQuery dataset for analytics exports.
target_output.workflows: Sample Intake and Chain-of-Custody: Lab receives physical samples with barcodes. Analyst scans barcode via mobile browser hitting POST /api/v1/samples endpoint. Go handler validates tenant context from Keycloak JWT, inserts into samples table, and appends chain_of_custody_events record (event_type: received). Test panel auto-assigned based on client contract rules. Analyst assignment dispatched via asynq job. Each transfer or status change appends new chain_of_custody_events row; trigger prevents any modification to existing rows. Monthly Billing Run: Celery-equivalent asynq scheduled job runs 1st of each month per tenant. Acquires PostgreSQL advisory lock pg_advisory_lock(tenant_id_hash). Checks billing_period_locks for (tenant_id, billing_period); skips if record exists. Aggregates completed test counts from analyst_assignments grouped by test type and client. Applies pricing from client contracts table. Inserts invoices with ON CONFLICT(client_id, billing_period) DO UPDATE. Inserts billing_period_locks record. Webhook to accounting system triggers invoice delivery. Releases advisory lock.
target_output.data_model: samples (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID FK, barcode VARCHAR UNIQUE per tenant, client_id UUID FK, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM, priority INT). chain_of_custody_events (id UUID PK, sample_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM received/transferred/assigned/resulted/qc_checked/approved/rejected, actor_id UUID, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, location VARCHAR, notes TEXT) -- BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger raises exception; INSERT-only RLS policy. test_panels (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID FK, name, included_tests JSONB, turnaround_hours INT, price_per_test NUMERIC). analyst_assignments (id UUID PK, sample_id UUID FK, analyst_id UUID FK, assigned_at, completed_at). result_reports (id UUID PK, sample_id UUID FK, report_pdf_gcs_key TEXT, generated_at, approved_by UUID). invoices (id UUID PK, client_id UUID FK, billing_period VARCHAR, line_items JSONB, total_amount NUMERIC, status ENUM, UNIQUE(client_id, billing_period)). billing_period_locks (tenant_id UUID, billing_period VARCHAR, processed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, PRIMARY KEY(tenant_id, billing_period)). instruments (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID FK, name, model, calibration_due_date DATE).
target_output.api_design: RESTful at /api/v1 (internal CRO staff, Keycloak JWT) and /partner/v1 (client organizations, mTLS + JWT). POST /api/v1/samples (barcode intake), GET /api/v1/samples?status=&tenant= (worklist), PATCH /api/v1/samples/{id}/assign (test assignment), POST /api/v1/samples/{id}/results (result entry), GET /api/v1/samples/{id}/custody (chain-of-custody timeline), POST /api/v1/reports/{sample_id} (trigger PDF generation), GET /partner/v1/reports (client portal report listing), GET /partner/v1/reports/{id}/download (signed GCS URL), GET /partner/v1/invoices (client invoice history). Auth: Keycloak JWT Bearer for all routes; mTLS client certificate required for /partner/v1.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak with MFA enforced for all analyst and supervisor roles; client portal MFA optional but recommended. PostgreSQL RLS policies on all tables enforced via SET LOCAL app.tenant_id from JWT claim in Go middleware. chain_of_custody_events: BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger raises 'custody record is immutable'; additional INSERT-only RLS policy as defense-in-depth. mTLS termination on Ingress-NGINX for /partner/v1 using client certificate signed by CRO-controlled CA. GCS report keys prefixed with tenant_id/client_id preventing cross-tenant access. ISO 17025 audit controls: chain-of-custody event export API for accreditation auditors, instrument calibration due-date tracking, QC failure rate dashboard. SOC 2: Datadog audit log forwarding, 90-day log retention in GCS archive.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: golint, go vet, go test (90% coverage gate), docker build, push to GCP Artifact Registry, helm upgrade --install lims-api to GKE with rolling update strategy (maxSurge: 1, maxUnavailable: 0). Database migrations via golang-migrate run as Kubernetes Job before app deployment. Environments: dev (GKE Autopilot dev namespace), staging (mirrors production config), production (GKE Autopilot with workload identity, Cloud SQL HA, Memorystore HA). Secrets: GCP Secret Manager with workload identity binding. Rollback: helm rollback to previous revision. Monthly billing job: Kubernetes CronJob 01:00 UTC 1st of month with asynq dispatcher.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM distributed tracing on all Go Gin handlers with tenant_id in span tags. Custom metrics: samples_ingested_per_hour (by tenant_id), chain_of_custody_events_per_minute, qc_failure_rate (by test panel), report_generation_latency_p99, billing_job_duration, invoice_generation_count. Grafana dashboard for per-tenant throughput and SLA compliance. PagerDuty alerts: sample intake API p99 >500ms, qc_failure_rate >10% over 1h, billing job failure or non-completion by 06:00 UTC on billing day, chain_of_custody trigger exception rate >0 (indicates attempted data tampering).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $4,200/month for 10 tenants at 50,000 samples/day: Cloud SQL HA PostgreSQL ~$780, Memorystore Redis HA ~$240, GKE Autopilot compute ~$1,800, GCS ~$90, Datadog APM ~$800, Keycloak compute ~$180, misc networking ~$310. GKE Autopilot scales API pods on CPU target 60%. Read replica for report queries to avoid impacting sample intake writes. GCS Coldline lifecycle for reports >1 year old reduces storage cost ~65%.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): sample intake with barcode registration, chain-of-custody event log with INSERT-only enforcement, test panel assignment, manual result entry, basic PDF report generation via asynq worker, single-tenant billing with idempotency guard, Keycloak auth with MFA, GKE deployment, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (8 weeks): multi-tenant onboarding and tenant admin panel, client portal with mTLS partner API, automated QC threshold checks, contract pricing tiers, billing_period_locks idempotency, Datadog APM integration. Phase 3 (6 weeks): analytics dashboard (BigQuery export, Looker Studio), instrument calibration tracking, email report delivery, SOC 2 audit log export, accreditation audit report generation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with explicit dependency versions pinned in go.sum. All database interactions via GORM with explicit tenant_id where-clause in every query (linted by custom go vet check). chain_of_custody_events may only be written via CustodyService.AppendEvent() function; direct SQL writes to this table are a CI linting violation. PostgreSQL migrations reviewed by lead engineer before merge. TanStack Query cache invalidation tested in Cypress E2E for analyst worklist real-time updates.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PostgreSQL advisory lock for billing deadlocks if job process crashes mid-run. Mitigation: advisory lock timeout set to 5 minutes; asynq dead-letter queue triggers PagerDuty alert for manual re-run. Risk: GKE Autopilot cold-start latency for infrequently-used worker pods during peak sample intake. Mitigation: minimum replica count of 2 for lims-api deployment. Tradeoff: Go monolith over microservices reduces operational complexity for MVP while module boundaries enable future extraction. Tradeoff: asynq (Redis-backed) over Kafka for job queue reduces infrastructure cost and operational complexity at current 50K samples/day scale; revisit at 500K/day.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed PostgreSQL trigger over application-layer immutability enforcement for chain_of_custody_events -- database-level is stronger. Advisory lock + billing_period_locks table selected over distributed lock (Redlock) for simplicity and reliability. GCS signed URLs over proxy endpoint for PDF delivery to reduce API bandwidth cost. mTLS via Ingress-NGINX over service mesh (Istio) to avoid sidecar overhead on GKE Autopilot.

metadata.notes: Strong revision plan with specific fixes to immutability trigger and billing idempotency; all LIMS domain entities and workflows are fully specified.

project_goal.value: Web platform for materials scientists to manage ML experiments on crystal structure datasets, including feature engineering, model training, hyperparameter tracking, and prediction visualization for novel compound discovery
project_goal.rationale: Central platform replacing fragmented local MLflow instances and shared spreadsheet experiment logs

target_users.value: Materials scientists, computational chemists, and ML engineers in academic and industrial research groups
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery sessions

project_class.rationale: Web UI with backend API, GPU training workers, and database

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, ai_llm, analytics, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Full experiment management platform with GPU training and LLM-assisted property prediction

complexity_level.rationale: GPU training job dispatch, hyperparameter search, crystal structure data handling

risk_level.rationale: Confidential pre-publication research data; export control considerations for dual-use materials

data_sensitivity.rationale: Crystal structure datasets and model checkpoints represent pre-publication IP

external_exposure.rationale: Restricted to research group members with Azure AD authentication

access_model.value: RBAC with researcher, PI, and admin roles; project-level access control on datasets and experiments
access_model.rationale: Research groups must isolate pre-publication data from other groups

feature_scope.value: Crystal structure dataset upload, feature engineering pipeline configuration, GPU training job dispatch with hyperparameter tracking, model checkpoint versioning, prediction visualization on virtual compound libraries, candidate annotation and wet-lab export
feature_scope.rationale: Full experiment lifecycle from data to candidate shortlisting

mvp_scope.value: Dataset upload, manual feature set configuration, training job submission with loss curve tracking, model checkpoint storage, basic prediction result table
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for pilot with 2 research groups

security_baseline.value: Azure AD SSO, TLS 1.3, AES-256 encryption at rest, project-level access control, export control screening for dual-use compound data

reasoner_reviews.product: Round 1 plan lacked detail on hyperparameter tracking schema and training job status streaming. The prediction visualization workflow for virtual compound libraries is the core scientific value delivery. Wet-lab export queue for candidate compounds is a key downstream integration point for research utility.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Celery with Redis backend for training job dispatch is the right pattern for GPU task queuing on AKS. However the previous plan used a generic MongoDB schema that does not capture the nested hyperparameter search space structure needed for systematic hyperparameter tracking. training_runs collection needs a search_space sub-document with typed parameter bounds.
reasoner_reviews.security: Export control screening for dual-use materials is a compliance risk that was not addressed in round 1. Need to specify a pre-upload screening step for crystal structure datasets that flags compounds matching EAR-controlled material classifications before storage. Also project-level access control was mentioned but not specified in the authorization model.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: $3000/month on Azure: AKS GPU node pool (NC6 Spot) at ~$0.9/hr is the dominant cost. Training jobs at 12h max on NC6 = $10.80/job. 200 training jobs/month = $2,160 GPU cost alone. Need GPU spot preemption handling.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with routers for datasets, feature_sets, training_runs, model_checkpoints, predictions, candidates. Celery workers: gpu_training_worker (AKS GPU node pool), cpu_preprocessing_worker (AKS CPU pool).
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1. Async training job pattern: POST /training-runs returns run_id with status queued; GET /training-runs/{id}/stream (SSE for live loss curve); GET /training-runs/{id} for final status.
backend.llm_integration_design: Azure OpenAI GPT-4o via azure-openai SDK for: (1) NL property query parsing on candidate search -- converts 'high conductivity low bandgap' to structured filter JSON; (2) candidate annotation summarization -- generates 1-paragraph synthesis rationale from prediction results. Prompts stored in prompt_templates collection.
backend.background_jobs: Celery beat: nightly inactive dataset cleanup, weekly model checkpoint deduplication. Celery task: train_model dispatched to GPU pool with crystal_structure_dataset_id, feature_set_id, hyperparameter_config.

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA with Pinia for state. Route groups: /datasets, /experiments, /experiments/:id (live training view), /predictions/:model_id, /candidates.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Dataset Upload: drag-drop CIF file upload with export control pre-check result display. Experiment Builder: feature set selector, hyperparameter form with range inputs, launch button. Live Training View: Plotly line chart updating via SSE loss curve stream. Prediction Dashboard: scatter plot of predicted vs experimental properties for candidate ranking.
frontend.performance_notes: SSE for loss curve streaming avoids polling overhead during training. Plotly renders up to 50K compound scatter points with WebGL renderer. Virtual compound library search results paginated at 500 rows.

security.auth_design: Azure AD via MSAL with PKCE flow. AKS workload identity for Blob Storage and Azure OpenAI access (no credential storage in app).
security.authorization_model: Project-level RBAC enforced in FastAPI middleware: researcher can read/write own project; PI can manage all group projects; admin has full access. MongoDB queries always include project_id filter.
security.privacy_controls: EAR pre-upload screening: CIF file parsed to extract chemical formula, checked against CCL (Commerce Control List) compound classifier before storage. Flagged uploads quarantined pending compliance review.

data.entities: crystal_structures (collection: _id, project_id, formula, cif_blob_path, lattice_params_json, uploaded_by, uploaded_at, export_control_status ENUM cleared/flagged/quarantined). feature_sets (collection: _id, project_id, name, descriptor_types[], normalization_method, created_at). training_runs (collection: _id, project_id, crystal_structure_dataset_id, feature_set_id, model_type, hyperparameters {search_space: {lr: {min, max, type}, layers: {values}}, selected: {}}, status ENUM queued/running/completed/failed, loss_curve_s3_key, checkpoint_blob_path, mae REAL, r2 REAL, submitted_at, completed_at). model_checkpoints (collection: _id, training_run_id, blob_path, epoch INT, val_mae REAL). prediction_results (collection: _id, model_id, compound_library_id, compound_id, predicted_properties {}, confidence REAL, created_at). compound_candidates (collection: _id, project_id, prediction_result_id, annotation_text, wet_lab_status ENUM shortlisted/in_queue/tested, annotated_by, annotated_at)
data.storage_design: MongoDB 7 via Cosmos DB MongoDB API for metadata; Azure Blob Storage for CIF files, model checkpoints, and loss curve JSON; Redis 7 for Celery broker and result backend

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster: api Deployment (CPU, 3 replicas), gpu_worker Deployment (GPU node pool, NC6 Spot, auto-scale 0-10), cpu_worker Deployment (CPU pool, 2 replicas), Redis StatefulSet. Azure Container Registry, Azure Key Vault for secrets, Azure Monitor for metrics.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff, mypy, pytest (85%), docker build, push to ACR, helm upgrade to AKS. GPU worker image built separately with CUDA base layer, cached in ACR.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor custom metrics: training_jobs_submitted, training_job_duration_hours, gpu_utilization (from DCGM exporter), prediction_throughput. Grafana on AKS for dashboards. Alerts: GPU utilization <10% on running job (idle node cost), job failure rate >15%.

ISS-001.title: MongoDB training_runs schema missing typed hyperparameter search_space sub-document
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 data model used flat hyperparameters field without search_space structure. Hyperparameter optimization workflows require bounded search_space definition separate from the selected values for a specific run.

ISS-002.title: Export control pre-upload screening not specified for dual-use crystal structures
ISS-002.detail: Compliance context requires EAR screening but round 1 plan has no pre-upload screening step, quarantine workflow, or export_control_status field on crystal_structures collection.

ISS-003.title: GPU Spot preemption on AKS not handled in training job design
ISS-003.detail: NC6 Spot nodes can be preempted mid-training. Round 1 plan has no checkpoint save on preemption or job re-queue mechanism.

focus_issues.title: MongoDB training_runs schema missing typed hyperparameter search_space sub-document
focus_issues.detail: Hyperparameter optimization requires search_space structure separate from selected values.

focus_issues.title: Export control pre-upload screening not specified
focus_issues.detail: No EAR screening, quarantine workflow, or export_control_status field specified.

Add export_control_status field to crystal_structures with pre-upload CCL screening step before Blob Storage write
Add AKS preemption handler saving model checkpoint to Blob on SIGTERM before Spot node reclaim

target_output.title: Crystal Structure ML Experiment Platform v2
target_output.executive_summary: Vue 3 and FastAPI platform on AKS for materials science ML experiments. Crystal structure CIF files uploaded and managed per project, feature engineering pipelines configured, GPU training jobs dispatched via Celery to AKS NC6 Spot workers. Hyperparameter tracking with typed search_space schema. Model predictions visualized on virtual compound libraries for candidate shortlisting.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: Vue 3 SPA communicating to FastAPI on AKS behind Azure Application Gateway. Celery with Redis broker dispatches training tasks to GPU node pool (NC6 Spot) and CPU workers. MongoDB via Cosmos DB API stores experiment metadata. Azure Blob Storage holds CIF files and model checkpoints. Azure AD workload identity for credential-free Blob and Azure OpenAI access. The platform manages the full experiment lifecycle from crystal structure upload through model training to compound candidate export.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Vite, Pinia, Plotly.js (WebGL mode for large scatter plots), Vue Router. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Celery 5, Motor (async MongoDB), azure-storage-blob, azure-openai. Database: MongoDB 7 via Azure Cosmos DB MongoDB API. Infrastructure: AKS with CPU and GPU (NC6 Spot) node pools, Azure Blob Storage, Azure Container Registry, Azure Key Vault, Azure AD. Observability: Azure Monitor, DCGM GPU exporter, Grafana.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Dataset Management: CIF crystal structure file upload, export_control_status field present in schema, dataset versioning, project-level access control. Feature Engineering: configurable descriptor type selection (SOAP, Coulomb matrix, graph neural network fingerprints), normalization options. Experiment Builder: training run configuration with hyperparameters.search_space bounds for Optuna HPO. Live Training Dashboard: loss curve streaming via SSE from Celery task progress. Prediction Browser: Plotly scatter of predicted vs experimental formation energy, bandgap, conductivity for virtual libraries. Candidate Queue: shortlisted compounds with annotation text and wet-lab export.
target_output.system_components: Azure Application Gateway (ingress, TLS termination), AKS api Deployment (FastAPI, 3 CPU replicas), AKS gpu_worker Deployment (Celery GPU tasks, NC6 Spot, 0-10 replicas), AKS cpu_worker Deployment (preprocessing tasks), Redis StatefulSet (Celery broker), MongoDB Cosmos DB, Azure Blob Storage (CIF files, checkpoints), Azure Key Vault, Azure AD workload identity, DCGM GPU exporter, Grafana.
target_output.workflows: Crystal Structure Upload: Scientist uploads CIF file via Vue drag-drop component. FastAPI receives file, extracts chemical formula via pymatgen. Checks formula against CCL compound classifier -- note: this check is described as present but the CCL lookup integration is not specified in security controls, leaving a compliance gap. File stored in Azure Blob at project_id/datasets/. MongoDB crystal_structures document inserted with export_control_status field (default value 'pending' but no actual screening logic defined). Training Job Submission: Scientist configures training run with feature_set_id and hyperparameter search_space bounds. POST /training-runs creates MongoDB document with search_space sub-document. Celery dispatches train_model task to GPU worker pool. Worker loads crystal structure dataset from Blob, computes feature matrix, runs Optuna trial using search_space bounds, logs loss to Redis pub/sub for SSE streaming. On completion writes checkpoint to Blob, updates MongoDB status to completed with mae and r2 scores.
target_output.data_model: crystal_structures (MongoDB collection: _id, project_id, formula VARCHAR, cif_blob_path TEXT, lattice_params_json OBJECT, uploaded_by, uploaded_at, export_control_status STRING -- field present in schema but populated with 'pending' by default; no CCL screening populates this field in the described implementation). feature_sets (_id, project_id, name, descriptor_types ARRAY, normalization_method, created_at). training_runs (_id, project_id, dataset_id, feature_set_id, model_type, hyperparameters: {search_space: {lr: {type: 'log_uniform', min: 0.0001, max: 0.1}, hidden_layers: {type: 'int_choice', values: [2,3,4]}}, selected: {lr: 0.001, hidden_layers: 3}}, status, loss_curve_blob_path, checkpoint_blob_path, mae, r2, submitted_at, completed_at). model_checkpoints (_id, training_run_id, blob_path, epoch, val_mae). prediction_results (_id, model_id, compound_id, predicted_properties OBJECT, confidence REAL). compound_candidates (_id, project_id, prediction_result_id, annotation_text, wet_lab_status ENUM, annotated_by).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1. POST /datasets/upload (multipart CIF upload, returns dataset_id). GET /datasets?project_id= (list datasets). POST /feature-sets (create feature set config). POST /training-runs (submit training job with search_space, returns run_id). GET /training-runs/{id}/stream (SSE loss curve). GET /training-runs/{id} (status and metrics). POST /predictions (run model on compound library). GET /candidates?project_id= (shortlist). PUT /candidates/{id}/annotate. Auth: Azure AD JWT Bearer on all endpoints.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SSO via MSAL with PKCE. AKS workload identity for Blob Storage and Azure OpenAI access. Project-level RBAC enforced in FastAPI middleware with project_id filter on all MongoDB queries. TLS 1.3 on Application Gateway. EAR compliance: export_control_status field added to crystal_structures collection. CCL screening implementation is noted as required but specifics of the CCL lookup service integration are not detailed -- this gap means ISS-002 is not fully resolved despite the fix_report claiming it was addressed.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff, mypy, pytest 85% coverage gate, docker build, push to ACR, helm upgrade to AKS. GPU worker image uses CUDA 12 base with PyTorch 2.2. Celery GPU tasks dispatched to GPU node pool via Kubernetes node selector gpu=true. AKS Cluster Autoscaler scales GPU node pool 0-10 based on Celery queue depth (KEDA). Spot preemption: SIGTERM handler saves current checkpoint epoch to Blob and re-queues task -- this handler is mentioned but not implemented in the described worker code.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor custom metrics: training_jobs_submitted, training_job_duration_hours, prediction_throughput_per_hour. DCGM GPU exporter for GPU utilization metrics. Grafana dashboard: per-project experiment count, mean training job duration, GPU utilization heatmap. Alerts: job failure rate >15%, GPU utilization <10% on active job for >30 min.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2,800/month: AKS GPU NC6 Spot ~$1,400 (200 job-hours/month), AKS CPU compute ~$480, Cosmos DB MongoDB ~$350, Azure Blob ~$120, Azure Monitor ~$200, ACR ~$50, misc ~$200. Stays within $3,000 budget. GPU Spot savings ~70% vs on-demand. KEDA scales GPU pool to zero when queue empty.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Azure AD auth, dataset upload with export_control_status field (no CCL screening), feature set configuration, training job submission with search_space schema, SSE loss curve streaming, model checkpoint storage, basic prediction result table, GitHub Actions CI, AKS deployment. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Optuna HPO integration using search_space bounds, prediction scatter plots with Plotly WebGL, candidate annotation with Azure OpenAI summarization, wet-lab export queue. Phase 3 (4 weeks): CCL screening integration (deferred from phase 1), Spot preemption checkpoint handler, analytics dashboard, Materials Project API integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. All MongoDB queries must include project_id filter (enforced by linting rule). hyperparameters.search_space validated by Pydantic SearchSpaceModel before task dispatch. Vue components typed with TypeScript; Plotly integration wrapped in typed composable. CUDA worker images pinned to specific PyTorch version hash.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Azure Cosmos DB MongoDB API has query compatibility gaps with native MongoDB 7. Mitigation: integration test suite validates all Motor queries against Cosmos DB emulator in CI. Risk: NC6 Spot preemption loses training progress. Mitigation: checkpoint save on SIGTERM (noted as unimplemented in phase 1). Risk: EAR CCL screening deferred to phase 3 means unscreened uploads possible in MVP. This is an accepted risk for the pilot but must be resolved before any external user access. Tradeoff: Cosmos DB MongoDB API over native AKS MongoDB reduces ops burden but limits some aggregation pipeline features.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Cosmos DB MongoDB API over AKS-hosted MongoDB for managed HA and backup. Azure OpenAI chosen over OpenAI API to keep data within Azure boundary per data sovereignty constraint. Plotly WebGL renderer handles 50K compound scatter points within browser memory limits. Celery over Kubeflow Pipelines reduces complexity for phase 1 GPU job dispatch.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: fix_report claims ISS-002 resolved but security_and_compliance and data_model sections do not implement CCL screening; export_control_status field is present but never populated by actual screening logic.

project_goal.rationale: Core need for reproducible bioinformatics preprocessing pipelines

target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in discovery sessions

mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum usable unit for bioinformaticians

project.goal: Preprocess RNA-seq FASTQ files into normalized count matrices with full provenance
project.target_users: Bioinformaticians on HPC clusters

backend.framework: Click for subcommand CLI; multiprocessing.Pool for parallelism
backend.parsing: Biopython SeqIO for FASTQ streaming; configurable adapter trimming

data.output: TSV count matrices, JSON provenance files

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy, pytest, pip-audit, wheel publish to PyPI
devops.packaging: pyproject.toml with setuptools; conda recipe for conda-forge

confirmed_decisions.parallelism: multiprocessing.Pool over threading for CPU-bound FASTQ work

reasoner_reviews.product: The scope is well-bounded: FASTQ input to count matrix output with configurable QC thresholds is the critical path bioinformaticians need. Provenance JSON is a smart inclusion given reproducibility requirements in scientific publishing.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Click subcommand architecture cleanly separates run, validate-config, and summarize concerns. multiprocessing.Pool is the right parallel primitive for CPU-bound FASTQ parsing on HPC nodes with many cores. SQLite for run history is appropriate given no network database is available.
reasoner_reviews.security: No network surface means the risk profile is low. The main concern is malformed FASTQ input; robust path validation and jsonschema config validation are the key controls. Dependency pinning prevents supply chain drift.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Conda-installable constraint rules out dependencies with complex native builds. PyYAML, Biopython, and Click are all conda-forge available. The no-internet-at-runtime constraint means adapter reference databases must be bundled or user-provided.

backend.service_design: Three Click subcommands: 'rnapreproc run' (main pipeline), 'rnapreproc validate-config' (YAML dry-run), 'rnapreproc summarize' (SQLite run history). Plugin entry points allow custom filter steps.
backend.api_patterns: No HTTP API. CLI flags: --input-dir PATH, --config YAML, --output-dir PATH, --threads INT, --dry-run FLAG. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 partial failure, 2 fatal error.
backend.business_modules: FASTQParser (Biopython SeqIO streaming in 10K-read chunks), AdapterTrimmer (3-prime adapter removal with configurable mismatch tolerance), QualityFilter (Phred score threshold and min read length cutoffs), CountMatrixBuilder (HTSeq-count-compatible gene-level aggregation), ProvenanceWriter (JSON manifest with tool version, params hash, per-sample checksums).
backend.background_jobs: multiprocessing.Pool with configurable worker count defaulting to cpu_count()-1. Each worker processes one sample end-to-end. Progress reported via tqdm to stderr.
backend.failure_handling: Per-sample failures logged and run continues; failed samples listed in summary TSV. Fatal config errors abort immediately with human-readable messages citing the failing YAML key.
backend.backend_risks: Memory spikes for large FASTQ files mitigated by streaming parser. Worker count misconfiguration on shared HPC nodes can starve other jobs; document recommended --threads guidance in README.

security.auth_design: No authentication layer. File access controlled by OS permissions on HPC cluster.
security.abuse_prevention: Input path validation rejects paths outside specified working directory. YAML config validated against jsonschema before execution begins.
security.secrets_management: No secrets required. Tool reads only files specified by the user. No API keys or credentials stored.
security.security_risks: Malformed FASTQ files could trigger parser edge cases; Biopython error-tolerant parsing mode used with explicit exception handling per record.

data.entities: fastq_files (sample_id, path, read_count_raw, md5_checksum, run_id), quality_reports (sample_id, run_id, mean_phred, pct_reads_passing, pct_adapter_trimmed), filtering_configs (run_id PK, min_phred INT, min_length INT, adapter_seq, gtf_path, created_at), count_matrices (run_id, sample_id, output_tsv_path, gene_count, created_at), run_metadata (run_id PK, tool_version, git_sha, params_json, started_at, finished_at, status ENUM started/completed/partial_failure/failed).
data.storage_design: SQLite database at ~/.rnapreproc/runs.db. Output TSV count matrices and JSON provenance written to user-specified --output-dir.
data.retention_and_deletion: No automated deletion. Researchers manage output files. SQLite run history prunable via 'rnapreproc summarize --prune-before DATE'.
data.data_risks: Large FASTQ files over 100GB require streaming access; any buffering design change could cause OOM on nodes with limited RAM allocation per SLURM job.

devops.deployment_topology: PyPI wheel and conda-forge recipe. No server deployment. Install via 'pip install rnapreproc' or 'conda install -c conda-forge rnapreproc'.
