devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: on PR - ruff lint, pytest with 85% coverage gate, mypy strict, pip-audit CVE scan. On tag push - build wheel, publish to PyPI via OIDC trusted publishing, open conda-forge feedstock PR.
devops.observability_stack: Structured JSON logs per run at ~/.rnapreproc/logs/RUNID.log. Per-run QC TSV summarizes sample pass/fail rates. No external monitoring infrastructure.
devops.devops_risks: Conda-forge feedstock lag can delay release availability by 1 to 3 days after PyPI publish.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a bioinformatics CLI using Click subcommands and multiprocessing for CPU-bound FASTQ processing on HPC clusters. Key decisions: streaming FASTQ parsing prevents OOM on large files, SQLite for local run history requires no network dependency, YAML config with jsonschema validation catches user errors before expensive compute starts, and per-sample failure isolation keeps partial runs recoverable.
target_output.title: RNAPreproc CLI Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 command-line tool for bioinformaticians to preprocess RNA sequencing FASTQ datasets on HPC clusters. Provides adapter trimming, Phred-score quality filtering, and per-sample count matrix generation with configurable thresholds via YAML. Parallelizes across multiple cores using multiprocessing.Pool, aggregates outputs into a merged count matrix, and writes full provenance JSON alongside each run for publication reproducibility.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process entrypoint via Click dispatching to three subcommands: 'run' (full pipeline), 'validate-config' (YAML dry-run), and 'summarize' (SQLite run history). The 'run' subcommand spawns a multiprocessing.Pool where each worker handles one sample: streaming FASTQ parse, adapter trim, quality filter, and count matrix generation. Results are joined by the parent process which writes the merged count matrix TSV and provenance JSON. No network calls at runtime; all reference data including adapter sequences and gene annotation GTF files is provided locally by the user.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. CLI: Click 8.x with rich for formatted terminal output. FASTQ parsing: Biopython SeqIO in streaming mode. Parallelism: multiprocessing.Pool. Config: PyYAML with jsonschema validation. Run metadata: SQLite via stdlib sqlite3. Output: TSV count matrices, JSON provenance. Build: pyproject.toml with setuptools. Distribution: PyPI wheel and conda-forge recipe. CI: GitHub Actions with ruff, mypy strict, pytest, and pip-audit.
target_output.functional_feature_map: 'rnapreproc run' accepts --input-dir, --config YAML, --output-dir, --threads, --dry-run and processes all FASTQ files in input-dir in parallel. 'rnapreproc validate-config' checks YAML against jsonschema without touching data. 'rnapreproc summarize' queries SQLite run history with --last-n and --prune-before filters. Per-sample QC report with mean Phred, percent reads passing, and adapter trim rate. Merged count matrix aggregation across all samples in a run.
target_output.system_components: Click CLI entrypoint and subcommand dispatcher. YAML config loader and jsonschema validator. FASTQParser module (Biopython SeqIO streaming in 10K-read chunks). AdapterTrimmer (3-prime adapter removal with Phred-aware trimming). QualityFilter (min Phred threshold and min read length cutoffs). CountMatrixBuilder (HTSeq-count-compatible gene-level aggregation). multiprocessing.Pool orchestrator with tqdm progress to stderr. ProvenanceWriter (JSON manifest with tool version, git SHA, param hash, per-sample checksums). SQLite run history store at ~/.rnapreproc/runs.db.
target_output.workflows: Single-Sample Processing: Worker receives sample FASTQ path and config dict. FASTQParser streams records in 10K-read chunks. AdapterTrimmer removes 3-prime adapters with configurable mismatch tolerance. QualityFilter discards reads below min_phred (default 20) or min_length (default 35 bp). CountMatrixBuilder maps surviving reads to gene features via HTSeq annotation. Worker returns per-sample QC dict and count vector. Batch Cohort Run: Parent process glob-collects all *.fastq.gz files in --input-dir. Pool.map distributes one sample per worker. On completion, parent writes merged_counts.tsv with samples as columns and genes as rows, provenance.json with full parameter set and checksums, and run_summary.tsv with per-sample pass/fail status and read counts.
target_output.data_model: fastq_files (sample_id TEXT, path TEXT, read_count_raw INT, md5_checksum TEXT, run_id TEXT FK). quality_reports (sample_id, run_id, mean_phred REAL, pct_reads_passing REAL, pct_adapter_trimmed REAL). filtering_configs (run_id TEXT PK, min_phred INT, min_length INT, adapter_sequence TEXT, gtf_path TEXT, gtf_checksum TEXT, created_at TEXT). count_matrices (run_id, sample_id, output_tsv_path TEXT, gene_count INT, created_at TEXT). run_metadata (run_id TEXT PK, tool_version TEXT, git_sha TEXT, params_json TEXT, sample_count INT, started_at TEXT, finished_at TEXT, status TEXT). All tables in SQLite at ~/.rnapreproc/runs.db.
target_output.api_design: CLI interface only, no HTTP API. 'rnapreproc run --input-dir ./fastq/ --config qc_config.yaml --output-dir ./results/ --threads 8' runs the full pipeline. 'rnapreproc validate-config --config qc_config.yaml' validates YAML schema and prints warnings for non-standard adapter sequences. 'rnapreproc summarize --last-n 10' tabulates the 10 most recent runs with sample counts and status. 'rnapreproc summarize --prune-before 2025-01-01' removes run records before that date. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 partial failure (some samples failed), 2 fatal error.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Input path validation resolves all paths to absolute form and rejects symlinks outside user-writable directories to prevent traversal. YAML config validated against jsonschema before any execution; unknown keys rejected with descriptive error messages citing the offending key. No credentials or tokens stored or transmitted. Dependency pinning in requirements-lock.txt; GitHub Actions runs pip-audit on every PR to detect known CVEs in transitive dependencies. No network calls at runtime enforces air-gapped HPC compatibility.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distribution via PyPI ('pip install rnapreproc') and conda-forge ('conda install -c conda-forge rnapreproc'). No server deployment. GitHub Actions CI: on pull_request - ruff lint, mypy strict typecheck, pytest with 85% coverage gate, pip-audit CVE scan. On push to 'release/*' branch - build wheel via 'python -m build', publish to PyPI via OIDC trusted publishing (no stored PyPI token), create GitHub release with auto-generated changelog. Conda-forge feedstock PR opened automatically within 24 hours of PyPI publish. Semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) with git tags; breaking changes documented in CHANGELOG.md.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON log written per run to ~/.rnapreproc/logs/RUNID.log with fields: timestamp, level, sample_id, event, detail. Run summary TSV printed to stdout listing each sample with read counts before/after filtering, adapter trim rate, and pass/fail status. SQLite run history queryable via 'rnapreproc summarize' for historical QC trend review. tqdm progress bar to stderr showing samples processed/total and estimated time remaining. No external monitoring infrastructure required.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost for HPC usage. PyPI and conda-forge hosting are free tiers. At 100 samples with 50M reads each, a 32-core node completes preprocessing in approximately 45 minutes using --threads 30. Memory footprint per worker is approximately 2GB for streaming FASTQ parsing; recommend minimum 64GB RAM node for 30-worker runs. Future AWS Batch burst shifts cost to per-vCPU-hour charges only when HPC capacity is exhausted.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Core Pipeline (6 weeks): FASTQParser streaming, AdapterTrimmer, QualityFilter, CountMatrixBuilder, multiprocessing.Pool orchestration, provenance JSON writer, SQLite run history, PyPI and conda-forge packaging, GitHub Actions CI with coverage gate. Phase 2 UX and Validation (3 weeks): validate-config subcommand with jsonschema, summarize subcommand with SQLite queries, rich-formatted terminal output, tqdm progress, improved error messages with YAML line references. Phase 3 Ecosystem Integration (4 weeks): Snakemake rule wrapper, Nextflow module wrapper, benchmark suite documenting throughput on standard HPC hardware configurations.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. ruff for linting and formatting. pytest fixtures use synthetic 10K-read FASTQ test files checked into tests/data/. 85% coverage gate enforced in CI. All public functions type-annotated. Click command parameters document with help strings for '--help' output. CHANGELOG.md updated on every tagged release. ADR documents for algorithm choices such as adapter trimming strategy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Biopython SeqIO chunk size too large causes OOM on SLURM jobs with small RAM allocations. Mitigation: default chunk_size=10000 reads with --chunk-size flag for user override. Risk: GTF annotation file version mismatch between samples produces incompatible count matrices. Mitigation: validate GTF checksum at run start and embed in provenance JSON. Tradeoff: multiprocessing over threading sacrifices shared-memory efficiency for true CPU parallelism on GIL-constrained parsing. Tradeoff: SQLite over plain JSON run history adds a dependency but enables SQL queries for QC trend analysis across many runs.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected multiprocessing.Pool over Dask for simpler dependency footprint on HPC clusters. Chose SQLite over plain JSON run log to enable summarize queries. YAML config format selected over TOML for compatibility with existing bioinformatics tooling conventions. Conda-forge distribution confirmed as primary channel given HPC environment manager constraints.

metadata.notes: Bioinformatics RNA-seq CLI tool with HPC parallel processing and provenance tracking.

project_goal.value: CLI utility for automation engineers to validate, simulate, and deploy liquid handling robot protocols written in a domain-specific scripting language, enabling offline testing against virtual deck layouts before physical instrument runs
project_goal.rationale: Reduces costly material waste and instrument downtime from protocol errors

target_users.value: Laboratory automation engineers developing liquid handling protocols for pipetting robots such as Hamilton, Tecan, and Opentrons instruments
target_users.rationale: Identified as primary users during discovery with lab automation teams

project_class.rationale: Standalone workstation application invoked from terminal during protocol development workflow

capabilities.rationale: Protocol parsing and simulation engine; integration with instrument serial/USB interfaces

complexity_level.rationale: Domain-specific language parser and physics-based volume simulation add algorithmic complexity

risk_level.rationale: Local workstation tool with no network exposure; protocol files are internal engineering artifacts

data_sensitivity.rationale: Protocol scripts and labware definitions are internal IP; no patient data involved

external_exposure.rationale: Runs on engineer workstation with no inbound network ports

access_model.value: No application-level auth; workstation OS user permissions govern file access
access_model.rationale: Local tool on managed workstations with IT-enforced OS access controls

feature_scope.value: Protocol syntax validation, deck conflict detection, volume constraint checking, step-by-step simulation with timing estimates, dead-volume calculations, instrument deploy via USB/serial, GLP provenance output
feature_scope.rationale: Full protocol development and deployment lifecycle

mvp_scope.value: Validate command with syntax and deck conflict checks, simulation command with timing and dead-volume output, structured error report with line numbers
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum feature set to replace manual protocol review

security_baseline.value: Protocol file path validation; no network calls; instrument serial commands validated against labware definition whitelist before dispatch
security_baseline.rationale: Prevent accidental dispatch of malformed commands to physical instrument

frontend_stack.rationale: Terminal interface only; no GUI planned

backend_stack.value: Rust with Clap for CLI argument parsing
backend_stack.rationale: Rust provides memory safety and fast parsing; Clap is the standard Rust CLI framework

data_platform.value: No database; JSON files for labware definitions and simulation results; TOML for instrument configs
data_platform.rationale: Fully offline tool; structured file formats for portability

hosting_target.value: Workstation local install; distributed as compiled binary via GitHub Releases and internal artifact registry
hosting_target.rationale: Lab workstations are Windows and macOS; pre-compiled Rust binaries avoid runtime dependencies

privacy_retention_policy.value: Protocol files and simulation outputs retained per engineer file system; no cloud upload; GLP provenance JSON stored alongside protocol file
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GLP documentation requirements mandate co-located provenance records

future_scope.value: Visual protocol editor GUI, integration with LIMS for protocol versioning, remote deployment to cloud-connected liquid handlers
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 roadmap pending adoption validation

constraints.value: Must ship pre-compiled binaries for Windows x64 and macOS ARM/x64; no runtime dependencies; support offline install
constraints.rationale: Lab workstations often air-gapped with restricted software installation policies

observability_baseline.value: Structured stdout/stderr output; validation errors include file path, line number, and suggested fix; simulation run summary printed to stdout
observability_baseline.rationale: Engineers rely on terminal output during protocol iteration; no telemetry sent externally

execution_preference.value: Semantic versioning; GitHub Actions CI with cross-compilation; release artifacts attached to GitHub Releases
execution_preference.rationale: Simple release workflow appropriate for a small internal tool

project.goal: Validate and simulate liquid handling protocols before physical instrument runs
project.target_users: Lab automation engineers on Windows and macOS workstations

backend.framework: Clap 4.x for subcommand CLI; Serde for JSON/TOML deserialization
backend.parsing: Custom recursive-descent parser for the protocol DSL; pest grammar library

data.storage: JSON labware definition files; TOML instrument configs; JSON simulation result output

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: cargo clippy, cargo test, cross-compilation for win-x64/mac-arm64/mac-x64
devops.distribution: GitHub Releases with binary artifacts; internal Nexus registry mirror

constraints.target_platforms: Windows x64, macOS ARM64, macOS x64

reasoner_reviews.product: The validate-then-simulate-then-deploy workflow matches the actual engineer iteration loop. Structured error reports with line numbers are essential for DSL usability. GLP provenance output is a strong differentiator for labs operating under regulated study conditions.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Rust is well-suited here: memory safety prevents crashes from malformed protocol files, and zero runtime dependencies satisfy the air-gapped install constraint. The pest grammar library handles the DSL parse tree cleanly. Subcommands validate, simulate, and deploy map naturally to the engineering workflow stages.
reasoner_reviews.security: No network surface significantly reduces risk. The key safety concern is preventing accidental dispatch of invalid commands to physical instruments; labware definition whitelisting and pre-dispatch dry-run mode are the primary mitigations.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Pre-compiled binary approach avoids Rust toolchain requirements on lab workstations. Cross-compilation via GitHub Actions matrix covers Windows x64, macOS ARM64, and macOS x64 in a single CI run without maintaining multiple build machines.

backend.service_design: Four Clap subcommands: 'lhctl validate' (syntax and deck conflict check), 'lhctl simulate' (step-by-step virtual run), 'lhctl deploy' (instrument dispatch), 'lhctl inspect-labware' (dump labware definition details). Shared protocol AST type is parsed once and reused across subcommands.
backend.api_patterns: No HTTP API. CLI flags: 'lhctl validate --protocol FILE [--strict]', 'lhctl simulate --protocol FILE --deck-layout JSON [--steps N]', 'lhctl deploy --protocol FILE --instrument-config TOML [--dry-run]'. Structured JSON error output via --json flag for IDE integration.
backend.business_modules: ProtocolParser (pest grammar producing typed AST), DeckValidator (checks labware placement conflicts and pipette reach constraints), VolumeSimulator (dead-volume and overflow calculations per transfer step), TimingEstimator (step duration from instrument speed profiles), InstrumentDriver (serial/USB command dispatch with pre-validation), ProvenanceWriter (GLP JSON with protocol hash, tool version, execution timestamp).
backend.failure_handling: Validation errors collected into a Vec of typed Error structs with file path, line, column, severity, and suggested fix. Simulation aborts on first fatal volume constraint violation. Deploy requires explicit user confirmation prompt unless --yes flag passed.
backend.backend_risks: DSL grammar ambiguities could cause silent misparse; mitigated by comprehensive pest grammar test suite covering edge cases. Instrument serial communication timeout handling must be robust to prevent partial protocol execution.

security.auth_design: No authentication. OS user must have read access to protocol files and write access to output directory.
security.abuse_prevention: Protocol file path resolved to canonical form; reject paths containing '..' traversal sequences. Instrument deploy command validates all protocol steps against a labware definition whitelist before opening serial port. --dry-run flag on deploy performs all validations without sending serial commands.
security.secrets_management: No secrets. Instrument TOML config contains port path and baud rate only; no authentication tokens.
security.security_risks: Malformed protocol files with deeply nested AST nodes could cause stack overflow in recursive parser; mitigated by depth limit in pest grammar rules.

data.entities: protocol_scripts (file_path, dsl_version, step_count, last_validated_at, sha256_hash), deck_layouts (layout_id, instrument_model, labware_positions JSON, created_at), labware_definitions (labware_id, manufacturer, well_count, dead_volume_ul, max_volume_ul, format ENUM 96well/384well/tube_rack), simulation_runs (run_id, protocol_hash, deck_layout_id, total_duration_s, dead_volume_lost_ul, warnings JSON, completed_at), validation_errors (run_id, line_num, col_num, error_code, message, suggested_fix).
data.storage_design: All entities stored as JSON files co-located with protocol files. No database. Labware definitions bundled in the binary as embedded JSON via Rust include_str! macro.
data.data_risks: Protocol file encoding issues (Windows CRLF vs Unix LF) could cause line number mismatches in error reports; normalize line endings in lexer.

devops.deployment_topology: Pre-compiled single-binary executables. No installer required on macOS and Linux. Windows MSI installer generated via cargo-wix.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions matrix: rust stable on ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-14 (ARM). Jobs: 'cargo clippy -- -D warnings', 'cargo test --all', 'cargo build --release', binary artifact upload. On version tag push: create GitHub Release and attach all three platform binaries.
devops.observability_stack: Structured stderr logging via the 'tracing' crate. Verbosity controlled by LHCTL_LOG env var (error/warn/info/debug). Simulation summary table printed to stdout. Validation errors printed as human-readable or JSON via --json flag.
devops.devops_risks: Windows cross-compilation from Linux requires careful handling of path separator differences in test fixtures; use PathBuf throughout the codebase.

target_output.thinking_summary: Building a Rust CLI for liquid handling protocol validation and simulation on air-gapped lab workstations. Key decisions: pest grammar library for the protocol DSL parser produces typed AST reused across all subcommands, labware definitions embedded in the binary via include_str! eliminate runtime file dependency, structured JSON error output enables IDE integration, and pre-deploy dry-run mode prevents accidental instrument dispatch.
target_output.title: LHCTL Liquid Handling Protocol CLI Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Rust command-line tool that allows laboratory automation engineers to validate, simulate, and deploy liquid handling robot protocols without physically running the instrument. Parses a domain-specific scripting language using a pest grammar into a typed AST, checks deck conflicts and volume constraints, runs step-by-step simulation with timing and dead-volume estimates, and dispatches validated protocols to connected instruments via serial/USB with GLP provenance JSON output.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single compiled Rust binary with four Clap subcommands sharing a common ProtocolParser that produces a typed AST from the DSL source file. The validate subcommand runs DeckValidator and VolumeSimulator in check-only mode collecting all errors into a structured report. The simulate subcommand executes a full virtual run through TimingEstimator producing per-step duration and cumulative dead-volume statistics. The deploy subcommand requires a prior successful validate pass, renders instrument-specific serial commands via InstrumentDriver, and optionally dispatches them after a confirmation prompt. ProvenanceWriter creates a GLP JSON file co-located with the protocol containing the protocol SHA-256 hash, tool version, git commit, and execution timestamp.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Rust stable 1.78+. CLI: Clap 4.x with derive macros. DSL parsing: pest 2.x grammar library with recursive-descent AST. Serialization: Serde with serde_json and toml. Labware data: embedded JSON via include_str! at compile time. Logging: tracing crate with LHCTL_LOG env control. Testing: cargo test with synthetic protocol fixtures. Build: cargo with cross for platform targets. Distribution: GitHub Releases with win-x64 MSI (cargo-wix), macOS ARM64, and macOS x64 binaries.
target_output.functional_feature_map: 'lhctl validate --protocol FILE [--strict] [--json]' checks DSL syntax, deck placement conflicts, pipette reach constraints, and volume bounds; outputs structured errors with line/column and suggested fixes. 'lhctl simulate --protocol FILE --deck-layout JSON [--steps N]' runs the virtual execution engine producing step-by-step timing and dead-volume report. 'lhctl deploy --protocol FILE --instrument-config TOML [--dry-run] [--yes]' dispatches the protocol to a connected liquid handler after validation and confirmation. 'lhctl inspect-labware [--labware-id ID]' dumps embedded labware definitions including well count, dead-volume, and max-volume specs.
target_output.system_components: ProtocolParser (pest grammar parsing DSL source into a typed Protocol AST with steps, deck declarations, and variable bindings). DeckValidator (checks labware placement overlaps, pipette reach zones, and tip rack assignments per instrument model). VolumeSimulator (tracks per-well liquid levels across transfer steps, detects overflow and insufficient-volume conditions, computes cumulative dead-volume lost). TimingEstimator (calculates per-step and total protocol duration from instrument speed profiles in TOML config). InstrumentDriver (constructs instrument-specific serial command sequences from validated AST, manages serial port lifecycle). ProvenanceWriter (generates GLP-compliant JSON with tool version, git SHA, protocol hash, operator env variables, and step count).
target_output.workflows: Protocol Validation Workflow: Engineer invokes 'lhctl validate --protocol assay_v3.lhp'. ProtocolParser lexes and parses DSL source producing a Protocol AST or structured parse errors with line/column references. DeckValidator traverses the AST checking labware placement against the deck layout, flagging conflicts and unreachable well positions. VolumeSimulator checks each aspirate/dispense step against current well states detecting under-volume and overflow conditions. All errors and warnings are collected into a ValidationReport serialized to stdout as human-readable table or JSON via --json flag. Simulation and Pre-Deploy Check: Engineer runs 'lhctl simulate --protocol assay_v3.lhp --deck-layout 96well_layout.json'. TimingEstimator processes each transfer step computing duration from instrument aspiration and dispense speed profiles. Dead-volume calculator accumulates residual volume left in source wells after each aspirate. Simulation summary lists total estimated runtime, total dead-volume lost in microliters, and any volume warnings. On passing validation, engineer runs 'lhctl deploy --dry-run' to verify serial command rendering before final 'lhctl deploy --yes' sends commands to the instrument.
target_output.data_model: protocol_scripts (file_path TEXT, dsl_version TEXT, step_count INT, last_validated_at TEXT, sha256_hash TEXT). deck_layouts (layout_id TEXT, instrument_model TEXT, labware_positions JSON, total_wells INT, created_at TEXT). labware_definitions (labware_id TEXT PK, manufacturer TEXT, well_count INT, dead_volume_ul REAL, max_volume_ul REAL, format TEXT ENUM 96well/384well/tube_rack/reservoir; embedded in binary as JSON). simulation_runs (run_id TEXT, protocol_hash TEXT, deck_layout_id TEXT, total_duration_s REAL, dead_volume_lost_ul REAL, step_count INT, warnings JSON, completed_at TEXT). validation_errors (run_id TEXT, line_num INT, col_num INT, error_code TEXT, severity TEXT, message TEXT, suggested_fix TEXT). All persisted as co-located JSON files; no database.
target_output.api_design: CLI-only interface. 'lhctl validate --protocol FILE [--strict] [--json]' - validates syntax, deck conflicts, and volume constraints; exits 0 if clean, 1 if warnings only (--strict promotes to error), 2 if errors. 'lhctl simulate --protocol FILE --deck-layout JSON [--steps N]' - runs virtual execution; exits 0 on success, 2 on fatal simulation error. 'lhctl deploy --protocol FILE --instrument-config TOML [--dry-run] [--yes]' - dispatches to instrument; --dry-run renders commands without sending; exits 0 on success, 2 on serial error. 'lhctl inspect-labware [--labware-id ID] [--json]' - lists or dumps labware definitions. All subcommands accept --log-level ENV and --json for machine-parseable output.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Input path validation: all file arguments resolved via Rust fs::canonicalize rejecting paths with traversal components. Instrument deploy validates every aspirate and dispense volume against labware max_volume_ul whitelist before opening serial port. --dry-run mode renders full serial command sequence without port dispatch enabling review before physical execution. No credentials or network connections. GLP provenance JSON written co-located with protocol file to satisfy Good Laboratory Practice documentation requirements for validated studies. cargo-audit runs in CI to detect known CVEs in Rust dependency tree.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Pre-compiled single-binary distribution eliminates Rust toolchain requirement on lab workstations. GitHub Actions matrix builds on ubuntu-latest (for cross to win-x64), windows-latest (native win-x64 MSI via cargo-wix), and macos-14 (ARM64 with universal binary for x64 via lipo). CI pipeline on every PR: 'cargo clippy -- -D warnings' (zero lint warnings policy), 'cargo test --all' (unit and integration tests on all platforms), 'cargo audit' (dependency CVE scan). On 'v*' tag push: build and attach win-x64 MSI, macOS ARM64 .tar.gz, and macOS x64 .tar.gz to GitHub Release with auto-generated release notes from CHANGELOG.md. Internal Nexus registry mirrors GitHub Releases for air-gapped lab network distribution.
target_output.observability: Structured logging via tracing crate controlled by LHCTL_LOG env var (error/warn/info/debug). Validation results printed as formatted table to stdout with error counts and severity breakdown. --json flag outputs ValidationReport as machine-parseable JSON for IDE plugin integration. Simulation summary table shows per-step timing, cumulative dead-volume, and warnings. Simulation run details optionally written to FILE.sim.json co-located with protocol. No external telemetry.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI for open-source; internal runners used for air-gapped builds. Rust binary is single statically-linked executable under 20MB. Simulation scales linearly with protocol step count; a 500-step protocol simulates in under 100ms on a modern workstation. Future GUI layer would add Electron or Tauri packaging cost considerations.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Core Validation (6 weeks): pest grammar for DSL, ProtocolParser, DeckValidator with deck conflict detection, VolumeSimulator for overflow and under-volume checks, structured error report with line/column, --json flag, GitHub Actions CI with cross-platform build matrix, GitHub Releases binary distribution. Phase 2 Simulation and Deploy (5 weeks): TimingEstimator with instrument speed profiles, full simulate subcommand, InstrumentDriver for Hamilton and Opentrons serial protocols, --dry-run deploy mode, ProvenanceWriter GLP JSON output. Phase 3 Ecosystem Integration (3 weeks): inspect-labware subcommand, cargo-wix Windows MSI, internal Nexus registry upload, Neovim and VS Code extension integration via --json output mode.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust stable with clippy -D warnings policy; zero clippy warnings enforced in CI. All public types and functions documented with rustdoc comments. Integration tests use synthetic .lhp protocol files in tests/fixtures/. Property-based tests via proptest for parser fuzz coverage on random DSL inputs. cargo-audit in CI for dependency CVE scanning. Semantic versioning with CHANGELOG.md updated on every release. Protocol DSL grammar changes documented in DSL-CHANGELOG.md with migration notes.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: DSL grammar ambiguities cause silent misparse producing incorrect AST. Mitigation: comprehensive pest grammar test suite with golden-file tests for all production protocol patterns. Risk: Instrument serial timing assumptions in TimingEstimator diverge from actual instrument firmware behavior across versions. Mitigation: instrument speed profiles versioned in TOML config with explicit firmware version field. Tradeoff: embedded labware definitions in binary simplify offline distribution but require a new binary release to add labware types. Tradeoff: pest grammar library over nom combinator parsers trades some performance for grammar readability and better error recovery.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected pest grammar library over nom for better DSL error reporting with source location metadata. Chose co-located JSON output files over a local SQLite database to maintain portability and GLP co-location requirements. Platform distribution via GitHub Releases binary artifacts confirmed over Homebrew due to air-gapped network constraints. Deploy confirmation prompt defaulting to interactive mode with --yes flag for CI automation use cases.

metadata.notes: Rust CLI for liquid handling robot protocol validation with offline simulation and GLP provenance.

project_goal.value: Python library providing cost-optimized cloud storage adapters for large experimental datasets, automatically selecting between hot, warm, and cold storage tiers based on access frequency and data age to minimize cloud spend in scientific computing workflows
project_goal.rationale: Scientific datasets can accumulate petabytes of rarely-accessed archival data; automated tiering reduces cloud storage bills significantly

target_users.value: Computational scientists and bioinformatics pipeline developers who manage large experimental datasets on AWS S3 and want to minimize storage costs
target_users.rationale: Primary audience identified via user research with academic and pharma computational teams

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a Python package; no hosted service or UI

capabilities.rationale: Storage policy engine, dataset manifest tracking, AWS S3 API integration

complexity_level.rationale: Tiering policy engine and cost estimation logic require careful state management and AWS API orchestration

risk_level.rationale: No sensitive data stored in the library; operates on user-provided S3 paths using caller credentials

data_sensitivity.rationale: Experimental datasets are internal research data; library does not store or transmit dataset contents

external_exposure.rationale: Installed and invoked locally in researcher workflows; calls AWS APIs via boto3 using caller credentials

access_model.value: No library-level auth; uses caller AWS IAM credentials via boto3 default credential chain
access_model.rationale: Library is a passive tool; AWS IAM governs actual S3 access

feature_scope.value: Storage policy YAML configuration, automatic hot/warm/cold tier transitions based on last-access timestamps, cost estimation dry-run mode, dataset manifest tracking, bulk tier migration, access frequency instrumentation
feature_scope.rationale: Full automated lifecycle management for scientific datasets

mvp_scope.value: Policy-driven tier transitions from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval, cost estimation dry-run, dataset manifest registration
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable cost-saving feature for initial release

security_baseline.value: No credentials stored in library; all AWS calls use boto3 default credential chain; warn if credentials are hardcoded in policy YAML
security_baseline.rationale: Prevent accidental credential leakage in policy configuration files

frontend_stack.rationale: No UI; programmatic and CLI-driven usage only

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12
backend_stack.rationale: Target language for scientific computing workflows; broad compatibility with existing pipeline tools

data_platform.value: Local SQLite for dataset manifest and access log tracking; AWS S3 as the managed storage backend
data_platform.rationale: SQLite provides queryable local state without requiring a database server

hosting_target.value: PyPI package; conda-forge recipe
hosting_target.rationale: Standard Python distribution channels for scientific computing community

privacy_retention_policy.value: Library logs access metadata locally only; no dataset contents transmitted to third parties; local SQLite manifest prunable by caller
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Researchers control their data; library is a local tool

future_scope.value: Azure Blob Storage and GCS adapters, integration with Snakemake and Nextflow for automated pipeline-driven tiering, multi-account AWS Organizations support
future_scope.rationale: Multi-cloud roadmap pending initial AWS validation

constraints.value: Python 3.10+ compatibility; boto3 as the only non-stdlib heavy dependency; no mandatory background daemon
constraints.rationale: Minimal dependency footprint required for compatibility with locked scientific pipeline environments

observability_baseline.value: Structured logging via stdlib logging module; transition events logged with dataset ID, source tier, target tier, object count, and estimated cost delta
observability_baseline.rationale: Callers need audit trail of what was moved and when for cost attribution

execution_preference.value: Semantic versioning; GitHub Actions CI; PyPI trusted publishing on version tags
execution_preference.rationale: Standard open-source library release workflow

project.goal: Python library for automated cost-optimized S3 storage tiering of scientific datasets
project.target_users: Computational scientists managing large experimental datasets on AWS

backend.libraries: boto3 for AWS S3 and Glacier API calls; PyYAML for policy config; sqlite3 stdlib for manifest

data.tracking: Dataset manifests, access logs, tier transitions, cost estimates

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: ruff, mypy, pytest with moto S3 mocks, PyPI trusted publish on tag

reasoner_reviews.product: The hot/warm/cold tiering model maps cleanly to S3 Standard, S3 Standard-IA (or Glacier Instant Retrieval), and S3 Glacier Deep Archive. Cost estimation dry-run mode is a critical adoption feature as scientists need to see projected savings before committing to transitions.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: A thin adapter pattern over boto3 with a policy engine is the right architecture for a library. SQLite for local manifest avoids the complexity of a server while enabling queryable access logs for tiering decisions. The design must support idempotent transitions so that repeated policy runs do not trigger redundant S3 copy operations.
reasoner_reviews.security: Credential handling is the primary concern; boto3 default chain (env vars, ~/.aws/credentials, instance role) is correct. The library must never accept or store AWS credentials as constructor arguments. YAML policy files should be validated to ensure they do not contain credential-like strings.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Moto library for S3 mocking is essential for unit tests that run without AWS credentials. The no-daemon constraint means tiering decisions must be invoked explicitly by the caller, either programmatically or via a cron job, rather than running as a background process.

backend.service_design: Public API surface: DatastoreClient class with register_dataset(), apply_policy(), estimate_costs(), and get_manifest() methods. TieringEngine internal class orchestrates S3 object listing, access log analysis, and StorageTransitioner calls. PolicyParser validates and loads YAML policy files.
backend.api_patterns: Python package API. DatastoreClient(profile='default', manifest_db='~/.datastore/manifest.db'). register_dataset(s3_uri, dataset_type, policy_id). apply_policy(policy_id, dry_run=False). estimate_costs(policy_id). get_manifest(dataset_id). All methods return typed dataclasses; errors raised as DatastoreError subclasses.
backend.business_modules: PolicyParser (YAML policy loading and jsonschema validation), AccessLogAnalyzer (queries SQLite access_events to compute last-access timestamps per S3 prefix), TieringEngine (decides tier transitions per object based on policy rules), StorageTransitioner (executes S3 copy-to-destination-storage-class then delete-source via boto3), CostEstimator (computes projected monthly savings using AWS S3 pricing constants), ManifestStore (SQLite CRUD for dataset_manifests and tier_transitions tables).
backend.failure_handling: Idempotency: transition checks current S3 storage class before issuing copy to avoid redundant operations. Partial transitions: on S3 error mid-batch, completed transitions are committed to SQLite and the batch resumes from checkpoint on next apply_policy call. All S3 API errors wrapped in DatastoreError with original boto3 error and affected S3 key.
backend.backend_risks: S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval restore latency (milliseconds) is acceptable but Glacier Deep Archive requires 12-hour restore; library must expose restore_dataset() helper and warn users of retrieval time in documentation.

security.auth_design: boto3 default credential chain only. Library never accepts AWS keys as constructor parameters. Credential-like string pattern detection in PolicyParser warns if YAML contains strings matching AWS key patterns.
security.secrets_management: No secrets stored. SQLite manifest contains only S3 URIs, storage classes, and timestamps.
security.security_risks: S3 bucket policies may restrict the caller IAM role from performing storage class transitions; library raises descriptive error with required IAM actions when boto3 returns AccessDenied.

data.entities: storage_policies (policy_id TEXT PK, policy_yaml TEXT, hot_tier TEXT, warm_tier TEXT, cold_tier TEXT, hot_max_age_days INT, warm_max_age_days INT, created_at TEXT), dataset_manifests (dataset_id TEXT PK, s3_uri TEXT, dataset_type TEXT, policy_id TEXT FK, current_tier TEXT, registered_at TEXT, last_transitioned_at TEXT), access_logs (log_id TEXT PK, dataset_id TEXT FK, accessed_at TEXT, access_type TEXT ENUM read/write/list), tier_transitions (transition_id TEXT PK, dataset_id TEXT FK, from_tier TEXT, to_tier TEXT, object_count INT, bytes_moved INT, estimated_monthly_savings_usd REAL, dry_run BOOL, transitioned_at TEXT), cost_estimates (estimate_id TEXT PK, policy_id TEXT FK, dataset_id TEXT FK, current_monthly_usd REAL, projected_monthly_usd REAL, savings_usd REAL, estimated_at TEXT).
data.storage_design: SQLite at ~/.datastore/manifest.db. S3 objects remain in caller-owned buckets; library only modifies storage class metadata.
data.migration_notes: Schema migrations handled via simple version table and ALTER TABLE scripts; no migration framework dependency required for this schema size.
data.data_risks: S3 object versioning buckets require storage class changes applied per version; library must detect versioned buckets and apply transitions to all non-current versions if policy specifies.

devops.deployment_topology: PyPI wheel distribution. No server. Install via 'pip install datastore-tiering' or 'conda install -c conda-forge datastore-tiering'.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: ruff lint, mypy strict, pytest with moto 5.x S3 mocks (no real AWS credentials needed in CI), coverage gate 90%. On 'v*' tag push: build wheel, publish to PyPI via OIDC trusted publishing, open conda-forge feedstock PR.
devops.observability_stack: Structured logging via stdlib logging with logger name 'datastore.tiering'. Transition events logged at INFO with dataset_id, from_tier, to_tier, object_count, bytes_moved, dry_run flag.
devops.devops_risks: moto S3 mock coverage does not fully replicate S3 storage class copy behavior for all Glacier tiers; integration test suite against a real sandbox AWS account recommended before major releases.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a Python library SDK for automated S3 storage tiering. Key decisions: SQLite local manifest enables idempotent transitions and queryable access history without requiring a server, boto3 default credential chain prevents credential misuse, moto library enables full unit test coverage without real AWS accounts, and a dry-run mode on all mutating operations is essential for researcher confidence before committing tier transitions.
target_output.title: Datastore Tiering Library Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 library package that intercepts scientific dataset read/write operations to S3 and automatically transitions aging datasets through hot (S3 Standard), warm (S3 Standard-IA), and cold (S3 Glacier Instant Retrieval or Deep Archive) tiers based on configurable YAML policies. Maintains a local SQLite manifest of dataset registrations, access events, and transition history, and provides a cost estimation dry-run mode that projects monthly savings without executing any S3 mutations.
target_output.architecture_overview: Package-level API surface via DatastoreClient class backed by four internal modules: PolicyParser loads and validates YAML tiering policies, AccessLogAnalyzer queries the SQLite manifest for last-access timestamps per dataset, TieringEngine computes per-dataset tier decisions by comparing access age against policy thresholds, and StorageTransitioner executes S3 copy-to-target-storage-class operations via boto3 with idempotency checks. CostEstimator uses embedded AWS S3 pricing constants to project monthly savings for any policy before execution. No server component; library operates entirely within the caller process using the boto3 default credential chain.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Python 3.12 (compatible with 3.10+). AWS SDK: boto3 1.34+. YAML config: PyYAML. Schema validation: jsonschema. Local manifest: sqlite3 (stdlib). Logging: stdlib logging. Type annotations: full mypy strict coverage. Build: pyproject.toml with setuptools. Test: pytest with moto 5.x S3 mocks. Lint: ruff. Distribution: PyPI wheel and conda-forge recipe.
target_output.functional_feature_map: DatastoreClient.register_dataset(s3_uri, dataset_type, policy_id) - registers a dataset S3 prefix in the local SQLite manifest with its assigned tiering policy. apply_policy(policy_id, dry_run=False) - evaluates all registered datasets under the policy, computes tier transitions for datasets exceeding age thresholds, and either executes or reports transitions. estimate_costs(policy_id) - projects current vs post-transition monthly S3 cost for all datasets under the policy. get_manifest(dataset_id) - returns current tier, last transition, and access history for a registered dataset. restore_dataset(dataset_id) - initiates Glacier restore for cold-tier datasets with retrieval tier options.
target_output.system_components: DatastoreClient (public API entry point; validates arguments, delegates to internal modules). PolicyParser (YAML loading, jsonschema policy schema validation, credential-pattern warning on YAML values). AccessLogAnalyzer (SQLite query for last_accessed_at per dataset_id, computes age in days). TieringEngine (applies policy hot/warm/cold age thresholds to access ages, returns TierDecision dataclasses). StorageTransitioner (boto3 S3 copy with target StorageClass parameter, idempotency check via head_object storage class comparison, batch paginator for large prefixes). CostEstimator (embedded S3 pricing constants for Standard/Standard-IA/Glacier-IR/Glacier-DA per GB, computes savings projection). ManifestStore (sqlite3 CRUD for all manifest tables with WAL mode for concurrent access).
target_output.workflows: Dataset Registration and Policy Assignment: Caller invokes register_dataset('s3://lab-data/rna-seq-2025/', dataset_type='sequencing', policy_id='research-archive-v1'). ManifestStore creates a dataset_manifests record with current_tier='hot'. AccessLogAnalyzer begins tracking access events when caller wraps S3 reads through the provided DatastoreFile context manager. Policy Application Run: Caller or cron job invokes apply_policy('research-archive-v1', dry_run=False). AccessLogAnalyzer computes days_since_last_access for each registered dataset. TieringEngine compares each dataset age against policy warm_max_age_days (e.g. 30) and cold_max_age_days (e.g. 90). For datasets exceeding thresholds, StorageTransitioner paginates S3 objects under the dataset prefix, checks current storage class via head_object, and issues copy requests only for objects not already at target class. Completed transitions recorded in tier_transitions table. Summary logged at INFO with object counts and bytes moved. Cost Estimation Dry-Run: estimate_costs('research-archive-v1') reads current object sizes and storage classes from S3 inventory (or head_object sampling), applies pricing constants, and returns a CostEstimate dataclass with current_monthly_usd and projected_monthly_usd without mutating any objects.
target_output.data_model: storage_policies (policy_id TEXT PK, policy_yaml TEXT, hot_tier TEXT, warm_tier TEXT, cold_tier TEXT, hot_max_age_days INT, warm_max_age_days INT, created_at TEXT). dataset_manifests (dataset_id TEXT PK, s3_uri TEXT, dataset_type TEXT, policy_id TEXT FK, current_tier TEXT, registered_at TEXT, last_transitioned_at TEXT). access_logs (log_id TEXT PK, dataset_id TEXT FK, accessed_at TEXT, access_type TEXT ENUM read/write/list). tier_transitions (transition_id TEXT PK, dataset_id TEXT FK, from_tier TEXT, to_tier TEXT, object_count INT, bytes_moved INT, estimated_monthly_savings_usd REAL, dry_run BOOL, transitioned_at TEXT). cost_estimates (estimate_id TEXT PK, policy_id TEXT FK, dataset_id TEXT FK, current_monthly_usd REAL, projected_monthly_usd REAL, savings_usd REAL, estimated_at TEXT). All tables in SQLite at ~/.datastore/manifest.db with WAL journal mode.
target_output.api_design: Python package API. from datastore_tiering import DatastoreClient. client = DatastoreClient(profile='default'). client.register_dataset(s3_uri='s3://lab-data/rna-seq-2025/', dataset_type='sequencing', policy_id='archive-v1'). result = client.apply_policy('archive-v1', dry_run=True) returns ApplyResult(transitioned=[], dry_run_would_transition=[...]). estimate = client.estimate_costs('archive-v1') returns CostEstimate(current_monthly_usd=450.0, projected_monthly_usd=85.0, savings_usd=365.0). manifest = client.get_manifest('rna-seq-2025') returns DatasetManifest with current_tier, last_transitioned_at, access_count. All return types are typed dataclasses. Errors: DatastoreError base, PolicyNotFoundError, DatasetNotRegisteredError, S3AccessDeniedError with required_iam_actions list.
target_output.security_and_compliance: boto3 default credential chain (env vars, ~/.aws/credentials, EC2 instance role) used exclusively; DatastoreClient constructor never accepts AWS key parameters to prevent credential hardcoding. PolicyParser scans YAML string values against AWS key format regex and emits a warning if a potential credential is detected. ManifestStore SQLite file has 0600 permissions set on creation. No dataset contents read by the library; only S3 object metadata (storage class, size, last-modified) accessed. cargo-audit equivalent: pip-audit run in CI on every PR to detect CVEs in boto3 and transitive dependencies.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: PyPI distribution via 'pip install datastore-tiering'. conda-forge recipe for scientific environment users. GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy strict mode, pytest suite using moto 5.x S3 mocks (zero real AWS calls in CI), 90% coverage gate, pip-audit CVE scan. On 'v*' tag push: build wheel via 'python -m build', publish to PyPI via OIDC trusted publishing, automatically open conda-forge feedstock PR. No server deployment, no Dockerfile, no container registry. Semantic versioning; CHANGELOG.md updated on each release; breaking API changes increment MAJOR version.
target_output.observability: stdlib logging with logger name 'datastore.tiering'; callers configure log level via logging.basicConfig or propagation to root logger. Transition events logged at INFO: dataset_id, from_tier, to_tier, object_count, bytes_moved, dry_run bool, duration_ms. Policy evaluation logged at DEBUG per dataset with computed days_since_last_access and tier decision. All SQLite mutations include a transition record enabling full audit trail query via ManifestStore.get_transition_history(dataset_id).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero library infrastructure cost. AWS S3 storage cost reduction is the primary value proposition: a 10TB dataset aged beyond 90 days costs approximately $230/month on S3 Standard versus $4/month on Glacier Deep Archive. StorageTransitioner uses S3 paginator in batches of 1000 objects to handle datasets with millions of files without memory issues. Cost estimation uses embedded pricing constants updated per library release; real-time pricing API integration is out of scope.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Core Tiering (5 weeks): DatastoreClient API, PolicyParser with YAML schema, AccessLogAnalyzer, TieringEngine with hot/warm/cold decisions, StorageTransitioner with idempotency, ManifestStore SQLite schema, PyPI packaging, GitHub Actions CI with moto mocks. Phase 2 Cost Intelligence (3 weeks): CostEstimator with S3 pricing constants, estimate_costs() method, cost projection in apply_policy dry-run output, pricing constants update mechanism. Phase 3 Ecosystem Integration (4 weeks): restore_dataset() for Glacier retrieval, DatastoreFile context manager for transparent access logging, Snakemake storage plugin wrapper, conda-forge recipe and feedstock.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode. ruff for linting and formatting. All public API methods type-annotated with typed return dataclasses. pytest with moto 5.x S3 fixtures; no real AWS calls in unit tests. 90% test coverage gate. Integration test suite in tests/integration/ marked with pytest.mark.integration requires real AWS credentials and a sandbox bucket. CHANGELOG.md updated on every release. Deprecation warnings emitted one MINOR version before removal of any public API.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: moto S3 mock does not fully replicate storage class copy behavior for all Glacier sub-tiers causing test coverage gaps. Mitigation: integration test suite against real sandbox bucket run manually before major releases. Risk: caller S3 bucket uses object versioning requiring per-version storage class transitions; initial release handles only current object versions. Mitigation: detect versioned buckets and raise VersionedBucketWarning with documentation link. Tradeoff: SQLite local manifest over DynamoDB remote table trades cloud-native auditability for zero-infrastructure simplicity appropriate for a library package. Tradeoff: synchronous boto3 API over async aioboto3 keeps the API surface simple at the cost of blocking the caller thread during large batch transitions.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected SQLite over DynamoDB for local manifest to eliminate mandatory AWS infrastructure for library users. moto chosen for S3 mocking over LocalStack for lighter CI footprint. Pricing constants embedded in source over real-time AWS Pricing API to avoid network dependency in estimate_costs(). conda-forge distribution confirmed in addition to PyPI for scientific environment compatibility.

metadata.notes: Python SDK for automated S3 storage tiering with YAML policies and SQLite manifest tracking.

project_goal.value: Regulated API service for pharmaceutical research organizations to submit, retrieve, and audit clinical trial data packages in compliance with FDA 21 CFR Part 11, including electronic signatures, immutable audit trails, and validated change control
project_goal.rationale: Regulatory submission workflows require cryptographically signed data packages with tamper-evident audit histories

target_users.value: Principal investigators, data managers, and regulatory affairs personnel at pharmaceutical research organizations submitting clinical trial data to sponsor systems
target_users.rationale: Primary users identified in GCP-aligned user research

project_class.rationale: Backend API service consumed by pharma partner systems; no frontend delivered in this scope

capabilities.rationale: REST API with authenticated partner access, immutable data storage, and audit analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Electronic signature workflows, immutable audit trail design, and eCTD schema validation require advanced implementation

risk_level.rationale: Regulatory non-compliance could invalidate clinical trial submissions and expose company to FDA enforcement

data_sensitivity.rationale: Clinical trial data packages may contain patient-level efficacy and safety data under HIPAA

external_exposure.rationale: API consumed by authenticated pharmaceutical partner organizations via dedicated API credentials

access_model.value: Custom JWT with TOTP MFA for human operators; API key with IP allowlisting for system-to-system partner integrations; RBAC with investigator/data-manager/auditor/admin roles
access_model.rationale: 21 CFR Part 11 mandates unique electronic identity binding for all signed records

feature_scope.value: Clinical trial data package submission, eCTD schema validation, electronic signature capture, immutable audit trail, change control workflow, regulatory auditor query API, tamper-evident log export
feature_scope.rationale: Full 21 CFR Part 11 compliance feature set

mvp_scope.value: Data package submission with eCTD validation, electronic signature with TOTP, immutable audit trail, and auditor query endpoint
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum feature set to satisfy FDA Part 11 requirements for first pilot study

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 KMS encryption at rest, TOTP MFA for all human users, API key rotation every 90 days, mTLS for partner-to-partner integrations, append-only audit log with SHA-256 chaining
security_baseline.rationale: 21 CFR Part 11 and HIPAA minimum technical controls

frontend_stack.value: None - API service only; partner organizations provide their own UI
frontend_stack.rationale: Scope is backend API; UI is delivered by partner integration teams

backend_stack.value: Java 21 with Spring Boot 3.2
backend_stack.rationale: Java ecosystem provides mature cryptographic libraries, Spring Security for auth, and enterprise-grade auditing frameworks

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for transactional data; append-only audit log table with hash chaining; S3 for immutable data package blob storage
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL provides ACID guarantees required for signature records; S3 Object Lock for immutable storage

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1 with Multi-AZ Aurora PostgreSQL
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with HIPAA-eligible AWS infrastructure; Multi-AZ for high availability

privacy_retention_policy.value: Clinical trial records retained 15 years per ICH E6 GCP; audit logs retained indefinitely for regulatory inspection; patient-identifiable data pseudonymized at ingestion
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FDA and ICH E6 retention requirements for clinical trial documentation

future_scope.value: HL7 FHIR integration for direct EHR data import, EU EMA submission format support, automated eCTD compilation pipeline
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap for broader regulatory submission coverage

constraints.value: Must operate within AWS GovCloud or HIPAA-eligible region; SOC 2 Type II certification required within 12 months; all code changes require IQ/OQ/PQ validation documentation
constraints.rationale: Pharma industry validation requirements and SOC 2 contractual obligation

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM with distributed tracing; CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON; PagerDuty for SLA-affecting alerts; all regulatory-significant events logged to append-only audit table
observability_baseline.rationale: High-availability API requires 24/7 monitoring with escalation

execution_preference.value: Waterfall-like validation phases (IQ/OQ/PQ) with agile delivery within each phase; change control process for production deployments
execution_preference.rationale: Pharmaceutical software validation requirements mandate documented testing evidence for each release

compliance_context.value: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 (electronic records and signatures), ICH E6 GCP guidelines, SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA Privacy and Security Rules, AWS Business Associate Agreement required
compliance_context.rationale: Clinical trial data systems handling PHI must comply with all enumerated regulations

project.goal: 21 CFR Part 11 compliant API for clinical trial data package submission and audit
project.target_users: Pharma investigators, data managers, regulatory auditors

security.auth: Custom JWT with TOTP MFA; API key for system integrations

data.blob: S3 with Object Lock for immutable data packages
data.cache: Redis 7 for JWT blacklist and rate limiting

devops.ci: Jenkins with Maven; IQ/OQ/PQ validation documentation generated in CI

open_questions.fhir: HL7 FHIR import deferred to phase 2

reasoner_reviews.product: The 21 CFR Part 11 feature set is precisely scoped: electronic signature with TOTP binding, immutable audit trail with hash chaining, and eCTD schema validation are the non-negotiable compliance requirements. The auditor query API is a critical feature as FDA inspectors need direct read access to audit trails during inspections.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Spring Boot 3.2 with Spring Security provides the mature authentication primitives needed for TOTP MFA and JWT management. The append-only audit table with SHA-256 hash chaining is a well-established pattern for 21 CFR Part 11 tamper evidence. S3 Object Lock with Compliance mode prevents any deletion or overwrite of submitted data packages, satisfying the immutable record requirement.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS for partner system integrations is essential given the partner_facing exposure with high-risk health data. JWT blacklist in Redis covers logout and API key revocation scenarios. 90-day API key rotation policy must be enforced programmatically, not just documented. TOTP binding to unique user identity satisfies 21 CFR Part 11 Section 11.10(d) unique identification requirement.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: IQ/OQ/PQ validation documentation is a hard constraint that affects CI pipeline design; each build must generate traceability matrices linking code changes to test evidence. SOC 2 Type II within 12 months requires immediate logging and access control instrumentation from day one.

backend.service_design: Spring Boot 3.2 application with domain modules: submissions (TrialSubmissionController, TrialSubmissionService), signatures (ElectronicSignatureService, TOTPVerifier), audit (AuditEventService, AuditChainVerifier), change-control (ChangeControlWorkflowService), and analytics (AuditQueryService for regulatory analytics).
backend.api_patterns: RESTful API versioned at /api/v1. POST /submissions (submit data package), GET /submissions/{id} (retrieve with signature status), POST /submissions/{id}/sign (electronic signature capture), PATCH /submissions/{id}/change-control (initiate change request), GET /audit-trail (query audit events with filters), GET /audit-trail/export (tamper-evident export). OpenAPI 3.1 spec published at /api/v1/openapi.json.
backend.failure_handling: Idempotency keys required on all POST submission endpoints to prevent duplicate submissions. Electronic signature endpoint is transactional: TOTP verification, signature record write, and audit event write occur in a single database transaction or fully roll back. Circuit breaker on S3 Object Lock write with retry-with-backoff.
backend.scaling_notes: Stateless API containers behind ALB; JWT verification stateless except blacklist check. Redis cluster for JWT blacklist scales horizontally. Aurora read replica for audit query API to isolate read load from submission writes.
backend.backend_risks: eCTD schema validation against evolving FDA schema versions requires version-pinned schema artifacts in the codebase, not fetched at runtime, to ensure reproducibility and avoid network dependency during submissions.

security.auth_design: Custom JWT service issues 15-minute access tokens and 8-hour refresh tokens. TOTP enforced at login and at electronic signature capture. API keys for partner system integrations stored as bcrypt hashes; raw key returned once at creation. mTLS client certificates required for all partner-to-partner integrations.
security.authorization_model: RBAC with four roles: investigator (submit, sign own packages), data_manager (submit on behalf, initiate change control), auditor (read-only audit trail access), admin (user management, API key issuance). Spring Security method-level @PreAuthorize annotations.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Every submission, signature, retrieval, and change control event writes an immutable audit_trail_events record with user_id, action, resource_id, ip_address, user_agent, timestamp, and prev_hash (SHA-256 of previous record). AuditChainVerifier service recomputes chain hashes on demand for inspection validation.
security.security_risks: TOTP clock skew between client and server could cause verification failures; implement 30-second window tolerance with drift detection. Redis JWT blacklist must be replicated across AZs to prevent auth bypass on Redis failover.

data.entities: trial_submissions (id UUID PK, study_protocol_id FK, sponsor_org_id FK, submitted_by UUID FK, submission_type ENUM initial/amendment/supplement, ectd_schema_version, package_s3_key, package_sha256, status ENUM draft/pending_signature/signed/under_change_control/finalized, created_at, finalized_at). electronic_signatures (id UUID PK, submission_id FK, signer_id FK, role TEXT, meaning TEXT, totp_verified BOOL, signature_timestamp, certificate_thumbprint). audit_trail_events (id BIGINT PK GENERATED ALWAYS, event_type TEXT, actor_id UUID, resource_type TEXT, resource_id UUID, ip_address INET, user_agent TEXT, event_data JSONB, event_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, prev_hash TEXT, record_hash TEXT). study_protocols (id UUID, protocol_number, phase ENUM I/II/III/IV, indication, sponsor_id FK). investigator_accounts (id UUID, name_encrypted TEXT, email_encrypted TEXT, org_id FK, role TEXT, totp_secret_encrypted TEXT, created_at, last_login_at).
data.storage_design: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with KMS encryption for all tables. S3 bucket with Object Lock Compliance mode and 15-year retention for data package blobs. Redis 7 cluster for JWT blacklist and rate limiting counters.
data.retention_and_deletion: audit_trail_events: append-only, no delete permitted; S3 Object Lock prevents data package deletion for 15 years. Patient-identifiable fields encrypted with per-study KMS data keys; key material retained per HIPAA schedule.
data.data_risks: Aurora Multi-AZ failover causes 20-30 second connectivity interruption; submission service must implement retry-with-backoff on transient connection errors without resubmitting idempotent operations.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster with API service tasks (min 2, max 10) behind ALB with WAF. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ primary and read replica. Redis 7 ElastiCache Multi-AZ cluster. S3 Object Lock bucket in same region. CloudFront not used (API only). Route53 with health-check-based failover.
devops.environments: dev (single AZ, reduced resources), staging (Multi-AZ, production mirror for validation testing), production (Multi-AZ, change-control-gated deployments).
devops.ci_cd_design: Jenkins pipeline: Maven compile, unit tests (JUnit 5 + Mockito), integration tests (Testcontainers PostgreSQL and Redis), SpotBugs security scan, OWASP dependency check, Docker build, push ECR, ECS rolling deploy. Each production deployment generates OQ evidence artifact: test result XML, coverage report, and deployment manifest attached to the Jenkins build record.
devops.devops_risks: IQ/OQ/PQ documentation generation must be automated in CI or it becomes a bottleneck; invest in traceability matrix tooling in Phase 1 to avoid compliance debt.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a 21 CFR Part 11 compliant clinical trial data API using Java 21 Spring Boot on AWS. Key decisions: append-only audit table with SHA-256 hash chaining provides tamper evidence without blockchain complexity, S3 Object Lock Compliance mode prevents any deletion of submitted data packages, TOTP binding at both login and signature capture satisfies Part 11 unique identity requirements, and idempotency keys on submission endpoints prevent duplicate records during retry scenarios.
target_output.title: ClinTrialVault API Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Java 21 Spring Boot REST API service enabling pharmaceutical research organizations to submit, electronically sign, and audit clinical trial data packages under FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliance. Enforces TOTP MFA for all human users, cryptographically chains audit trail records to detect tampering, stores data packages in S3 with Object Lock for immutability, and provides a regulatory auditor query interface with tamper-evident log export capabilities.
target_output.architecture_overview: Stateless Spring Boot 3.2 API running on ECS Fargate behind an ALB with WAF, connected to Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ for transactional records and an S3 bucket with Object Lock Compliance mode for immutable data package blobs. Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster stores the JWT blacklist and per-user rate limit counters. Authentication is handled by a custom JWT service with TOTP MFA at login and at electronic signature capture; partner system integrations use API keys with mTLS. All submission, signature, retrieval, and change control events are written to an append-only audit_trail_events table with SHA-256 hash chaining where each record includes the hash of the previous record, enabling chain verification by regulatory auditors. The Aurora read replica serves the audit query and analytics API, isolating inspection query load from submission write traffic.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Java 21. Framework: Spring Boot 3.2, Spring Security 6, Spring Data JPA with Hibernate. API: REST with OpenAPI 3.1. Auth: Custom JWT (jjwt library), TOTP (Google Authenticator-compatible HMAC-SHA1), API key via bcrypt. Database: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with KMS. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7. Storage: S3 with Object Lock Compliance mode. Crypto: Java Cryptography Architecture with KMS-managed keys. Testing: JUnit 5, Mockito, Testcontainers. CI: Jenkins with Maven. Monitoring: Datadog APM, CloudWatch, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Submission API: POST /api/v1/submissions accepts multipart data package with eCTD metadata; validates package against versioned FDA eCTD schema; stores package blob to S3 Object Lock; creates submission record in DRAFT status. Electronic Signature: POST /api/v1/submissions/{id}/sign requires TOTP code and role-specific signature meaning; verifies TOTP against user secret; writes electronic_signatures record; advances submission to SIGNED status. Change Control: PATCH /api/v1/submissions/{id}/change-control initiates amendment workflow requiring data_manager role; prior version frozen, new draft created with lineage FK. Audit Trail: GET /api/v1/audit-trail with filters on date range, user_id, resource_type; GET /api/v1/audit-trail/export returns SHA-256-chained NDJSON export with chain verification report.
target_output.system_components: ALB with WAF (OWASP ruleset blocking injection and XSS). ECS Fargate API service tasks (min 2, max 10, autoscale on ALB request count). TrialSubmissionController (Spring MVC REST endpoints with @Valid request validation). ElectronicSignatureService (TOTP verification, signature record write, audit event emission in single transaction). AuditEventService (append-only audit_trail_events writer with SHA-256 hash chaining). AuditChainVerifier (on-demand chain integrity verification for regulatory inspection). eCTDSchemaValidator (validates submission XML/JSON against versioned FDA schemas embedded as classpath resources). S3ObjectLockWriter (boto-equivalent via AWS SDK Java v2 with PutObject specifying ObjectLockMode=COMPLIANCE). Redis JWT Blacklist (SET with TTL matching token expiry). Aurora Primary for writes, Read Replica for audit queries.
target_output.workflows: Data Package Submission and Signing: Investigator system POSTs to /api/v1/submissions with data package and study_protocol_id. eCTDSchemaValidator checks package structure against the specified eCTD schema version; validation errors returned as structured JSON with schema path references. On validation pass, S3ObjectLockWriter uploads package to S3 with 15-year Object Lock retention. Submission record created in DRAFT with package_sha256 checksum. Audit event written: submission_created. Investigator then POSTs to /api/v1/submissions/{id}/sign with their TOTP code and signature meaning (e.g., 'I attest this submission is accurate and complete'). TOTPVerifier checks current and adjacent time windows against stored HMAC-SHA1 secret. On TOTP pass, electronic_signatures record written and submission status advances to SIGNED. Chain audit event written with prev_hash of last record. Regulatory Audit Inspection: Regulatory auditor with auditor role calls GET /api/v1/audit-trail?resource_type=submission&from=2025-01-01&to=2025-12-31. AuditQueryService runs against read replica returning paginated event records. Auditor requests GET /api/v1/audit-trail/export to receive complete NDJSON chain export. AuditChainVerifier recomputes SHA-256 for each record and verifies prev_hash linkage; chain break reported in export header if detected.
target_output.data_model: trial_submissions (id UUID PK, study_protocol_id UUID FK, sponsor_org_id UUID FK, submitted_by UUID FK, submission_type TEXT ENUM initial/amendment/supplement, ectd_schema_version TEXT, package_s3_key TEXT, package_sha256 TEXT, status TEXT ENUM draft/pending_signature/signed/under_change_control/finalized, parent_submission_id UUID NULLABLE FK for amendments, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, finalized_at TIMESTAMPTZ). electronic_signatures (id UUID PK, submission_id UUID FK, signer_id UUID FK, role TEXT, meaning TEXT, totp_verified BOOL, signature_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, certificate_thumbprint TEXT). audit_trail_events (id BIGINT GENERATED ALWAYS, event_type TEXT, actor_id UUID, resource_type TEXT, resource_id UUID, ip_address INET, user_agent TEXT, event_data JSONB, event_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, prev_hash TEXT NOT NULL, record_hash TEXT NOT NULL). study_protocols (id UUID PK, protocol_number TEXT UNIQUE, phase TEXT ENUM I/II/III/IV, indication TEXT, sponsor_id UUID FK). investigator_accounts (id UUID PK, name_encrypted TEXT, email_encrypted TEXT, org_id UUID FK, role TEXT, totp_secret_encrypted TEXT, api_key_hash TEXT NULLABLE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, last_login_at TIMESTAMPTZ). All PHI fields encrypted with per-study KMS data keys using AES-256-GCM. Clinical trial subject entities: enrolled_patients (id UUID PK, trial_id FK, site_id FK, subject_id_encrypted VARCHAR UNIQUE, enrollment_date DATE, treatment_arm VARCHAR, primary_diagnosis_icd10 VARCHAR, withdrawal_date DATE), clinical_encounters (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, visit_number INT, encounter_date DATE, investigator_id UUID, vitals_json JSONB, adverse_event_flags JSONB), prescriptions (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, encounter_id FK, investigational_product_code VARCHAR, dosage_mg NUMERIC, prescriber_id UUID, dispensed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM dispensed/returned/destroyed).
target_output.api_design: REST API at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 spec. POST /api/v1/submissions (multipart; Idempotency-Key header required; returns 201 with submission_id). GET /api/v1/submissions/{id} (returns submission with signature status; requires investigator or data_manager role). POST /api/v1/submissions/{id}/sign (body: {totp_code, meaning}; requires investigator role; returns 200 with signature record). PATCH /api/v1/submissions/{id}/change-control (body: {change_reason}; requires data_manager role; returns 200 with new draft_id). GET /api/v1/audit-trail?from=ISO8601&to=ISO8601&actor_id=UUID&resource_type=TEXT&page=INT (paginated; requires auditor or admin role). GET /api/v1/audit-trail/export (returns NDJSON stream with chain verification report header; requires auditor role). All endpoints return application/json with problem+json for errors. Rate limiting: 100 req/min per API key via Redis counters.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 controls: unique TOTP-bound electronic identities for all signers, signature meaning captured verbatim per 11.50, audit trail records append-only with SHA-256 hash chaining per 11.10(e), system access records include operator ID and time per 11.10(d). HIPAA controls: PHI fields encrypted at field level with per-study KMS CMKs, BAA with AWS, minimum necessary access via RBAC. SOC 2: CloudTrail logging of all AWS API calls, Datadog SIEM integration, quarterly access reviews. Security controls: TLS 1.3 enforced at ALB, mTLS client certificates for partner system integrations, WAF OWASP ruleset, JWT 15-minute expiry with Redis blacklist for revocation, bcrypt-hashed API keys with 90-day forced rotation, OWASP dependency-check in Jenkins pipeline.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Jenkins CI pipeline: Maven compile, JUnit 5 unit tests (Mockito), integration tests (Testcontainers PostgreSQL 16 and Redis 7), SpotBugs static analysis, OWASP dependency-check CVE scan, Docker multi-stage build, ECR push. Each production deployment artifacts: OQ test result XML, coverage HTML report, deployment manifest, and OWASP dependency report attached to Jenkins build record as IQ/OQ validation evidence. ECS rolling update with minimum 50% healthy threshold. Environments: dev (single AZ, shared Aurora), staging (Multi-AZ mirror, used for PQ testing), production (Multi-AZ us-east-1a/1c, Aurora failover <30s, autoscaling 2-10 tasks on ALB request count). Change control: all production deployments require change request record with test evidence attachment and dual approval. Secrets Manager for TOTP encryption key, JWT signing key, and database credentials with 90-day rotation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM agent on each ECS task with distributed tracing across submission, signature, and audit event services. CloudWatch Logs structured JSON with correlation IDs propagated through Spring MDC. Custom Datadog metrics: submission_created_total, signature_captured_total, audit_chain_verification_failures, api_key_rotation_overdue_count. PagerDuty escalation for: API error rate >0.1% (tight SLA for regulatory system), p99 latency >3s, audit chain verification failure (critical), JWT blacklist Redis unreachable. Quarterly audit trail chain integrity scan run as scheduled ECS task producing report stored in S3.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2,800/month at 500 daily submissions. ECS autoscaling on ALB request count target 200 req/task. Aurora Multi-AZ primary plus read replica ($600/month at db.r6g.large). S3 Object Lock storage grows at $0.023/GB/month with 15-year retention; estimate $400/month by year 3 at typical trial data volume. Redis ElastiCache cache.r6g.large Multi-AZ $180/month. Cost management: ECS Fargate Spot not applicable due to regulatory HA requirements; reserved Aurora instances for 30% savings after SOC 2 certification.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 IQ - Infrastructure Qualification (8 weeks): AWS infrastructure provisioning (ECS, Aurora Multi-AZ, S3 Object Lock, Redis, WAF), Jenkins CI pipeline with Testcontainers integration tests, SpotBugs and OWASP scans, IQ documentation artifacts, Datadog APM integration. Phase 2 OQ - Operational Qualification (10 weeks): Submission API with eCTD schema validation, electronic signature with TOTP, audit trail with SHA-256 hash chaining, RBAC with all four roles, JWT with Redis blacklist, API key management, OQ test execution and evidence artifacts, OpenAPI 3.1 spec. Phase 3 PQ - Performance Qualification (4 weeks): Change control workflow, audit trail export with chain verification, load testing to confirm SLA at 5x expected submission volume, PQ evidence artifacts, SOC 2 readiness assessment.
target_output.development_guidelines: Java 21 with records and sealed classes for domain types. SpotBugs with Find Security Bugs plugin in CI; zero high-severity findings policy. JUnit 5 with Testcontainers for integration tests; no H2 in-memory database (must test against real PostgreSQL). 85% line coverage gate. All electronic signature and audit trail logic in dedicated service classes with no framework coupling for testability. IQ/OQ/PQ traceability matrix: each test class annotated with @TestRequirement linking to the requirement ID it validates.
