previous_plan.title: HTS Screening Data Platform Architecture v1
previous_plan.executive_summary: FastAPI backend with ClickHouse for activity measurement OLAP and PostgreSQL for compound metadata, React 18 frontend with plate heatmaps, Celery batch normalization jobs, Okta SSO authentication, deployed on ECS Fargate in internal VPC.
previous_plan.data_model: ClickHouse activity_measurements table with compound_id, target_protein_id, assay_id, percent_inhibition columns. PostgreSQL tables for compounds, screening_plates, assay_protocols. Single ClickHouse node with no replication specified.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Okta OIDC with PKCE, TLS 1.3, Redis caching for query results. No user-scoped cache keys. No export audit logging.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate for API and Celery. Single ClickHouse EC2 node. Aurora PostgreSQL Multi-AZ. Internal ALB.

target_output.thinking_summary: This is a round 2 revision addressing two unresolved issues: Redis cache cross-user data leakage and missing export audit trail. Fixing ISS-002 requires user_id-scoped cache keys and cache invalidation on role changes. Fixing ISS-003 requires an export_audit table with DLP-relevant fields and audit-gated export API endpoints. Both fixes are targeted; the rest of the architecture from round 1 is sound and should be preserved, particularly the ClickHouse MergeTree schema and Celery batch job design.

fix_report.action_taken: All Redis cache keys now include a user_id component using the pattern 'query:{user_id}:{query_hash}'. Cache entries for a user are invalidated on role change by deleting all keys matching 'query:{user_id}:*'. ActivityQueryService updated to construct user-scoped keys. Redis cache TTL reduced to 30 minutes to limit exposure window for any stale access patterns.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Scientist A cannot read cached query results from Scientist B's compound activity queries; role downgrade immediately invalidates all cached results for the affected user.

fix_report.action_taken: Added export_audit table to PostgreSQL schema with user_id, export_type, filter_criteria_json, record_count, ip_address, and initiated_at fields. DataExportService writes an export_audit record before generating the S3 export file. Auditors can query GET /api/v1/admin/export-audit with date and user filters. Export endpoints now require lead or admin role to prevent bulk scientist exfiltration.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Every compound data export is logged with identity, scope, and record count, satisfying the DLP compliance requirement from compliance_context. Export volume anomaly detection possible via audit table queries.

target_output.title: HTS Screening Data Platform Architecture v2
target_output.executive_summary: A high-throughput screening data management platform for drug discovery scientists ingesting plate reader assay results into a ClickHouse column-store for sub-second OLAP queries across hundreds of millions of compound-target activity measurements. React 18 dashboard provides plate heatmaps, compound browser, and hit list visualization. This revision resolves two round 1 security and compliance issues: Redis cache keys are now user-scoped to prevent cross-scientist compound data leakage, and all CSV/SDF exports are logged to an export_audit table for DLP compliance.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture with React 18 SPA behind an internal ALB, FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate, and a dual-database layer: ClickHouse 2-shard 2-replica cluster for activity measurement OLAP and Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ for compound metadata and application tables. Redis 7 ElastiCache provides query result caching with user-scoped keys (pattern 'query:{user_id}:{query_hash}') to prevent cross-session compound data leakage. Celery workers handle overnight plate normalization and Z-prime calculation. All services run in private VPC subnets with no public IP; access via corporate VPN through internal ALB. Okta OIDC authenticates all users with RBAC enforced at FastAPI endpoint level. Export operations write to export_audit table before generating S3 signed URLs, providing a complete DLP audit trail.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, React Query, Zustand, Recharts, canvas plate heatmap renderer. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, asynch ClickHouse driver, SQLAlchemy 2.0 for PostgreSQL, Celery with Redis broker. Databases: ClickHouse 24.x ReplicatedMergeTree 2-shard 2-replica on EC2 r6i.2xlarge; Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ; Redis 7 ElastiCache. Auth: Okta OIDC with PKCE. Infra: ECS Fargate, EC2 for ClickHouse nodes, internal ALB, S3 for exports. Monitoring: Prometheus, Grafana, Slack webhooks. CI: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Plate Ingest API: POST /ingest/plates accepts batch plate reader measurement arrays; validates plate barcode against registered assay_protocols; inserts activity_measurements to ClickHouse in 1000-row batches. QC Dashboard: per-plate Z-prime score badge, signal-to-background ratio, and pass/fail status. Hit List View: configurable percent-inhibition threshold per assay; sortable compound hit table with activity sparklines. Compound Browser: full-text search over compound_name and CAS_number; structure image thumbnails from SMILES. Data Export: CSV and SDF export of hit lists logged to export_audit before S3 URL generation; lead/admin role required.
target_output.system_components: Internal ALB (HTTPS only, VPN CIDR allowlist). ECS API tasks (FastAPI, autoscale 2-10 on CPU 60%). ECS Celery worker tasks (autoscale 1-5 on queue depth). ClickHouse cluster (2-shard, 2-replica ReplicatedMergeTree on EC2 r6i.2xlarge, ClickHouse Keeper for coordination). Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (compound metadata, user tables, export_audit). Redis 7 ElastiCache Multi-AZ (user-scoped query cache, Celery task queue). S3 bucket for exported CSV/SDF files (90-day lifecycle to Glacier). ActivityQueryService (user-scoped cache key construction, ClickHouse async query execution). DataExportService (export_audit write, S3 presigned URL generation). Prometheus sidecar and Grafana server.
target_output.workflows: Plate Reader Data Ingest: Plate reader instrument POSTs to POST /ingest/plates with plate_barcode, assay_id, and array of well measurements (well_position, raw_signal). Ingest service validates plate_barcode against screening_plates table. Raw measurements normalized against positive and negative control wells to compute percent_inhibition per well. ClickHouse batch INSERT of activity_measurements rows in 1000-row batches. Celery NormalizationJob queued for Z-prime calculation. Scientist Hit Query with User-Scoped Cache: Scientist queries GET /screens/{id}/hits?threshold=50. ActivityQueryService constructs Redis cache key 'query:{scientist_user_id}:{sha256(screen_id+threshold)}'. Cache hit returns immediately; miss executes ClickHouse query with compound_id aggregation. Result stored in Redis under user-scoped key with 30-minute TTL. Scientist never reads a cache entry from another user. Data Export with DLP Audit: Lead or admin triggers POST /exports with filter criteria. DataExportService writes export_audit record with user_id, export_type=csv, filter_criteria_json, record_count, ip_address before any export generation. Celery HitExporter job generates CSV, writes to S3, returns presigned download URL. Admin queries GET /admin/export-audit for DLP review.
target_output.data_model: ClickHouse: activity_measurements (compound_id UInt64, target_protein_id UInt32, assay_id UInt32, plate_id UInt32, well_position String, raw_signal Float64, percent_inhibition Float64, is_hit UInt8, z_prime Float32, measured_at DateTime) ENGINE=ReplicatedMergeTree ORDER BY (compound_id, assay_id, measured_at). Materialized view hit_counts_by_assay pre-aggregates hit counts per assay_id for dashboard. PostgreSQL: compounds (id BIGINT PK, compound_name TEXT, cas_number TEXT, smiles TEXT, mol_weight REAL, library_id FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ), screening_plates (id UUID PK, assay_id FK, plate_barcode TEXT UNIQUE, instrument_id FK, run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, z_prime REAL, signal_to_background REAL, status TEXT), assay_protocols (id UUID, assay_name TEXT, target_protein_id FK, hit_threshold_pct REAL, positive_control TEXT, negative_control TEXT), target_proteins (id UUID, gene_symbol TEXT, uniprot_id TEXT, therapeutic_area TEXT), export_audit (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, export_type TEXT, filter_criteria_json JSONB, record_count INT, ip_address INET, initiated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Redis key pattern: 'query:{user_id}:{query_hash}' with 30-minute TTL.
target_output.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /ingest/plates (batch plate ingest; returns 202 with job_id). GET /assays/{id}/qc (plate QC metrics; scientist+). GET /compounds/{id}/activity (compound activity across all assays; scientist+). GET /screens/{id}/hits?threshold=FLOAT&page=INT (hit list with pagination; scientist+). POST /exports (initiate CSV/SDF export; lead or admin role required; returns export_job_id). GET /exports/{id}/status (job status). GET /exports/{id}/download (returns S3 presigned URL when complete). GET /admin/export-audit?from=ISO&to=ISO&user_id=UUID (DLP audit log; admin role). All endpoints require Okta JWT. Rate limit: 200 req/min per user via Redis counters. ClickHouse queries bounded to 20 concurrent via semaphore.
target_output.security_and_compliance: ISS-002 fix: All Redis query cache keys include user_id prefix in pattern 'query:{user_id}:{sha256(query_params)}'. On user role change, FastAPI auth middleware deletes all 'query:{user_id}:*' keys via SCAN+DEL. Cache TTL reduced to 30 minutes. ISS-003 fix: export_audit table records every export event with user_id, filter_criteria_json, record_count, and ip_address before export file generation; GET /admin/export-audit provides DLP review interface. Additional controls: Okta OIDC with MFA enforced at corporate Okta policy level; TLS 1.3 on internal ALB; all EBS and S3 encrypted at rest; internal ALB restricted to VPN CIDR; API rate limiting per user via Redis; ClickHouse query log retention 1 year; Prometheus alert for export volume anomaly (>10K records in single export).
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate private subnets: API service tasks (min 2, max 10, CPU 60% autoscale), Celery worker tasks (min 1, max 5, queue depth autoscale). ClickHouse cluster: 2-shard 2-replica ReplicatedMergeTree on EC2 r6i.2xlarge instances in private subnets; ClickHouse Keeper ensemble on 3 separate t3.medium instances for coordination; 500GB gp3 EBS per node. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ (db.r6g.large primary + read replica for export audit queries). Redis 7 ElastiCache Multi-AZ cluster (cache.r6g.large). Internal ALB with HTTPS listener; security group allows only VPN CIDR. GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy, pytest with testcontainers ClickHouse 24.x and PostgreSQL 16 fixtures, 85% coverage gate, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Feature flags via LaunchDarkly for progressive scientist group rollout.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics scraped from FastAPI via prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator: ingest_plates_total, query_latency_seconds histogram (p50/p95/p99), cache_hit_ratio (user-scoped), batch_job_duration_seconds, export_record_count histogram. Grafana dashboards: real-time ingest rate, ClickHouse query latency heatmap, hit rate trend per active campaign, export audit volume. Slack webhook alerts: Celery job failure (immediate), ingest error rate >5% (5-minute window), ClickHouse p95 query latency >1s (breaches sub-second SLA), export anomaly (single export >10K records). Structured JSON logs from FastAPI with correlation ID and user_id for security audit trail.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $3,600/month at 200 active scientists and 50 plate ingest batches/day. ClickHouse EC2 r6i.2xlarge x4 (2-shard 2-replica) $1,200/month reserved. Aurora db.r6g.large Multi-AZ $350/month. Redis cache.r6g.large $180/month. ECS Fargate API and Celery $400/month. S3 for exports $50/month. Remaining budget headroom $400/month for data transfer and CloudWatch. ClickHouse scales vertically to r6i.4xlarge before horizontal shard expansion needed.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Plate ingest API, ClickHouse activity_measurements schema with ReplicatedMergeTree topology (ISS-001 fix applied), Z-prime calculator, hit threshold service, compound browser, React plate heatmap, user-scoped Redis cache (ISS-002 fix), export_audit table and export API (ISS-003 fix), Okta OIDC, GitHub Actions CI, Grafana/Prometheus. Phase 2 Analytics (6 weeks): Dose-response curve fitting with Hill equation, automated hit prioritization scoring, multi-assay cross-screen comparison view, ClickHouse materialized view optimization for dashboard queries. Phase 3 Integrations (4 weeks): Compound procurement system integration for automated library registration, HL7 FHIR-style compound export format, LaunchDarkly feature flag rollout to all scientist groups.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode; ruff for linting. All ClickHouse queries validated against a test ClickHouse instance via testcontainers in CI; no query string concatenation (use parameterized ClickHouse driver queries). React TypeScript strict mode; React Query for all server state; no direct fetch calls in components. 85% test coverage gate. Redis cache key construction centralized in CacheKeyService class to enforce user-scoping policy. Export endpoint integration tests verify export_audit record is written before S3 URL is returned.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ClickHouse Keeper ensemble (3 nodes) adds operational overhead; mitigated by co-locating on small EC2 instances rather than dedicated hosts. Risk: ClickHouse on EC2 requires manual OS patching and cluster upgrade procedures with no managed service safety net; allocate dedicated DevOps capacity for quarterly maintenance windows. Tradeoff: user-scoped Redis cache keys reduce cache hit ratio compared to global keys (estimated 40% vs 70% hit rate); this is an accepted tradeoff to resolve ISS-002 confidential data leakage. Tradeoff: requiring lead/admin role for exports reduces scientist self-service convenience but is required for DLP compliance given confidential compound IP sensitivity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-002 resolved: user_id-scoped Redis cache keys with role-change invalidation. ISS-003 resolved: export_audit table with DLP-relevant fields and admin query endpoint. ClickHouse Keeper confirmed over ZooKeeper for native ClickHouse coordination without additional ZooKeeper cluster. Export role restriction (lead/admin only) confirmed with security team as DLP control balancing scientist workflow against IP protection.

metadata.notes: HTS drug discovery platform round 2 revision resolving Redis cache leakage and export audit trail gaps.

project_goal.value: JavaScript/TypeScript SDK for scientific instrument vendors to abstract hardware communication protocols into a unified async API for cloud lab platforms
project_goal.rationale: Core SDK mission to reduce custom integration development effort across vendor ecosystem

target_users.value: Instrument integration developers at scientific instrument vendors and lab software teams consuming the SDK
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are developers building vendor drivers and platform integrators

project_class.rationale: Distributed as an npm package with public API surface, not a hosted service

capabilities.rationale: Library provides data transformation, protocol abstraction, and hardware integration capabilities

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-protocol abstraction and async event streaming add meaningful complexity

risk_level.rationale: Internal lab network usage, no sensitive data transmitted, no auth surface

data_sensitivity.rationale: Measurement data stays within lab infrastructure and consumer applications

external_exposure.rationale: SDK operates on local instrument connections; no public network endpoints

access_model.value: No auth required; SDK is a library embedded in consumer processes
access_model.rationale: Library pattern does not expose network endpoints requiring authentication

feature_scope.value: Typed instrument driver classes, serial/USB/Ethernet protocol adapters, async command queuing, event stream subscriptions, normalized measurement response objects
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope for unified instrument communication abstraction

mvp_scope.value: Serial and USB protocol adapters, 3 reference driver implementations (pipetting robot, spectrophotometer, plate reader), typed command/response API
mvp_scope.rationale: Sufficient for initial vendor adopters to validate SDK contract

security_baseline.value: Input validation on all protocol frames, no credential storage, safe serial port handling with timeouts
security_baseline.rationale: Prevent buffer overruns and hung connections on faulty instrument firmware

frontend_stack.rationale: SDK is a Node.js package, no browser frontend

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with TypeScript 5.4, serialport, noble-winrt for BLE optional
backend_stack.rationale: Node.js serialport ecosystem is most mature for lab instrument USB/serial connections

data_platform.value: No persistent database; measurement data returned to caller, optional in-memory ring buffer for streaming instruments
data_platform.rationale: Library does not own storage; consumers choose their persistence strategy

hosting_target.value: npm package registry (npmjs.com and optional private GitHub Packages mirror)
hosting_target.rationale: Standard distribution channel for Node.js libraries

privacy_retention_policy.value: SDK emits no telemetry; measurement data lifecycle owned by consuming application
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Library pattern transfers data ownership entirely to consumer

future_scope.value: GPIB and MODBUS protocol adapters, OPC-UA server bridge, WebSocket streaming adapter for browser lab dashboards
future_scope.rationale: Roadmap expansion to cover legacy industrial protocols common in older lab equipment

constraints.value: Must support Node.js LTS (20 and 22), zero production dependencies beyond serialport, bundle size under 200 KB
constraints.rationale: Consumers impose strict dependency auditing; large transitive trees are rejected

observability_baseline.value: Structured debug logging via pino with configurable log level; consumers route SDK log output to their own sinks
observability_baseline.rationale: SDK must not prescribe a logging framework but must emit useful debug events

execution_preference.value: Trunk-based development, semver releases via GitHub Actions, changelog maintained in CHANGELOG.md
execution_preference.rationale: SDK consumers need predictable versioning for upgrade planning

project.goal: Unified instrument driver SDK abstracting serial/USB/Ethernet protocols
project.target_users: Instrument integration developers and lab platform teams

backend.api: Exported typed driver classes and EventEmitter/AsyncIterator streams

security.auth: None - library embedded in consumer process
security.transport: Serial port timeouts and frame validation

data.db: None - in-memory ring buffer for streaming

reasoner_reviews.product: SDK scope is appropriately bounded for v1.0. Three reference driver implementations provide enough coverage to validate the abstraction contract. The ring buffer for streaming instruments is a pragmatic choice that avoids forcing consumers into a specific persistence layer.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The EventEmitter-based streaming API is idiomatic Node.js but risks consumer friction as modern TypeScript code uses async/await patterns. Adding AsyncIterableIterator as a first-class export via InstrumentDriver.stream() eliminates this friction. Driver class generics over TCommand/TResponse ensure type safety across all concrete driver implementations.
reasoner_reviews.security: Serial port access on most OS requires elevated privileges or udev rules; SDK documentation must clearly state OS-level prerequisites. Frame validation prevents malformed instrument responses from causing buffer overruns. No network exposure means attack surface is minimal.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Zero-production-dependency constraint is achievable with serialport as the only exception. Bundle size under 200 KB is feasible if GPIB/MODBUS adapters are deferred to future scope as optional peer dependencies.

backend.service_design: Three-layer package: InstrumentDriver<TCmd,TResp> abstract base class defining command/response contract and owning internal worker_threads pool; ProtocolAdapter layer with SerialAdapter, UsbAdapter, EthernetAdapter; concrete vendor driver classes extending InstrumentDriver per device family
backend.api_patterns: Named TypeScript class exports. InstrumentDriver.sendCommand(cmd: TCmd, opts?: {timeoutMs: number}): Promise<TResp>. InstrumentDriver.stream(): AsyncIterableIterator<Measurement>. InstrumentDriver.on('measurement'|'disconnected'|'error', handler). connect(): Promise<void>. disconnect(): Promise<void>.
backend.failure_handling: Command timeout (configurable, default 5s) rejects pending Promise with InstrumentTimeoutError including commandId and elapsedMs. Malformed response frames throw ProtocolParseError with rawFrame hex. Serial port disconnect emits disconnected event and rejects all queued command promises.
backend.scaling_notes: Internal WorkerFrameParser in worker_threads decodes high-frequency frames off the main event loop. Pool defaults to min(2, OS CPU count - 1). Ring buffer capped at 10,000 Measurement objects per driver instance; consumer-configurable via ringBufferSize option.

security.auth_design: No auth layer; SDK embeds in consumer process inheriting its privilege context
security.abuse_prevention: Input validation rejects command payloads exceeding instrument-defined max frame sizes. Serial port open calls wrapped with OS-level timeout to prevent hang on unresponsive instruments.
security.secrets_management: No secrets stored; connection credentials passed by consumer at driver instantiation, never persisted by SDK

data.entities: instrument_drivers (class instances: driverId uuid, vendorId, modelId, firmwareVersion, connectionState ENUM disconnected/connecting/connected/error, lastSeenAt). measurement_responses (typed interface: instrumentId, channelId, value number, unit ENUM AU/nm/RFU/degC, timestamp ISO8601, rawFrame Buffer optional). command_queues (per-driver FIFO: commandId, payloadBuffer, enqueuedAt, timeoutMs, resolve fn, reject fn). device_configs (consumer object: portPath, baudRate, dataBits, stopBits, parity, connectTimeoutMs).
data.storage_design: No persistent storage; ring buffer is circular array of Measurement objects capped per driver instance
data.retention_and_deletion: Ring buffer evicts oldest measurement on capacity; consumers own all persistence

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on PR: tsc --noEmit, eslint no-any rule, vitest unit suite with mocked adapters, vitest integration suite using socat virtual serial port pairs on Linux runner. Coverage gate 85%. On tag v*: npm publish to npmjs.com and packages.github.com, dual CJS/ESM esbuild bundle verification under 200 KB.
devops.environments: No deployed environments; local dev uses socat virtual serial port pairs for integration testing without physical instruments
devops.rollback_strategy: Semver allows consumers to pin to prior minor; npm dist-tag rollback via npm dist-tag add pkg@prev latest

ISS-001.title: AsyncIterator API not exported alongside EventEmitter API
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described only EventEmitter-based measurement streaming. Modern TypeScript consumers using for-await-of patterns must manually wrap EventEmitter, adding boilerplate and risking listener leak.

history.note: Identified during architect_reasoner review of streaming API design

ISS-002.title: worker_threads pool ownership not specified in API contract
ISS-002.detail: Scaling notes mention offloading frame parsing to worker_threads but do not state whether consumers must provide a thread pool or SDK manages it internally, creating integration ambiguity.

focus_issues.title: AsyncIterator API not exported alongside EventEmitter API
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan described only EventEmitter-based measurement streaming. Modern TypeScript consumers must manually wrap EventEmitter.

latest_recommendations: Export AsyncIterableIterator from InstrumentDriver.stream() alongside existing EventEmitter interface
Document worker_threads lifecycle - clarify SDK-managed vs consumer-provided thread pools
revision_memory.latest_plan_title: Scientific Instrument Integration SDK v1.0 Architecture

previous_audits.summary: Solid library architecture with clean driver abstraction, but EventEmitter-only streaming API reduces adoption readiness for modern async/await TypeScript consumers.
strengths: Clean three-layer separation of InstrumentDriver, ProtocolAdapter, and concrete vendor drivers
Zero-dependency constraint properly enforced in design
Semver release pipeline well-specified with conventional commits
concerns: EventEmitter-only API excludes for-await-of consumers without manual wrapping
worker_threads ownership ambiguity creates consumer integration uncertainty
blocking_issues: AsyncIterator omission forces manual EventEmitter wrapping in all modern TypeScript consumer code
recommendations: Add InstrumentDriver.stream(): AsyncIterableIterator<Measurement> as first-class export
Clarify in api_design that SDK manages its own worker pool internally

issue_updates.title: AsyncIterator API not exported
issue_updates.detail: API design section only documented EventEmitter pattern; AsyncIterator missing from exported type surface

previous_plan.title: Scientific Instrument Integration SDK v1.0 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Node.js TypeScript SDK providing typed instrument driver classes and protocol adapters for serial, USB, and Ethernet connected lab instruments, enabling vendor integration teams to expose hardware via a uniform async API consumed by cloud lab management platforms.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Three-layer package: InstrumentDriver abstract base, ProtocolAdapter transport layer, and concrete vendor driver classes. EventEmitter streams for continuous measurement instruments. All framing and timeout logic isolated in adapters.
previous_plan.api_design: InstrumentDriver.sendCommand(cmd: Command): Promise<Response>. InstrumentDriver.on('measurement', handler). connect(): Promise<void>. disconnect(): Promise<void>. Typed errors: InstrumentTimeoutError, ProtocolParseError, ConnectionError.
previous_plan.data_model: instrument_drivers class instances with connectionState ENUM. measurement_responses typed objects with channelId, value, unit, timestamp. command_queues per-driver FIFO with timeout tracking. device_configs plain objects at instantiation.

target_output.thinking_summary: Revising SDK to add AsyncIterableIterator as first-class export alongside EventEmitter to resolve ISS-001. Worker_threads pool is SDK-managed internally with no consumer configuration required. Both streaming modes documented with TypeScript usage examples. library_sdk class means no frontend specialist, no deployed infrastructure beyond npm registry.

fix_report.action_taken: Added InstrumentDriver.stream() returning AsyncIterableIterator<Measurement> implemented via internal PassThrough stream bridge. Both EventEmitter and AsyncIterator patterns documented in api_design with TypeScript examples. worker_threads pool explicitly documented as SDK-managed in development_guidelines.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Modern TypeScript consumers can use for-await-of over instrument measurement streams without manual EventEmitter wrapping; ISS-002 also addressed by documenting SDK-managed worker pool.

target_output.title: Scientific Instrument Integration SDK v1.0 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A zero-dependency Node.js TypeScript SDK providing typed instrument driver classes with dual-mode streaming (EventEmitter and AsyncIterableIterator) across serial, USB, and Ethernet protocol adapters. Enables instrument vendor teams to expose hardware APIs uniformly to cloud lab management platforms without per-vendor custom integration code.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-layer package: InstrumentDriver<TCmd,TResp> abstract base class owns an internal worker_threads pool for CPU-intensive frame parsing and provides both EventEmitter 'measurement' events and AsyncIterableIterator via stream(). ProtocolAdapter layer implements SerialAdapter wrapping serialport, UsbAdapter for libusb HID transfers, and EthernetAdapter with TCP reconnect backoff. Concrete vendor driver classes extend InstrumentDriver per device family, implementing command encoding and response parsing specific to that instrument. Published as dual CJS/ESM build under 200 KB to npm and GitHub Packages.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Node.js 20 and 22 LTS. Language: TypeScript 5.4 strict mode. Protocol: serialport 12.x (sole production dependency). Threading: Node.js built-in worker_threads for frame decode. Streaming: Node.js Readable objectMode bridged to AsyncIterableIterator. Build: tsc for .d.ts declarations, esbuild for CJS/ESM dual bundle. Testing: vitest with socat virtual serial port pairs for integration coverage. Publish: GitHub Actions to npmjs.com and packages.github.com.
target_output.functional_feature_map: InstrumentDriver base: sendCommand() with configurable timeout, connect/disconnect lifecycle, command queue FIFO, dual-stream bridge. ProtocolAdapters: SerialAdapter (baud/parity/stop-bit config), UsbAdapter (HID endpoint selection), EthernetAdapter (TCP nagle-disabled with reconnect). Streaming: EventEmitter 'measurement' for legacy consumers; stream(): AsyncIterableIterator<Measurement> for async/await consumers. Reference drivers: SpectrophotometerDriver (absorbance scan), PipettingRobotDriver (aspirate/dispense sequences), PlateReaderDriver (well-plate fluorescence reads).
target_output.system_components: InstrumentDriver<TCmd,TResp> (exported abstract class): command queue, timeout manager, SDK-managed worker pool reference, MeasurementBridge for dual-stream emission. SerialAdapter/UsbAdapter/EthernetAdapter (internal): protocol framing, byte buffer management, OS port lifecycle. WorkerFrameParser (internal worker_threads worker): CPU-intensive protocol decode for high-frequency instruments. MeasurementBridge (internal): routes WorkerFrameParser postMessage to both EventEmitter events and AsyncIterator yields. Error classes (exported): InstrumentTimeoutError, ProtocolParseError, ConnectionError.
target_output.workflows: Driver instantiation and connect: consumer calls new SpectrophotometerDriver({portPath: '/dev/ttyUSB0', baudRate: 9600}), then await driver.connect(). SDK opens serial port, sends instrument identification command, validates firmware response bytes, resolves connection. On-demand command: consumer awaits driver.sendCommand({type: 'ABSORBANCE_SCAN', wavelengthRange: [400,700], stepNm: 2}); SDK frames command per instrument protocol, enqueues in FIFO, sends via SerialAdapter, awaits response within 5s timeout, decodes via WorkerFrameParser, resolves Promise<AbsorbanceScanResult>. Continuous streaming (AsyncIterator): consumer iterates with 'for await (const m of driver.stream())' to receive typed PlateReaderMeasurement objects; MeasurementBridge feeds WorkerFrameParser output into the AsyncIterator without any consumer EventEmitter setup.
target_output.data_model: instrument_drivers (class instances): driverId uuid, vendorId string, modelId string, firmwareVersion string, connectionState ENUM disconnected/connecting/connected/error, lastCommandAt Date, lastSeenAt Date. measurement_responses (exported interface Measurement): instrumentId string, channelId string, value number, unit ENUM AU/nm/RFU/degC, timestamp ISO8601, rawFrame Buffer optional. command_queues (per-driver internal): commandId uuid, payloadBuffer Buffer, enqueuedAt Date, sentAt Date optional, timeoutMs number, resolveCallback function, rejectCallback function. device_configs (consumer-provided plain object): portPath string, baudRate number, dataBits 5|6|7|8, stopBits 1|1.5|2, parity none|even|odd, connectTimeoutMs number, ringBufferSize number optional.
target_output.api_design: Named TypeScript exports. InstrumentDriver<TCmd,TResp>: connect(): Promise<void>, disconnect(): Promise<void>, sendCommand(cmd: TCmd, opts?: {timeoutMs: number}): Promise<TResp>, stream(): AsyncIterableIterator<Measurement>, on('measurement', (m: Measurement) => void): this, on('disconnected', () => void): this, on('error', (e: Error) => void): this. Concrete exports: SpectrophotometerDriver.sendCommand(AbsorbanceScanCommand): Promise<AbsorbanceScanResult>. PlateReaderDriver.stream(): AsyncIterableIterator<WellMeasurement>. Error exports: InstrumentTimeoutError (commandId, elapsedMs), ProtocolParseError (rawFrame hex), ConnectionError (portPath, osError).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Serial port file descriptor access requires OS-level udev rules or Windows device manager permissions documented as consumer prerequisites in SDK README. All command payloads validated against instrument-defined max frame size before transmission to prevent firmware buffer overrun. Configurable command timeout (default 5000 ms) closes pending operation with InstrumentTimeoutError to prevent indefinite hangs on unresponsive instruments. No network sockets opened by SDK; no credentials stored. WorkerFrameParser workers run with no additional OS privileges beyond parent process. Input sanitization applied to portPath to prevent path traversal before serialport open.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI on every PR: tsc --noEmit strict type check, eslint with no-any and explicit-module-boundary-types rules enforced, vitest unit suite with mocked ProtocolAdapters, vitest integration suite on Linux runner using socat-paired virtual serial ports for end-to-end frame parse validation. Coverage gate 85%. Release workflow on git tag v*: full test suite runs, then parallel npm publish --access public to npmjs.com and packages.github.com. Dual build: tsc emits .d.ts type declarations; esbuild bundles CJS (dist/index.cjs) and ESM (dist/index.mjs) targets; bundle size assertion fails CI if combined output exceeds 200 KB. Changelog auto-generated from conventional commits via standard-version.
target_output.observability: SDK emits structured pino log entries at debug level for command enqueue, serial write, frame parse, measurement emit, and port reconnect events. Log level configurable by consumer via driver constructor options.logLevel. Key debug events: 'cmd.enqueued' (commandId, queueDepth), 'frame.parsed' (instrumentId, parseLatencyMs), 'measurement.emitted' (channelId, value), 'port.reconnect' (attempt, backoffMs). Consumers route pino output to their own sink. No external telemetry emitted by SDK.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud infrastructure cost; SDK runs in consumer process. npmjs.com hosting is free for public packages. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI for open-source repository. Worker thread pool defaults to min(2, OS CPU count - 1) to avoid starving consumer event loop. Ring buffer defaults to 10,000 measurements per driver; tunable via device_config.ringBufferSize. Ethernet adapter uses nagle-disabled TCP to minimize latency for command-response instruments.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - v1.0 MVP (6 weeks): SerialAdapter and UsbAdapter, SpectrophotometerDriver and PipettingRobotDriver reference implementations, full typed API including AsyncIterableIterator stream(), 85% test coverage with socat integration tests, dual CJS/ESM publish pipeline. Phase 2 - v1.1 (4 weeks): PlateReaderDriver, EthernetAdapter with TCP reconnect backoff, WorkerFrameParser performance benchmarking at 1000 Hz sustained, migration guide for EventEmitter to AsyncIterator upgrade path. Phase 3 - v2.0 (roadmap): GPIB adapter via linux-gpib as optional peer dependency, MODBUS-RTU adapter, OPC-UA bridge as separate @lab-sdk/opcua scoped package.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode enforced; no-any ESLint rule enabled across all source. All exported symbols carry JSDoc with parameter types and for-await-of usage examples. Protocol adapters tested with real socat virtual port pairs, not mocks, to catch framing edge cases. Conventional commit messages enforced via commitlint. Breaking changes require semver major and CHANGELOG migration section. WorkerFrameParser module must not import from SDK public API to prevent circular require. SDK-managed worker pool is internal; consumers must not reference worker internals.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: serialport native addon compilation fails on consumer CI lacking build tools. Mitigation: ship prebuilt binaries for Node 20/22 on linux-x64, darwin-arm64, win32-x64 via serialport prebuild-install. Risk: worker_threads unavailable in some bundler environments that strip workers. Mitigation: WorkerFrameParser degrades gracefully to synchronous parsing with console.warn on spawn failure. Tradeoff: dual CJS/ESM esbuild step adds build complexity but eliminates ESM/CJS interop issues in mixed consumer environments. Tradeoff: SDK managing its own worker pool adds 2-3 MB resident memory per process but removes consumer threading burden.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: AsyncIterator vs EventEmitter: both exported; stream() is preferred for new consumer code, EventEmitter retained for backward compatibility. Worker pool ownership: SDK manages internally; consumers do not configure or provide thread pools - resolves ISS-002. GPIB and MODBUS: deferred to v2.0 as optional peer dependencies to keep v1.0 zero-dependency. EthernetAdapter: included in v1.1 after serial/USB validates abstraction contract.

metadata.notes: Round 2 SDK revision adding AsyncIterableIterator streaming API alongside EventEmitter to resolve adoption gap for modern TypeScript consumers.

project_goal.value: Automated data ingestion and harmonization tool for multi-site epidemiological studies, collecting survey responses, biomarker measurements, and environmental sensor readings from disparate source systems into a unified analytical dataset
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual per-site ETL scripts with a governed pipeline supporting study replication and audit

target_users.value: Study coordinators, epidemiologists, and biostatisticians managing multi-site longitudinal cohort studies
target_users.rationale: Primary operators are study staff managing data flows from field sites

project_class.rationale: Orchestrated pipeline tool with no end-user UI; operates as scheduled batch automation

capabilities.rationale: Requires pipeline orchestration, multi-source integration, data transformation, and scheduled batch processing

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-site source heterogeneity, PHI handling, IRB compliance, and quality validation logic make this advanced

risk_level.rationale: Processes participant health data across multiple institutions with HIPAA, GDPR, and IRB obligations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Ingests participant biomarker results, survey responses with clinical data, and environmental exposure readings linked to study participants

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline operates within the study consortium internal network; no public API surface

access_model.value: Service account tokens per source site; pipeline authenticates to each REDCap instance and sensor API via site-specific credentials stored in Secret Manager
access_model.rationale: Per-site credential isolation ensures breach at one site does not expose credentials for others

feature_scope.value: Per-site REDCap and sensor API connectors, field-mapping and unit-conversion harmonization rules, common data model (CDM) loading, automated quality checks (outlier detection, missing field validation, cross-variable logic), study coordinator review queue for flagged records
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope to replace manual per-site ETL and manual QC

mvp_scope.value: REDCap connector for 3 pilot sites, 50 harmonization rules for baseline survey fields, CDM load to analytical warehouse, outlier and missing-field quality flags
mvp_scope.rationale: Pilot with 3 sites validates pipeline design before expanding to full 12-site cohort

security_baseline.value: PHI encrypted at rest (Cloud SQL with CMEK), TLS 1.3 for all source API calls, per-site credential rotation every 90 days, all PHI access logged to immutable audit table
security_baseline.rationale: HIPAA minimum controls plus IRB data use agreement requirements

frontend_stack.value: None - automation_tool with no UI; coordinator review via exported reports only
frontend_stack.rationale: No browser frontend; study coordinators receive flagged-record reports via email or shared drive

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow 2.9 on GCP Cloud Composer 2
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow provides DAG-based orchestration with retry logic, lineage tracking, and GCP-native integrations

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL for harmonized CDM storage; BigQuery for analytical workloads; GCS for raw source file staging
data_platform.rationale: Cloud SQL for transactional CDM writes; BigQuery for biostatistician queries across millions of records

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Composer 2 (managed Airflow) in us-central1 region
hosting_target.rationale: Managed Airflow reduces ops burden; us-central1 satisfies data residency requirements

privacy_retention_policy.value: Participant-level data retained per IRB protocol (minimum 10 years post-study); de-identification required before any data sharing outside consortium; right-to-withdrawal triggers participant record deletion cascade
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: IRB data use agreements and GDPR Article 17 right-to-erasure obligations

future_scope.value: Genomic data integration (VCF files from sequencing centers), real-time streaming ingestion for wearable sensor data, self-service harmonization rule editor for study staff
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 expansion as cohort data sources grow

constraints.value: Data must not leave GCP us-central1; all processing on GCP; study coordinator review cycle must complete within 48 hours of nightly ingestion
constraints.rationale: IRB data residency requirements and study protocol SLA

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards on Prometheus metrics from Cloud Composer; per-DAG run audit log in Cloud Logging; email alerts for failed DAG runs and quality flag counts exceeding thresholds
observability_baseline.rationale: Study operations team must be notified of pipeline failures within 30 minutes

execution_preference.value: GitLab CI with two-approver merge for harmonization rule changes; nightly DAG runs at 02:00 UTC after all site data collection windows close
execution_preference.rationale: Harmonization rules are data-impacting changes requiring review; 02:00 UTC avoids site business hours

compliance_context.value: HIPAA Privacy and Security Rule, IRB data use agreements for all 12 study sites, GDPR for European sites (Frankfurt sub-processor), NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy, export controls on genomic data
compliance_context.rationale: Multi-jurisdiction health research data with US federal funding obligations

project.goal: Automated multi-site epidemiological data harmonization pipeline
project.target_users: Study coordinators and biostatisticians

backend.api: Internal DAG tasks and operators; no external API surface

security.auth: Per-site service account tokens in Secret Manager
security.transport: TLS 1.3 for all REDCap and sensor API calls

devops.ci: GitLab CI with two-approver merge for rule changes

reasoner_reviews.product: Scope is well-bounded for MVP with 3 pilot sites. REDCap is the dominant survey platform in academic epidemiology, making it the right integration priority. The 48-hour coordinator review SLA is realistic given the flag-based triage approach rather than manual inspection of all records.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Cloud Composer 2 is the appropriate orchestration choice for GCP-native deployments. The separation of Cloud SQL for transactional CDM and BigQuery for analytical queries is architecturally sound. However, the plan lacks specificity on the harmonization rule engine design - rules should be stored as versioned configuration, not hardcoded in Python, to allow study staff to update them without code deployments.
reasoner_reviews.security: HIPAA requires audit logging of every PHI access event including pipeline read operations. The plan mentions an immutable audit table but does not specify that the pipeline's service account must be logged when it reads from source APIs. Per-site credential isolation in Secret Manager is correct. GDPR right-to-withdrawal deletion cascade must be explicitly implemented as a tested DAG task.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Nightly 02:00 UTC schedule is consistent with site data collection windows. The 48-hour coordinator review cycle is achievable if flagged-record reports are generated and distributed automatically at pipeline completion. Region constraint to us-central1 is feasible; GDPR Frankfurt sub-processor requirement for European sites needs architectural clarification.

backend.service_design: Three DAG types: nightly IngestDAG per source site (REDCapIngestOperator or SensorAPIIngestOperator), daily HarmonizeDAG consuming staged raw files and applying field-mapping rules from harmonization_rules table, and weekly QualityReportDAG aggregating flag counts per site and participant cohort
backend.api_patterns: No external API surface. Internal operator pattern: BaseSourceOperator abstract class with execute() hook; REDCapOperator extends it with project-specific API pagination. HarmonizationEngine class applies FieldMappingRule and UnitConversionRule objects loaded from versioned DB config.
backend.background_jobs: WithdrawalProcessingDAG: triggered on coordinator request, deletes or de-identifies records for a withdrawn participant across CDM tables, writes deletion audit event, updates participant status to withdrawn_deleted.
backend.failure_handling: Airflow retry with exponential backoff (3 retries, 5/15/45 min delays) for transient source API failures. Dead-letter table captures records that fail 3 harmonization rule applications for manual review. Failed DAG runs trigger PagerDuty alert within 15 minutes.

security.auth_design: Per-site service account credentials stored in GCP Secret Manager; Cloud Composer Airflow connections reference secret versions. Credentials rotated every 90 days via automated Secret Manager rotation trigger.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: pipeline_audit_log table (append-only, no DELETE privilege to pipeline service account): event_type ENUM source_api_read/cdm_write/harmonize_apply/qc_flag/withdrawal_delete, participant_id, source_site_id, dag_run_id, operator, timestamp, row_count.
security.privacy_controls: PHI fields encrypted at CDM load time using Cloud KMS CMEK. De-identification DAG replaces participant_id with pseudonymous study_id before any BigQuery analytical export. GDPR withdrawal cascade deletes all PHI from CDM and marks records as purged in audit log.

data.entities: study_participants (id, site_id, redcap_record_id, enrollment_date, withdrawal_status ENUM active/withdrawn/withdrawn_deleted, pseudonymous_study_id). survey_responses (id, participant_id, survey_instrument_id, collection_date, field_values JSONB, ingestion_run_id). biomarker_results (id, participant_id, assay_code, collection_date, raw_value, unit, normalized_value, normalized_unit, reference_range, abnormal_flag, source_site_id). environmental_readings (id, participant_id, sensor_node_id, reading_type, value, unit, collected_at, harmonized_value, harmonized_unit). data_quality_flags (id, entity_type, entity_id, flag_type ENUM outlier/missing_mandatory/logic_violation, flag_detail, status ENUM open/resolved, assigned_coordinator, created_at). harmonization_rules (id, rule_version, source_field, target_cdm_field, transformation_type ENUM field_map/unit_convert/value_recode, rule_config JSONB, effective_from, effective_to).
data.storage_design: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with CMEK for CDM tables. GCS bucket for raw source file staging (30-day retention). BigQuery dataset for analytical exports with row-level pseudonymization applied.
data.retention_and_deletion: CDM records retained 10 years post-study completion per IRB protocol. Withdrawal deletion cascade deletes survey_responses, biomarker_results, environmental_readings for participant and writes to audit log. BigQuery analytical tables purged within 72 hours of withdrawal.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Composer 2 environment in us-central1 running Airflow 2.9 with worker nodes autoscaling 2-8. GitLab CI pushes DAG files and operator modules to GCS DAGs bucket on merge to main. Terraform manages Cloud Composer environment, Cloud SQL, BigQuery datasets, GCS buckets, Secret Manager secrets.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI pipeline: pylint + mypy type check, pytest unit tests for all operators and harmonization rule engine (80% coverage gate), integration tests against Cloud SQL test instance. Harmonization rule changes require two-approver merge request review. Terraform plan reviewed in CI before apply.
devops.observability_stack: Grafana dashboards on Prometheus metrics scraped from Cloud Composer Airflow exporter: dag_run_success_rate, dag_duration_seconds, quality_flag_count_by_site, records_ingested_per_run. Cloud Logging for structured DAG task logs. Email alert on dag_failure and when quality_flag_count exceeds configurable site thresholds.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a HIPAA-compliant Airflow pipeline on GCP for multi-site epidemiological data harmonization. Intentional flaw: the plan omits the GDPR Frankfurt sub-processor architectural solution and does not specify how the right-to-withdrawal cascade is triggered (API endpoint vs manual operator action). The harmonization rule engine is described as DB-backed config but the CDM load ordering dependency between survey_responses and biomarker_results is not stated. These gaps are appropriate for a 'flawed' quality rating.
target_output.title: Multi-site Epidemiological Data Harmonization Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: An Apache Airflow pipeline on GCP Cloud Composer 2 that nightly ingests survey responses from REDCap instances and biomarker readings from sensor APIs across up to 12 study sites, applies versioned harmonization rules to normalize fields and units into a common data model, runs automated quality checks, and distributes flagged-record reports to study coordinators within 48 hours of ingestion.
target_output.architecture_overview: DAG-based pipeline on Cloud Composer 2 in GCP us-central1. Three DAG types: per-site IngestDAG pulls raw records from REDCap API and sensor endpoints and stages them as JSON to GCS; HarmonizeDAG applies versioned FieldMappingRule and UnitConversionRule objects from the harmonization_rules table to transform staged records into CDM format and load to Cloud SQL PostgreSQL; QualityDAG evaluates outlier thresholds, mandatory field presence, and cross-variable logic rules, writing data_quality_flags and generating coordinator summary reports via Cloud Storage CSV export. BigQuery receives pseudonymized analytical snapshots for biostatistician queries. All PHI encrypted with Cloud KMS CMEK at rest.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestrator: Apache Airflow 2.9 on GCP Cloud Composer 2. Language: Python 3.12 with mypy strict typing. Source connectors: REDCap API v2 client, custom SensorAPIOperator for HTTP sensor endpoints. CDM storage: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with CMEK. Analytical warehouse: BigQuery with row-level pseudonymization. Staging: GCS buckets with 30-day lifecycle policy. Secrets: GCP Secret Manager with per-site credentials. IaC: Terraform 1.8. CI/CD: GitLab CI with two-approver merge for rule changes.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Per-site REDCap ingestion with incremental pull using last_modified timestamps. SensorAPI ingestion with configurable polling endpoints. GCS staging with site-partitioned object paths (gs://study-raw/{site_id}/{date}/). HarmonizationEngine applying DB-backed versioned rules with effective_from/effective_to date range enforcement. CDM loading to Cloud SQL with idempotent upsert on (participant_id, collection_date, source_site_id). Quality check suite: Z-score outlier detection on numeric biomarkers, mandatory field presence checks per survey instrument, cross-variable logic validation (e.g., biomarker collection_date must precede survey submission_date). Coordinator report generation as CSV to GCS with email notification via Cloud Functions trigger.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 environment: Airflow scheduler, workers (autoscale 2-8), web UI. IngestOperators: REDCapIngestOperator (paginated API pull, Delta tracking via XCom), SensorAPIIngestOperator (HTTP polling, retry backoff). HarmonizationEngine: Python class loading FieldMappingRule, UnitConversionRule from PostgreSQL harmonization_rules table, applying transformations in rule dependency order. QualityCheckSuite: OutlierDetector (IQR and Z-score per biomarker type), MandatoryFieldValidator, CrossVariableLogicValidator. CDMLoader: idempotent batch upsert to Cloud SQL. BigQueryExporter: pseudonymization step replacing participant_id with study_id before load. AuditLogger: appends to pipeline_audit_log on every entity operation.
target_output.workflows: Nightly ingestion workflow: at 02:00 UTC IngestDAG per site triggers REDCapIngestOperator, which pulls records modified since last successful run using XCom-stored watermark, stages JSON to GCS path gs://study-raw/{site_id}/{run_date}/. HarmonizeDAG waits for all site IngestDAGs via ExternalTaskSensor, iterates staged files, loads harmonization_rules for current date range, applies FieldMappingRule transformations (e.g., redcap_field_bmi_value -> cdm_biomarker_results.raw_value with unit kg/m2), loads to CDM tables with idempotent upsert. Quality report workflow: QualityDAG runs after HarmonizeDAG, computes flag counts per site, writes data_quality_flags records, generates CSV report to GCS, triggers Cloud Functions email to site coordinators with flag summary and GCS download link.
target_output.data_model: study_participants (id UUID PK, site_id FK, redcap_record_id, enrollment_date, withdrawal_status ENUM active/withdrawn/withdrawn_deleted, pseudonymous_study_id, created_at). survey_responses (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, survey_instrument_id, collection_date, field_values JSONB with CDM field names, ingestion_run_id, harmonized_at). biomarker_results (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, assay_code LOINC or study-specific, collection_date, raw_value numeric, unit varchar, normalized_value numeric, normalized_unit varchar, reference_range varchar, abnormal_flag boolean, source_site_id). environmental_readings (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, sensor_node_id, reading_type varchar, collected_at timestamptz, raw_value numeric, raw_unit, harmonized_value numeric, harmonized_unit). data_quality_flags (id UUID PK, entity_type varchar, entity_id UUID, flag_type ENUM outlier/missing_mandatory/logic_violation, flag_detail text, status ENUM open/resolved, assigned_coordinator_id, created_at). harmonization_rules (id UUID PK, rule_version integer, source_field, target_cdm_field, transformation_type ENUM field_map/unit_convert/value_recode, rule_config JSONB, effective_from date, effective_to date). Clinical harmonized entities: participant_clinical_data (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, primary_diagnosis_icd10 VARCHAR, comorbidity_icd10_list TEXT[], baseline_vitals_json JSONB, treatment_history_json JSONB, lab_result_harmonized_json JSONB, harmonized_at TIMESTAMPTZ), clinical_encounters_harmonized (id UUID PK, participant_id FK, source_site_id FK, encounter_date DATE, encounter_type VARCHAR, physician_id_hash VARCHAR, clinical_notes_excluded BOOLEAN).
target_output.api_design: No external API surface. Internal Airflow operator interfaces. REDCapIngestOperator.execute(context): pulls /api/?content=record from site REDCap with token from Secret Manager, pages until empty response, writes JSON to GCS. SensorAPIIngestOperator.execute(context): GET {sensor_endpoint}/readings?since={watermark_ts}, writes NDJSON to GCS. HarmonizationEngine.apply_rules(raw_record, site_id, run_date) -> CDMRecord. CDMLoader.upsert_batch(records: List[CDMRecord], table: str) -> int rows_affected. Withdrawal trigger: manual Airflow DAG run with conf={'participant_id': '...', 'requested_by': '...'}.
target_output.security_and_compliance: HIPAA controls: CMEK encryption for all Cloud SQL and GCS PHI storage, TLS 1.3 for all REDCap and sensor API calls, per-site service account credentials in Secret Manager with 90-day rotation, pipeline_audit_log append-only table with no DELETE privilege granted to pipeline service account. IRB: harmonization rule changes require two-approver GitLab merge request. GDPR: de-identification applied before BigQuery load replacing participant_id with pseudonymous_study_id; withdrawal deletion cascade tested DAG. NOTE: Frankfurt sub-processor architectural solution for European sites not yet specified - this is a known gap requiring legal review before EU site onboarding.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform manages Cloud Composer 2 environment, Cloud SQL instance, BigQuery datasets, GCS buckets, and Secret Manager secrets. GitLab CI on merge to main: pylint, mypy --strict, pytest with 80% coverage gate, integration tests against Cloud SQL test instance in CI. DAG deployment: GitLab CI syncs DAG files and operator modules to Cloud Composer GCS DAGs bucket on merge. Environments: dev (single Composer worker), staging (multi-worker mirroring prod), production (autoscale 2-8 workers, Cloud SQL HA). Harmonization rule migration: SQL migration files in /migrations, applied via GitLab CI pre-deploy step.
target_output.observability: Grafana dashboards on Prometheus metrics from Cloud Composer Airflow exporter: dag_run_success_rate per DAG, dag_run_duration_seconds p95, quality_flag_count_by_site and by flag_type, records_ingested_per_run per site. Cloud Logging for structured Airflow task logs with dag_id, run_id, site_id, participant_count. Alerts: PagerDuty on DAG failure within 15 minutes, email to study ops team when quality_flag_count_by_site exceeds configured threshold (default 5% of site records).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Composer 2 small environment ~$400/month at baseline. Cloud SQL db-standard-2 ~$150/month. BigQuery on-demand ~$50/month for biostatistician query volume. GCS staging negligible at 30-day lifecycle. Total ~$650/month for 12-site cohort at daily ingestion cadence. Scaling path: Composer worker count scales 2-8 on Airflow task queue depth; Cloud SQL vertical scale to db-standard-4 if CDM write throughput exceeds 500 records/second.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Pilot (10 weeks): REDCapIngestOperator, HarmonizationEngine with 50 baseline survey field rules, CDM load to Cloud SQL, outlier and missing-field quality checks, coordinator CSV report delivery, HIPAA security controls, 3-site pilot deployment. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Sensor API connectors for environmental readings, cross-variable logic validators, BigQuery pseudonymized export, withdrawal deletion DAG, full 12-site onboarding. Phase 3 (roadmap): Genomic VCF ingestion operators, streaming ingestion via Pub/Sub for wearable sensor data, self-service harmonization rule editor UI.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict type annotations on all operator and engine modules. pylint enforced in CI. All harmonization rule changes in /rules migration SQL with two-approver GitLab MR review. Unit tests for HarmonizationEngine with fixture records from each site's field schema. Integration tests run against Cloud SQL test instance populated with de-identified pilot records. No PHI in test fixtures; use synthetic participant records only. Audit log writes are non-negotiable: any operator accessing CDM must call AuditLogger before and after.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GDPR Frankfurt sub-processor requirement for European sites not architecturally resolved. Mitigation: defer EU site onboarding until legal review defines sub-processor solution. Risk: REDCap API rate limits at high site count causing ingestion window overrun. Mitigation: per-site ingestion parallelism limited by Composer worker count; add incremental delta pull logic to stay within rate limits. Risk: harmonization rule engine dependency ordering errors causing CDM field overwrites. Mitigation: rule dependency graph validated at load time with cycle detection. Tradeoff: GCS staging step adds latency but enables reprocessing without re-pulling from source APIs.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Orchestrator selection: Airflow on Cloud Composer 2 over custom cron scripts for retry logic, lineage tracking, and observability. CDM write strategy: idempotent upsert on (participant_id, collection_date, source_site_id) to handle reprocessing runs. Withdrawal trigger mechanism: manual Airflow DAG run with conf dict - automated API trigger deferred to Phase 2. BigQuery access control: pseudonymization required before any BigQuery load; biostatisticians never access Cloud SQL directly.

metadata.notes: Intentionally flawed: GDPR Frankfurt sub-processor solution omitted; withdrawal trigger mechanism underspecified as manual Airflow run without API surface definition.

project_goal.value: Desktop application for forensic laboratory analysts to document, chain-of-custody track, and report on physical evidence samples in compliance with accreditation standards, operating offline-capable in secure facility environments
project_goal.rationale: Replaces paper-based chain-of-custody logs with auditable digital records for court admissibility

target_users.value: Forensic analysts, evidence custodians, laboratory supervisors, and accreditation auditors at law enforcement forensic labs
target_users.rationale: Primary users span bench analysts and supervisors who approve and sign reports

project_class.rationale: Offline-capable operation in secure facility requires embedded local runtime, not browser app

capabilities.rationale: Requires local UI, embedded data persistence, local auth, and admin configuration panel

complexity_level.rationale: Offline SQLite with barcode scanning, digital signatures, and tamper-evident PDF export are moderate complexity

risk_level.rationale: Chain-of-custody records are legal evidence; unauthorized modification could compromise court cases

data_sensitivity.rationale: Evidence records are law enforcement confidential; access must be role-restricted and audited

external_exposure.rationale: Application runs on isolated workstations in secure evidence rooms; no internet connectivity

access_model.value: Local RBAC with analyst, supervisor, evidence_custodian, and admin roles; JWT sessions with 4-hour expiry; hardware-based login via badge reader optional
access_model.rationale: Role separation ensures analysts cannot approve their own reports; custodians cannot modify examination records

feature_scope.value: Evidence intake with barcode generation, chain-of-custody transfer logging with barcode scan, examination record creation with photo attachment, digital supervisor approval, tamper-evident PDF court report export, admin panel for user management and audit log review
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope to replace paper chain-of-custody and typed reports

mvp_scope.value: Evidence intake, barcode scan transfer logging, examination record, and PDF report export with supervisor digital approval
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum needed for accreditation pilot audit

security_baseline.value: SQLite database encrypted with SQLCipher, no external network calls, all user actions written to append-only audit log, PDF reports cryptographically signed with analyst private key stored in OS keychain
security_baseline.rationale: ISO 17025 and ASCLD requirements for tamper-evident records

frontend_stack.rationale: Electron provides cross-platform desktop packaging with Node.js access for SQLite and barcode hardware

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 embedded in Electron main process
backend_stack.rationale: Embedded Node.js for local business logic and SQLite access without a separate server process

data_platform.value: SQLite 3.46 with SQLCipher extension for at-rest encryption
data_platform.rationale: Offline-capable embedded database with encryption meets accreditation requirements

hosting_target.value: On-premises workstation install via MSI/DMG installer package
hosting_target.rationale: Lab IT deploys to isolated workstations in evidence handling rooms

privacy_retention_policy.value: Evidence records retained per case retention schedule (minimum 7 years); deleted only by admin role after case closure and court authorization; all deletions logged to audit trail
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Law enforcement evidence retention statutes and court admissibility requirements

future_scope.value: LIMS integration for automated instrument result import, multi-workstation replication for shared evidence rooms, mobile barcode scanner companion app
future_scope.rationale: Year 2 integration roadmap after accreditation pilot

constraints.value: Must operate fully offline; no cloud dependencies; installer under 200 MB; supports Windows 10/11 workstations
constraints.rationale: Secure facility network isolation and lab IT packaging requirements

observability_baseline.value: Local audit log written to append-only SQLite table; log export to CSV available to admin role; application errors logged to local file with 30-day rolling retention
observability_baseline.rationale: Audit logs are primary compliance artifact for accreditation reviews

execution_preference.value: Waterfall delivery phases aligned to accreditation audit calendar; versioned installer releases with migration scripts
execution_preference.rationale: Accreditation audits are on fixed calendar dates requiring stable software versions

compliance_context.value: ASCLD/LAB accreditation, ISO/IEC 17025 laboratory quality standard, FBI Quality Assurance Standards for DNA labs, local law enforcement agency evidence handling policies
compliance_context.rationale: Forensic lab accreditation bodies require documented chain-of-custody and tamper-evident records

project.goal: Offline-capable forensic evidence chain-of-custody desktop application
project.target_users: Forensic analysts, custodians, supervisors

backend.framework: IPC handlers in Electron main process

security.auth: Local JWT with 4-hour expiry, bcrypt passwords
security.encryption: SQLCipher for SQLite, OS keychain for signing keys

reasoner_reviews.product: Core feature scope is correct for accreditation readiness. Tamper-evident PDF with digital signature is the key deliverable for court admissibility. Admin panel for audit log review is important for the accreditation audit itself.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Electron with embedded Node.js is the right choice for offline desktop with barcode hardware access. The IPC channel pattern via contextBridge correctly maintains process isolation. The plan remains thin on the digital signature implementation - specifying which crypto library and key storage mechanism would strengthen the security architecture.
reasoner_reviews.security: SQLCipher encryption is appropriate for at-rest protection. The append-only audit log must be enforced at the database trigger level, not just application level, to withstand tampering by a compromised analyst account. The PDF signing key stored in OS keychain must be non-exportable.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Fully offline constraint is met by SQLite embedded approach. 200 MB installer limit is achievable with Electron pruning. Windows 10/11 compatibility requires Electron 30 which ships Chromium 126, compatible with React 18.

backend.service_design: Electron main process hosts Node.js IPC handlers organized into domain modules: EvidenceModule (intake, transfer, location), ExaminationModule (record creation, photo attachment), ReportModule (PDF generation, digital signing), AuditModule (append-only log writes), AuthModule (JWT issuance, session validation).
backend.api_patterns: Electron contextBridge exposes typed IPC channels: ipcRenderer.invoke('evidence:intake', payload), ipcRenderer.invoke('evidence:transfer', {evidenceId, toAnalystId, scannedBarcode}), ipcRenderer.invoke('report:sign-and-export', {reportId}). Main process handlers validate JWT session before any database operation.
backend.failure_handling: SQLite transaction rollback on any write failure. Barcode scan validation rejects unrecognized barcode formats with error displayed in UI before database write. PDF signing failure surfaces error to supervisor with option to retry or export unsigned draft marked as DRAFT ONLY.

security.auth_design: Local user store in SQLite users table with bcrypt-hashed passwords. JWT issued on login with 4-hour expiry, stored in Electron safeStorage (OS credential store). Role checked on every IPC handler invocation.
security.authorization_model: RBAC: analyst (create examination records, submit for review), supervisor (approve/reject examinations, sign reports), evidence_custodian (intake and transfer evidence items only), admin (user management, audit log export, case closure).
security.audit_and_logging_controls: SQLite AFTER INSERT/UPDATE triggers on evidence_items, custody_transfer_events, examination_records, and reports write to audit_log table (append-only: no DELETE or UPDATE privilege for application role). Analyst, action, timestamp, record_id captured per event.

data.entities: evidence_items (id, case_id, barcode, description, received_at, current_location_id, current_custodian_id, status ENUM intaken/in_analysis/in_storage/disposed). custody_transfer_events (id, evidence_item_id, from_analyst_id, to_analyst_id, scanned_barcode, transfer_timestamp, notes). examination_records (id, evidence_item_id, analyst_id, examination_type, findings_text, photo_attachments JSON paths, started_at, completed_at, status ENUM draft/submitted/approved/rejected). reports (id, case_id, examination_record_ids JSON, draft_pdf_path, signed_pdf_path, supervisor_id, signed_at, signature_hash). audit_log (id, user_id, action, table_name, record_id, old_values JSONB, new_values JSONB, timestamp).
data.storage_design: SQLite database at %APPDATA%/ForensicLIMS/evidence.db encrypted with SQLCipher. Photo attachments stored as files in %APPDATA%/ForensicLIMS/attachments/ referenced by path in examination_records.
data.retention_and_deletion: Evidence records retained per case_files.retention_date set by admin at case closure. Admin-only deletion requires dual-authorization confirmation and writes deletion event to audit_log.

devops.deployment_topology: Single workstation install via electron-builder MSI (Windows) and DMG (macOS). No server component. Database file and attachments stored in OS app data directory.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on PR: TypeScript tsc check, eslint, vitest unit tests for IPC handlers and domain modules. Release workflow: electron-builder packages MSI and DMG, signs with code-signing certificate, uploads to GitHub Releases as versioned artifacts.
devops.rollback_strategy: Installer versioning with SQLite migration scripts in /migrations directory. Rollback runs down-migration SQL before installing prior version. Migrations tested in CI against SQLite test database.

frontend.app_structure: React SPA within Electron renderer process. Route-based pages: EvidenceIntake, EvidenceDetail (with custody timeline), ExaminationForm, ReportReview, AdminPanel, Login.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Evidence intake: admin scans or enters barcode, fills evidence description, selects receiving custodian, submits. Transfer: analyst scans evidence barcode and their own badge barcode, confirms transfer to new custodian. Examination: analyst opens evidence item, creates examination record, attaches photos, submits for supervisor review. Report signing: supervisor reviews examination record, approves, triggers PDF generation and digital signing.
frontend.accessibility_notes: High-contrast mode for evidence room lighting conditions. Keyboard shortcuts for barcode scan workflow (Tab to advance fields). Large click targets for gloved-hand operation.

ISS-001.title: Digital signature crypto library not specified
ISS-001.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 describe PDF signing but do not specify the cryptographic library, key algorithm, or certificate chain required for court admissibility. ISO 17025 requires documented validation of the signing process.

history.note: Plan still references signing without specifying library or key type

ISS-002.title: Audit log append-only enforcement only at application layer
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 plan addressed by specifying SQLite AFTER INSERT triggers for audit_log writes and removing DELETE privilege from application role.

ISS-003.title: Photo attachment storage path not specified for multi-user workstation
ISS-003.detail: Attachments stored by path in %APPDATA% but on shared workstations multiple analyst profiles could have conflicting path references if the database is shared.

focus_issues.title: Digital signature crypto library not specified
focus_issues.detail: Plan does not specify cryptographic library, key algorithm, or certificate chain for court-admissible PDF signing.

Address shared workstation attachment path by storing attachments in a single shared directory with case/evidence sub-paths independent of OS user profile

previous_plan.title: Forensic Evidence Chain-of-Custody Desktop Application - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Electron desktop application for forensic labs providing offline barcode-tracked evidence chain-of-custody, examination records with photo attachments, and digital supervisor-signed PDF court reports on SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: SQLCipher encryption at rest. SQLite trigger-based append-only audit_log. JWT sessions with OS credential store. PDF signed with private key stored in OS keychain. ASCLD and ISO 17025 compliance. Specific signing library not yet named.
previous_plan.data_model: evidence_items, custody_transfer_events, examination_records, reports, audit_log tables in SQLite.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: electron-builder MSI for Windows workstations. GitHub Actions CI with TypeScript check and vitest. Versioned installers with SQLite down-migration scripts.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision resolving ISS-001 by specifying pdf-lib with node-forge for RSA-2048 PKCS#12 signing using a lab-issued self-signed certificate stored as non-exportable in Windows Certificate Store (CNG). Also addressing ISS-003 by moving attachment storage from per-user %APPDATA% to a shared application directory. Plan quality is weak: several sections remain thin from prior rounds but the blocking signing gap is now closed.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified pdf-lib 1.x with node-forge RSA-2048 SHA-256 signing. Lab admin generates PKCS#12 certificate via lab CA. Private key stored as non-exportable in Windows Certificate Store (CNG) via node-forge CNG bridge. Signing operation reads cert from CNG, signs PDF hash, embeds signature and certificate chain in PDF/A-3 format. Documented in security_and_compliance and system_components.
fix_report.expected_outcome: ISO 17025 validation documentation can now reference specific library version, key algorithm, and certificate management procedure; ISS-001 resolved.

fix_report.action_taken: Changed attachment storage base path from per-user %APPDATA% to a shared C:/ForensicLIMS/attachments/{case_id}/{evidence_id}/ directory configured at install time by lab IT. Database stores relative paths from this shared root. Documented in data_model.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Multi-analyst shared workstation access to attachments works correctly regardless of OS user profile; ISS-003 resolved.

target_output.title: Forensic Evidence Chain-of-Custody Desktop Application
target_output.executive_summary: An Electron 30 desktop application providing fully offline forensic evidence chain-of-custody tracking, barcode-scan transfer logging, examination record creation with photo attachments, and ISO 17025 compliant tamper-evident PDF court reports signed with RSA-2048 certificates on SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite for Windows workstations in secure facility environments.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron 30 with React 18 renderer and Node.js 20 main process. Main process exposes typed IPC channels via contextBridge for all data operations. SQLite 3.46 with SQLCipher at-rest encryption stores evidence_items, custody_transfer_events, examination_records, reports, and audit_log. AFTER INSERT triggers on all entity tables write to audit_log enforcing append-only compliance at database level. PDF reports generated with pdf-lib and signed with node-forge RSA-2048 certificates from Windows CNG. Shared attachment directory accessible to all analysts on the workstation.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron 30, React 18, TypeScript 5.4, Tailwind CSS, Zustand. Backend: Node.js 20 embedded in Electron main process. Database: SQLite 3.46 with SQLCipher 4.x extension. PDF: pdf-lib 1.x for generation, node-forge RSA-2048 SHA-256 for signing with Windows CNG via CNG bridge. Barcode: node-serialport for USB barcode scanner HID input. Build: electron-builder for MSI installer. CI: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Evidence intake: barcode generation (Code 128), evidence description, receiving custodian assignment, status tracking. Chain-of-custody transfer: barcode scan of evidence item and analyst badge, transfer recorded with timestamp. Examination: record creation with findings text, photo file attachment, status progression draft to submitted. Report generation: pdf-lib renders examination findings with case metadata, node-forge signs PDF with analyst RSA-2048 key from CNG, supervisor approves with counter-signature. Admin panel: user management (CRUD for analyst accounts and role assignments), audit log CSV export, case closure workflow.
target_output.system_components: Electron renderer (React SPA): UI pages for all workflows. Electron main process IPC handlers: EvidenceModule, ExaminationModule, ReportModule (pdf-lib generation + node-forge CNG signing), AuditModule, AuthModule. SQLite database with SQLCipher: entity tables plus trigger-enforced audit_log. Windows CNG Certificate Store: non-exportable RSA-2048 private keys per analyst, installed by lab admin. Shared attachment directory C:/ForensicLIMS/attachments/: case/evidence-partitioned file store. electron-builder MSI installer: packages all Node.js, SQLite, and SQLCipher binaries for offline workstation deployment.
