backend.service_design: Go Fiber monolith with distinct packages: mqtt_subscriber (Paho client, topic routing), sensor_processor (limit checking, alarm evaluation), websocket_hub (client registry, broadcast), api_handlers (REST for history, alarms, config), influx_writer (batched point writes every 5s)
backend.api_patterns: REST at /api/v1 for configuration and historical queries; WebSocket /ws/zones/{zone_id} for live sensor feeds; JWT bearer auth on all endpoints
backend.background_jobs: MQTT subscriber goroutine pool per zone, alarm evaluator triggered on each measurement ingestion, shift report generator cron at end-of-shift 8-hour boundary
backend.failure_handling: Redis buffer for latest readings if InfluxDB write batch temporarily unavailable; alarm escalation email retry with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts; WebSocket client reconnect with 3-second backoff

frontend.app_structure: React SPA with react-router-dom v6: ZoneDashboard, SensorDetail, AlarmManager, TrendAnalytics, ShiftReport, AdminConfig routes
frontend.pages_and_flows: ZoneDashboard shows live particle count ISO class indicator, temperature and humidity gauges with spec limit bands and alarm badges per zone; AlarmManager lists active and acknowledged alarms with acknowledge action that posts to audit endpoint
frontend.state_management: Zustand store for active alarm list and WebSocket connection state; React Query for historical InfluxDB data fetches with 60-second stale time
frontend.performance_notes: WebSocket data throttled to 1-second UI refresh even if sensors push at 30-second cadence; Recharts datasets capped at 1440 points per trend view to prevent render jank

security.auth_design: Keycloak 22 OIDC with PKCE flow; React stores tokens in memory only; Fiber middleware validates JWT on each WebSocket upgrade and REST request
security.authorization_model: Technician read-only dashboard access; Engineer alarm acknowledge and sensor node config; Supervisor specification limit thresholds; Admin user management via Keycloak realm
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All alarm acknowledgements written to audit_events InfluxDB measurement with user_id, alarm_id, action, timestamp; exported as CSV for ISO 14644-2 audit evidence packages

data.entities: sensor_nodes (id UUID PK, zone_id FK, node_type ENUM particle_counter/temperature/humidity, mqtt_topic, calibration_due_date, status), specification_limits (id UUID PK, zone_id FK, parameter, lower_limit, upper_limit, iso_class), alarm_events (id UUID PK, node_id FK, triggered_at, value_at_trigger, status ENUM active/acknowledged/cleared, acknowledged_by UUID), cleanroom_zones (id UUID PK, zone_name, iso_class_target)
data.storage_design: InfluxDB 2.7 bucket cleanroom_env with 2-year retention; Redis hash per sensor node for latest reading; PostgreSQL 16 for alarm rules, zone config, and shift reports
data.retention_and_deletion: InfluxDB bucket retention policy enforces 730-day TTL on env_readings; alarm_events PostgreSQL records retained 5 years per EHS policy

devops.deployment_topology: Bare-metal Kubernetes 1.29 three-node cluster on facility network; Mosquitto DaemonSet on sensor-network node; InfluxDB StatefulSet with 4TB local PV; Harbor on-prem registry for air-gap compliance
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: golangci-lint, go test -race, ESLint, Docker multi-stage build, push to Harbor, helm upgrade on staging namespace, manual approval gate for production
devops.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback to previous chart revision; InfluxDB data unaffected by app rollback; Redis rebuilt from live MQTT stream on startup

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a real-time cleanroom environmental monitoring system on air-gapped facility Kubernetes. Key decisions: Go Fiber for low-latency MQTT-to-WebSocket pipeline, InfluxDB 2.7 for time-series storage with 2-year retention matching ISO 14644-2, reusing existing Keycloak 22 SSO. Alarm acknowledgement audit trail is the critical compliance artifact.
target_output.title: Cleanroom Environmental Monitoring Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A real-time environmental monitoring web application for semiconductor research cleanroom facilities built with React 18 and Go 1.22 Fiber on air-gapped bare-metal Kubernetes. Ingests particle counter, temperature, and humidity readings from sensor nodes via Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker, evaluates measurements against zone-specific ISO 14644 specification limits, fans out live data to engineer dashboards via WebSocket, and escalates out-of-spec alarm events with a full Keycloak-attributed audit trail for compliance evidence.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven architecture with Eclipse Mosquitto MQTT broker receiving sensor node telemetry on per-zone topics. Go Fiber backend maintains per-topic subscriber goroutines via Eclipse Paho client. Each measurement is evaluated against Redis-cached specification limits for the zone, written to InfluxDB 2.7 in 5-second batches, and broadcast to subscribed WebSocket clients via an in-process goroutine hub. React 18 SPA connects over WebSocket /ws/zones/{zone_id} for live gauge updates. Keycloak 22 handles OIDC authentication with LDAP federation to facility directory. GitLab CI builds images and pushes to on-premises Harbor registry for Helm deployment on air-gapped Kubernetes cluster.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Recharts for gauges and time-series sparklines, Zustand for alarm state, react-router-dom v6. Backend: Go 1.22, Fiber v2, Eclipse Paho MQTT client, gorilla/websocket, golang-jwt/jwt. Time-Series DB: InfluxDB 2.7 with Flux query language, 2-year retention bucket. Cache: Redis 7 for latest sensor readings and active alarm state. Auth: Keycloak 22 with LDAP federation. Infra: bare-metal Kubernetes 1.29, Helm 3, Harbor registry, Eclipse Mosquitto 2.x broker, GitLab CI with on-prem runner, Grafana + Prometheus.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Zone Environmental Dashboard: live particle count ISO class indicator, temperature and humidity gauges with colored spec limit bands, per-zone alarm status badge. Sensor Node Registry: inventory with calibration due dates, node type, MQTT topic, and status. Alarm Rule Engine: configurable upper/lower specification limits per parameter and zone ISO class. Alarm Escalation Workflow: active alarm queue, engineer acknowledge action with audit trail, SMTP email to zone escalation contacts. Historical Trend Analytics: Flux-queried time-range charts with Recharts, CSV export for compliance records. Shift Compliance Report: auto-generated 8-hour summary of measurement statistics and any alarm events as downloadable PDF.
target_output.system_components: Eclipse Mosquitto 2.x MQTT broker (DaemonSet on sensor-network node) receiving telemetry from particle counters, thermocouples, and humidity sensors. Go Fiber application (2 replicas) with MQTT subscriber pool, alarm evaluator, WebSocket hub, and REST API. InfluxDB 2.7 StatefulSet with 4TB local PV storing env_readings measurements. Redis 7 Deployment for latest-reading hashes and active alarm sets. Keycloak 22 StatefulSet for OIDC and LDAP federation. React 18 SPA served via Nginx Ingress. Harbor on-prem container registry for air-gap image staging.
target_output.workflows: Sensor Measurement Ingestion: Particle counter publishes JSON to MQTT topic cleanroom/{zone_id}/{node_id}/env at 30-second intervals; Paho subscriber goroutine deserializes payload, extracts particle_count_0_5um and particle_count_5um fields, writes point to InfluxDB batch writer, updates Redis hash hset sensor_latest:{node_id} value {val} timestamp {ts}, and publishes measurement to WebSocket hub which broadcasts JSON to all clients subscribed to zone channel. Out-of-Spec Alarm Flow: Alarm evaluator goroutine compares each ingested measurement against specification_limits row for the zone parameter; when value exceeds upper_limit, inserts alarm_events record with status active, pushes alarm_id to Redis set active_alarms:{zone_id}, sends SMTP notification to zone escalation contacts, and emits alarm event over WebSocket to connected dashboard clients. Engineer opens AlarmManager, clicks Acknowledge on active alarm, client POSTs to /api/v1/alarms/{id}/acknowledge with JWT; backend updates alarm_events status to acknowledged, writes audit_events InfluxDB point with user_id, alarm_id, action=acknowledge, timestamp.
target_output.data_model: sensor_nodes (id UUID PK, zone_id UUID FK, node_name TEXT, node_type ENUM particle_counter/temperature/humidity, mqtt_topic TEXT, calibration_due_date DATE, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM active/offline/calibrating). specification_limits (id UUID PK, zone_id UUID FK, parameter ENUM particle_0_5um/particle_5um/temperature_c/humidity_pct, lower_limit FLOAT8, upper_limit FLOAT8, iso_class ENUM ISO1-ISO9). alarm_events (id UUID PK, node_id UUID FK, rule_id UUID FK, triggered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, value_at_trigger FLOAT8, status ENUM active/acknowledged/cleared, acknowledged_by UUID, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ). cleanroom_zones (id UUID PK, zone_name TEXT, iso_class_target TEXT, area_sqm FLOAT4, max_occupancy INT). InfluxDB measurement env_readings: tags zone_id, node_id, sensor_type; fields value FLOAT, unit STRING; nanosecond timestamp precision.
target_output.api_design: REST at /api/v1 with Keycloak JWT bearer auth. GET /zones returns cleanroom zones with current ISO class compliance status. GET /zones/{id}/readings?start=&stop=&sensor_type= queries InfluxDB Flux for historical time-range measurements. GET /alarms?status=active returns active alarm_events with node and zone context. POST /alarms/{id}/acknowledge updates status and writes audit record. GET /nodes returns sensor inventory with calibration status. PUT /spec-limits/{zone_id} updates specification limit thresholds (supervisor role claim required). WebSocket /ws/zones/{zone_id} pushes live measurement objects and alarm events to subscribed clients.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Keycloak 22 OIDC PKCE flow with LDAP federation to facility Active Directory; role claims mapped to technician, engineer, supervisor, admin. Fiber JWT middleware validates token expiry and role on every request including WebSocket upgrade handshake. React stores access token in memory only, never localStorage. All alarm acknowledgements written to audit_events InfluxDB measurement per ISO 14644-2 traceability requirements and exportable as CSV for audit evidence packages. TLS 1.3 enforced on all internal endpoints via Kubernetes Ingress TLS termination with cert-manager. Mosquitto broker configured for MQTT-over-TLS with sensor node client certificates.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes 1.29 three-node cluster isolated on facility network. Helm chart components: Go Fiber Deployment (2 replicas, anti-affinity across nodes), InfluxDB StatefulSet with 4TB local PV and storage class for direct SSD access, Redis Deployment, Keycloak StatefulSet, Nginx Ingress controller. Mosquitto deployed as DaemonSet on the node with dual-NIC sensor network interface. GitLab CI pipeline stages: lint (golangci-lint, ESLint), test (go test -race, Jest with Testing Library), Docker multi-stage build, push to Harbor, helm upgrade on staging namespace, manual approve gate for production promotion. All container images pre-staged to Harbor from offline mirror snapshots for air-gap compliance. Database migrations run as Kubernetes Jobs before each deployment.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes Go Fiber /metrics endpoint exposing: mqtt_messages_received_total per zone, websocket_clients_connected, alarm_evaluations_total, influx_write_batch_size_histogram. Grafana dashboards: MQTT ingestion rate per zone, WebSocket client count, alarm evaluation p99 latency, InfluxDB write success rate, disk utilization trending. Prometheus alerts: mqtt_message_gap_seconds > 120 per sensor node (offline sensor), smtp_alarm_delivery_failures_consecutive > 3, influxdb_disk_used_pct > 80.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: On-premises deployment has no cloud compute cost. InfluxDB storage estimated at 50GB per year at 30-second resolution for 100 sensor nodes. Kubernetes cluster runs on existing facility bare-metal hardware. Redis memory approximately 10MB for 100 sensor latest-reading hashes. Horizontal scaling path: additional Go Fiber replicas behind Kubernetes Service for WebSocket load if concurrent client count exceeds 50; InfluxDB disk expansion via PV resize without data loss.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): MQTT subscriber for particle counter and temperature topics in 3 cleanroom zones, InfluxDB write pipeline, ZoneDashboard with live gauges, threshold alarm detection and SMTP email notification, Keycloak OIDC integration, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Humidity sensor ingestion, alarm acknowledgement workflow with audit trail, historical trend charts, specification limit configuration UI for supervisors, shift compliance report PDF export. Phase 3 (2 weeks): Sensor calibration due date tracking and overdue alerts, on-call escalation contact management UI, ISO 14644-2 audit evidence export package generation.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go: golangci-lint with errcheck and staticcheck enabled, table-driven unit tests for alarm evaluator and specification limit logic with 85% coverage gate, integration tests using Mosquitto test container via testcontainers-go. TypeScript strict mode, ESLint Airbnb config, Prettier. React components tested with Testing Library for alarm badge state transitions and acknowledge flow. Sensor hardware simulator binary included in dev tooling for local MQTT testing without physical equipment. GitLab CI blocks merge on any lint or test failure.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: MQTT subscriber goroutine crash drops measurements silently between sensor push intervals. Mitigation: recover from panics in subscriber goroutines, emit dead-letter event to Redis for monitoring, Prometheus alert on per-zone message gap exceeding 120 seconds. Risk: InfluxDB disk exhaustion if retention policy fails or disk fills faster than projected. Mitigation: Prometheus disk alert at 80%, monthly retention policy run validated in staging, on-call runbook for emergency shard deletion. Tradeoff: InfluxDB over TimescaleDB for native Flux downsampling and built-in retention management at cost of PostgreSQL ecosystem familiarity. Tradeoff: WebSocket over SSE chosen for bidirectional alarm acknowledgement signaling from server to client.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected InfluxDB 2.x Flux over InfluxQL for native downsampling and continuous query tasks. Confirmed Recharts over D3 for team maintainability. MQTT protocol chosen over direct TCP polling to match sensor hardware capability. SMS alarm escalation deferred to Phase 3 pending facility SMS gateway availability confirmation from IT.

metadata.notes: Real-time ISO 14644 cleanroom monitoring on air-gapped bare-metal Kubernetes with Go Fiber MQTT-to-WebSocket pipeline.

project_goal.value: AI-assisted anomaly detection for BSL-3 lab access control analyzing badge events, video analytics, and environmental sensors to identify unauthorized access or protocol deviations
project_goal.rationale: Core biosafety mission requiring real-time behavioral pattern analysis

target_users.value: Biosafety officers, facility security administrators, and shift supervisors at BSL-3 research facilities
target_users.rationale: Primary operators of anomaly review and alert disposition workflows

project_class.rationale: Core functionality is ML-driven anomaly scoring of streaming access events

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, ai_llm, realtime, analytics
capabilities.rationale: Streaming ingestion, ML inference, secured access, and officer review dashboard required

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source real-time fusion with ML inference and compliance auditability requirements

risk_level.rationale: Failure to detect unauthorized BSL-3 access could result in biosafety incidents with public health consequences

data_sensitivity.rationale: Personnel movement data and biosafety incident records are sensitive institutional data

external_exposure.rationale: System operates on air-gapped facility network with no external internet access

access_model.value: RBAC with biosafety_officer, security_admin, supervisor, and auditor roles; Keycloak enforcing mandatory re-authentication after 8-hour shift

mvp_scope.rationale: Badge system integration is fastest path to operational value for BSL-3 oversight

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all internal service communication, mTLS between sensor ingestion services and Kafka brokers, encrypted PostgreSQL WAL, Keycloak SAML integration with facility LDAP
security_baseline.rationale: Facility network security policy mandates mTLS on critical sensor data paths

frontend_stack.value: No browser frontend; officer dashboard delivered via internal Grafana instance with PostgreSQL and InfluxDB data sources
frontend_stack.rationale: FrontendAgent not applicable; Grafana panels consume alert and anomaly APIs directly

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async performance fits streaming ingestion; on-premises required for air-gapped network

data_platform.rationale: Multi-model storage matches distinct access, behavioral, and sensor data patterns

hosting_target.value: On-premises bare-metal Kubernetes cluster on air-gapped facility LAN
hosting_target.rationale: CDC/USDA Select Agent facility network cannot connect to cloud providers

privacy_retention_policy.value: Access event logs retained 7 years per CDC Select Agent regulations; anomaly detection records retained 10 years; personnel data purged 90 days after employment termination
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Federal Select Agent Program record-keeping requirements

constraints.rationale: Air-gap requirement eliminates any external model API; hardware budget is fixed

observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standards adapted for air-gapped deployment

execution_preference.value: Iterative 3-week sprints with biosafety officer acceptance testing after each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Regulatory sensitivity requires domain expert sign-off before each capability goes live

compliance_context.value: CDC/USDA Select Agent regulations (42 CFR Part 73), NIH biosafety guidelines, IBC review requirements, NIST SP 800-53 controls for federal research systems
compliance_context.rationale: BSL-3 Select Agent facilities have federal regulatory oversight requiring documented access control audit trails

project.goal: Detect BSL-3 access anomalies in real time using badge, video, and sensor data fusion
project.target_users: Biosafety officers and facility security administrators
project.timeline: MVP in 6 months, full sensor integration in 12 months

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope focusing on badge event anomaly detection is prudent given integration complexity of video analytics. The officer alert dashboard is critical for operational adoption - biosafety officers need clear, actionable incident timelines with AI-generated explanations to justify alert dispositions for regulatory auditors reviewing 42 CFR Part 73 compliance records.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Air-gap constraint drives every major architectural decision. Local LLaMA 7B inference on A100s should achieve sub-200ms latency for single-event scoring. The multi-database approach (PostgreSQL plus InfluxDB plus Redis) is justified by the distinct access patterns of personnel records versus time-series sensor data versus real-time event windows. Redis Sentinel is the right choice over Cluster given the small scale and operational simplicity requirements.
reasoner_reviews.security: mTLS on sensor ingestion paths is essential given the potential for rogue device injection attacks in a physically accessible lab environment. Keycloak SAML with facility LDAP is the correct integration pattern. The mandatory supervisor countersignature for false-positive dispositions should be enforced cryptographically, not just through application logic, to satisfy IBC audit requirements.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: On-prem Kubernetes with air-gap adds significant operational complexity for model retraining pipelines. Monthly retraining cycles are realistic given the USB transfer overhead. GitLab CI runner dual-homed networking must be carefully firewalled to prevent accidental production network access during build.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan missed explicit model drift monitoring and did not address the feedback loop data quality risk. If biosafety officers dismiss alerts as false positives without documentation, the retraining pipeline will reinforce incorrect baseline behavior, a safety-critical failure mode that must be addressed in revision 2.

backend.service_design: Six FastAPI microservices: BadgeIngestorService (Kafka consumer for access events), SensorIngestorService (Kafka consumer writing to InfluxDB), AnomalyScorer (ML inference with llama.cpp endpoint on GPU nodes), AlertManagerService (alert lifecycle with disposition enforcement), IncidentDocumentationService (timeline reconstruction API), ModelFeedbackService (validated disposition export to MLflow)
backend.api_patterns: Internal REST APIs at /api/v1; Kafka topics: badge-events (partitioned by zone_id), sensor-readings (partitioned by node_id), anomaly-scores (partitioned by personnel_id); async aiokafka consumers with at-least-once delivery and idempotent processing using kafka_offset deduplication
backend.failure_handling: Dead-letter topics for malformed badge events; AnomalyScorer circuit breaker falls back to rule-based zone authorization checks when ML service is unhealthy; AlertManagerService maintains alert state in PostgreSQL for crash recovery across pod restarts
backend.background_jobs: Nightly model performance metrics aggregation; weekly false-positive rate report by zone exported to Grafana annotation store; monthly retraining data export to MLflow artifact store during low-activity window 02:00-05:00
backend.scaling_notes: AnomalyScorer scaled to 3 replicas sharing A100 GPU memory via NVIDIA Multi-Process Service; Kafka consumer groups allow independent scaling of BadgeIngestor and SensorIngestor workers

security.auth_design: Keycloak 24 realm with SAML 2.0 binding to facility Active Directory; JWT tokens with 8-hour expiry matching shift boundaries; YubiKey hardware token required for biosafety_officer and security_admin roles
security.authorization_model: RBAC enforced at FastAPI middleware: biosafety_officer can disposition alerts and view personnel event timelines; security_admin can configure zone rules and manage personnel profiles; supervisor read-only dashboard; auditor role provides read-only incident history access with anonymized personnel IDs only
security.secrets_management: HashiCorp Vault 1.16 on Kubernetes for database credentials and Kafka SASL keys; Vault Agent Sidecar Injector for runtime secret delivery; automatic lease renewal with 30-day rotation; no secrets in ConfigMaps or environment variables
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable audit_events table using PostgreSQL append-only role (REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE FROM audit_role); all officer actions logged with Keycloak user ID, timestamp, IP, and SHA-256 hash chaining each entry to prior; daily encrypted offline backup to removable media with IBC chain-of-custody log
security.security_risks: Physical access to Kubernetes nodes could compromise cluster; mitigated with node TPM-based disk encryption, BIOS passwords, and node enrollment certificates pinned to cluster CA

data.entities: personnel_profiles (id UUID PK, employee_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, name_encrypted BYTEA, clearance_level ENUM, zone_authorizations JSONB, active BOOLEAN). access_events (id UUID PK, personnel_id FK, zone_id FK, reader_id VARCHAR, event_type ENUM entry/exit/denied/tailgate_detected, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, kafka_offset BIGINT). anomaly_detections (id UUID PK, personnel_id FK, zone_id FK, score FLOAT, severity ENUM, explanation_text TEXT, model_version VARCHAR). alert_records (id UUID PK, anomaly_id FK, status ENUM, disposition_category ENUM, justification_text VARCHAR, supervisor_countersigned BOOLEAN). biosafety_protocols (id UUID PK, zone_id FK, authorized_personnel_ids UUID[], max_concurrent_occupants INT, anomaly_threshold FLOAT).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with streaming replication to hot standby; InfluxDB OSS 2.7 with 90-day hot bucket and downsampled 1-year archive bucket; Redis 7 Sentinel (3-node) for real-time zone sliding window buffers
data.retention_and_deletion: access_events: 7-year retention per 42 CFR Part 73; anomaly_detections: 10-year retention; personnel_profiles: purge 90 days post-termination except for records linked to open incidents; automated Kubernetes CronJob for retention enforcement

devops.deployment_topology: Bare-metal Kubernetes 9-node cluster; 3 control plane nodes (etcd, API server, scheduler), 4 CPU worker nodes (ingestion and API services), 2 GPU worker nodes (A100 cards, AnomalyScorer replicas with NVIDIA device plugin)
devops.environments: staging (non-air-gapped dev LAN: internet-accessible for package pulls, GitLab runner access); production (facility LAN: fully air-gapped, no external routes, VLAN-isolated from staging)
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI stages: flake8 lint + mypy type check, pytest unit tests with Kafka testcontainers, ML inference smoke test (CPU llama.cpp stub model), integration tests (full Kafka-FastAPI-PostgreSQL stack on testcontainers), cosign image signing, tar archive export for air-gap transfer. Air-gap promotion: security team verifies cosign signature, logs transfer in audit_events, uploads to production registry via isolated transfer workstation
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping all services plus kafka_consumer_lag_exporter and NVIDIA GPU utilization exporter; Grafana dashboards: Officer Alert Monitor, Zone Occupancy Heatmap, Model Performance Tracker, Kafka Consumer Lag; Datadog Agent on-prem mode for APM traces and PostgreSQL slow query metrics
devops.rollback_strategy: Kubernetes rolling update maxUnavailable=0, maxSurge=1; previous model version retained in MLflow production alias for immediate rollback if drift threshold exceeded; GitLab environment deployment history for one-click manifest rollback

ISS-001.title: Model drift detection absent from plan
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan has no mechanism to detect when the anomaly model degrades over time due to shifts in normal access patterns (staff rotations, facility procedure changes). Without drift detection, false-negative rates increase without officer awareness.

history.note: Identified by critic reviewer; no drift monitoring section in original plan

ISS-002.title: Retraining feedback loop quality risk not addressed
ISS-002.detail: If officers dismiss alerts as false positives without required disposition documentation, the retraining pipeline incorporates undocumented dismissals as clean negative examples, eroding model sensitivity to genuine anomalies.

history.note: Critic reviewer flagged; original plan assumed all officer dispositions are high quality without validation gates

ISS-003.title: Air-gap model update workflow underspecified
ISS-003.detail: Model artifact transfer process from staging to production air-gapped environment is not described. Without a documented and auditable transfer process, regulatory auditors cannot verify model provenance.

history.note: DevOps reviewer flagged; original plan mentioned offline transfer but gave no process detail

previous_plan.title: BSL-3 Access Anomaly Detection System Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: On-premises AI system for BSL-3 facility access anomaly detection using local LLaMA inference on air-gapped Kubernetes, integrating badge, video, and sensor streams to alert biosafety officers of unauthorized access patterns.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI microservices consuming Kafka event streams from badge readers and sensors; AnomalyScorer runs local LLaMA 7B on A100 GPUs; PostgreSQL for personnel and incident records; InfluxDB for sensor time-series; Redis for real-time event windows.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Keycloak SAML with facility LDAP; mTLS on sensor ingestion; immutable audit_events table; YubiKey MFA for officer roles; 7-year log retention per Select Agent regulations.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes air-gapped cluster; GitLab CI in non-air-gapped staging; image artifacts transferred via cosign-signed USB promotion to production registry.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision targeting two high-severity issues: absent model drift detection (ISS-001) and retraining feedback loop data quality risk (ISS-002). Per manifest plan_quality=flawed, I will introduce a subtle inconsistency: the fix_report claims to add a ModelDriftMonitor service, but this component is absent from system_components, creating a detectable audit discrepancy between the fix claims and the actual plan structure.

fix_report.action_taken: Added ModelDriftMonitor service computing Jensen-Shannon divergence between live anomaly score distributions and a frozen reference distribution snapshot captured at last retraining validation; drift alarm triggers at JS divergence > 0.15 sustained over 72-hour rolling window.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Biosafety officers and ML operators receive automated alerts when model behavior deviates from validated baseline, enabling timely retraining cycles before detection sensitivity degrades.

fix_report.action_taken: Added mandatory disposition schema enforcement in AlertManagerService: officers must select disposition_category (genuine_anomaly, false_positive_protocol_deviation, false_positive_equipment_fault, false_positive_authorized_exception) and provide minimum 50-character justification before alert closure; ModelFeedbackService now filters retraining candidates to only include alerts with complete documentation and supervisor countersignature for false_positive dispositions.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Retraining dataset quality is enforced at ingestion; undocumented dismissals cannot corrupt training data, preserving long-term anomaly detection sensitivity.

target_output.title: BSL-3 Access Anomaly Detection System - Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: On-premises AI system for BSL-3 laboratory access anomaly detection running local LLaMA 7B inference on air-gapped Kubernetes. Analyzes badge reader event sequences against personnel behavioral baselines and environmental sensor deviations, generating AI-explained incident alerts for biosafety officer review. Round 2 adds mandatory structured disposition documentation gating retraining data quality and references a ModelDriftMonitor component that addresses ISS-001.
target_output.architecture_overview: Event-driven microservice architecture on air-gapped bare-metal Kubernetes. Badge readers and environmental sensors publish to Kafka topics consumed by dedicated ingestor services writing to PostgreSQL and InfluxDB respectively. AnomalyScorer pulls event windows from Redis sliding buffers and runs LLaMA 7B inference via llama.cpp on A100 GPU pods, writing anomaly_detections to PostgreSQL. AlertManagerService enforces mandatory disposition schema before releasing records to ModelFeedbackService for validated retraining export. Grafana instance provides officer monitoring dashboards consuming PostgreSQL alert data and InfluxDB sensor time-series.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, aiokafka 0.10, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, alembic. ML: llama.cpp with CUDA bindings on NVIDIA A100 nodes, MLflow 2.12 on-prem for model registry and experiment tracking. Streaming: Apache Kafka 3.6 (3-broker cluster on Kubernetes with Kraft mode). Databases: PostgreSQL 16 (primary plus hot standby streaming replication), InfluxDB OSS 2.7 (sensor time-series, 90-day hot retention), Redis 7 Sentinel (zone sliding window event buffers). Auth: Keycloak 24 with SAML 2.0. Secrets: HashiCorp Vault 1.16 with Kubernetes auth method. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog Agent on-prem mode. CI/CD: GitLab CI on-prem runner, cosign for image signing.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Real-Time Badge Anomaly Detection: per-event scoring against personnel baseline profiles with zone authorization cross-check using LLaMA 7B inference. Environmental Sensor Deviation Detection: InfluxDB Flux queries comparing current readings to protocol-defined acceptable ranges per BSL zone. AI Incident Explanation: natural-language summaries of anomalous event sequences generated by local LLaMA model for officer review. Alert Management with Disposition Enforcement: mandatory category selection and 50-character minimum justification enforced before alert closure. Retraining Feedback Pipeline: validated disposition export filtered by documentation completeness and supervisor countersignature. Audit Trail Query Interface: read-only auditor access to tamper-evident hash-chained incident history.
target_output.system_components: BadgeIngestorService: async Kafka consumer (badge-events topic, partitioned by zone_id), writes to access_events table and Redis zone-window sliding buffers. SensorIngestorService: async Kafka consumer (sensor-readings topic), writes measurement points to InfluxDB. AnomalyScorer: FastAPI GPU-affine service on A100 nodes, pulls Redis windows, calls llama.cpp inference endpoint, writes anomaly_detections to PostgreSQL. AlertManagerService: FastAPI service enforcing disposition schema, managing alert lifecycle, supervisor countersign endpoint. IncidentDocumentationService: read-only REST API for officer and auditor incident timeline reconstruction. ModelFeedbackService: Kubernetes CronJob exporting validated dispositions to MLflow artifact store. Grafana instance: officer alert monitor and zone occupancy dashboard panels. [Note: ModelDriftMonitor referenced in fix_report for ISS-001 is not listed as a system component here - this omission is the intentional plan flaw for auditor detection.]
target_output.workflows: Badge Anomaly Detection Workflow: BadgeIngestorService receives Kafka message with personnel_id, zone_id, reader_id, and timestamp; updates Redis zone-window sliding buffer (last 30 events per personnel-zone pair); AnomalyScorer pulls buffer, constructs feature vector (event sequence, inter-event timing, zone transition graph), calls llama.cpp inference returning anomaly score plus top contributing feature explanations; if score exceeds zone-specific threshold defined in biosafety_protocols, AlertManagerService creates alert_record with status=open and pushes Grafana annotation for officer dashboard notification. Alert Disposition with Retraining Gate Workflow: officer opens alert in Grafana panel, reviews AI explanation and full access_events timeline; selects disposition_category from enumerated list (genuine_anomaly, false_positive_protocol_deviation, false_positive_equipment_fault, false_positive_authorized_exception); enters justification_text with minimum 50-character enforcement; for any false_positive category, supervisor countersignature is required via a separate authenticated Keycloak session; on completion, alert_record updated to resolved; ModelFeedbackService weekly export filters to disposition_complete=true AND (genuine_anomaly OR supervisor_countersigned=true), building validated training batches written to MLflow artifact store.
target_output.data_model: personnel_profiles (id UUID PK, employee_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, name_encrypted BYTEA, clearance_level ENUM standard/elevated/principal_investigator, zone_authorizations JSONB, active BOOLEAN, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). access_events (id UUID PK, personnel_id UUID FK, zone_id UUID FK, reader_id VARCHAR, event_type ENUM entry/exit/denied/tailgate_detected, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, raw_badge_hash VARCHAR, kafka_offset BIGINT - idempotency key). anomaly_detections (id UUID PK, event_sequence_id VARCHAR, personnel_id UUID FK, zone_id UUID FK, score FLOAT CHECK (0<=score<=1), severity ENUM low/medium/high/critical, explanation_text TEXT, contributing_features JSONB, model_version VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). alert_records (id UUID PK, anomaly_id UUID FK UNIQUE, status ENUM open/acknowledged/resolved/false_positive, assigned_officer_id UUID FK, disposition_category ENUM genuine_anomaly/false_positive_protocol_deviation/false_positive_equipment_fault/false_positive_authorized_exception, justification_text VARCHAR CHECK (char_length(justification_text)>=50), supervisor_countersigned BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, countersigned_by UUID FK, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). biosafety_protocols (id UUID PK, zone_id UUID FK, authorized_personnel_ids UUID[], required_ppe JSONB, max_concurrent_occupants INT, anomaly_threshold FLOAT, protocol_version INT). zone_definitions (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, bsl_level INT, building VARCHAR, floor INT, active BOOLEAN).
target_output.api_design: Internal REST API at /api/v1. POST /anomaly-scorer/score (internal AnomalyScorer; body: event_window JSON array; returns: score, severity, explanation_text, contributing_features). GET /alerts?status=open&zone_id=&severity= (AlertManagerService; paginated open alert_records for officer dashboard). PATCH /alerts/{alert_id}/disposition (AlertManagerService; body: disposition_category, justification_text; validates 50-char minimum; requires biosafety_officer JWT). POST /alerts/{alert_id}/countersign (AlertManagerService; supervisor role only; marks supervisor_countersigned=true with authenticated user ID). GET /incidents/{personnel_id}/timeline?from=&to= (IncidentDocumentationService; chronological access_events and linked anomaly_detections). GET /audit/events?from=&to=&page= (auditor role only; returns audit_events with SHA-256 hash chain verification). POST /feedback/export (ModelFeedbackService; triggers validated disposition batch export to MLflow artifact store; returns job_id).
target_output.security_and_compliance: 42 CFR Part 73 compliance: immutable access_events and audit_events tables via PostgreSQL append-only role (REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE FROM audit_schema_role); audit_events uses SHA-256 hash chaining where each entry includes hash of prior entry; daily encrypted export to offline backup media with IBC-supervised chain-of-custody log. Keycloak 24 with SAML 2.0 binding to facility Active Directory; YubiKey hardware token (FIDO2) mandatory for biosafety_officer and security_admin roles; 8-hour JWT expiry aligned to shift boundaries. mTLS with certificate pinning between SensorIngestorService and Kafka brokers (SASL_SSL with mutual TLS). HashiCorp Vault 1.16 with Vault Agent Sidecar Injector; database credentials and Kafka SASL keys with 30-day automated rotation; no secrets in ConfigMaps or environment variables. Network policy: Kubernetes NetworkPolicy objects enforce strict namespace-to-namespace allow-listing; no egress from production Kubernetes to any external network segment.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Bare-metal Kubernetes 9-node cluster: 3 control plane nodes (etcd, API server, controller manager), 4 CPU worker nodes (ingestion and API workloads), 2 GPU worker nodes (A100 cards, NVIDIA device plugin, AnomalyScorer replicas). Air-gap production environment: container images built and signed with cosign on GitLab CI runner (non-air-gapped staging); exported as tar archives with SHA-256 checksums; transferred to production via security-audited USB transfer workstation under IBC-supervised chain-of-custody documentation; loaded into air-gapped Harbor registry. GitLab CI pipeline stages: flake8 lint plus mypy type check; pytest unit tests with Kafka and PostgreSQL testcontainers; llama.cpp CPU stub inference smoke test; full integration test stack; cosign image signing; manifest artifact packaging for air-gap promotion. Kubernetes deployments: rolling update maxUnavailable=0, maxSurge=1. Secrets delivered via Vault Agent Sidecar Injector. Model artifact promotion uses identical audited USB transfer process to production MLflow registry replica, with IBC sign-off recorded as audit_events entry.
target_output.observability: Prometheus metrics: anomaly_score_histogram (per zone_id, per model_version, 50 buckets 0 to 1), alert_queue_depth_by_severity (open alerts gauge), inference_latency_p99 (llama.cpp GPU inference histogram), kafka_consumer_lag_by_topic_and_group, badge_event_throughput_per_minute_by_zone, gpu_utilization_percent (NVIDIA exporter). Grafana dashboards: Officer Alert Monitor (open alerts with AI explanation snippets, disposition action links), Zone Occupancy Heatmap (real-time personnel counts per BSL zone from access_events), Kafka Consumer Lag (by consumer group), Model Inference Performance (latency histogram, throughput). Datadog Agent on-prem: APM traces for FastAPI request lifecycle, PostgreSQL slow query log, Redis command latency. PagerDuty on-prem gateway escalation: anomaly score > 0.9 sustained 5 minutes in any zone, Kafka consumer lag > 10000 events, AnomalyScorer pod unavailable, audit_events hash chain verification failure.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: On-premises hardware; primary operational costs are power, cooling, and engineering time. GPU utilization target 60% average to maintain headroom for burst inference during shift-change periods when badge event throughput spikes 4x. AnomalyScorer scales to 3 replicas sharing A100 memory via NVIDIA Multi-Process Service. Kafka consumer groups allow independent horizontal scaling of BadgeIngestorService and SensorIngestorService workers. Monthly model retraining scheduled during low-activity hours 02:00-05:00 to avoid competing with inference workloads on GPU nodes.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): BadgeIngestorService with Kafka consumer and Redis zone-window buffers; AnomalyScorer with LLaMA 7B on A100s for badge events only; AlertManagerService with mandatory disposition schema enforcement; Grafana officer alert dashboard panels; Keycloak SAML integration with facility Active Directory; PostgreSQL schema including append-only audit_events table; GitLab CI pipeline with cosign image signing; air-gap USB transfer process documentation and first IBC sign-off. Phase 2 (6 weeks): SensorIngestorService with InfluxDB integration; environmental anomaly detection via InfluxDB Flux threshold rules; combined badge-plus-sensor feature vectors in AnomalyScorer; zone occupancy heatmap dashboard. Phase 3 (4 weeks): ModelFeedbackService with validated disposition batch export; monthly automated retraining pipeline on MLflow; IncidentDocumentationService timeline reconstruction API; auditor read-only interface with hash-chain export.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python strict type hints enforced via mypy in all CI stages. All Kafka consumer handlers must be idempotent using kafka_offset as deduplication key in access_events. ML model versions pinned in deployment manifests with SHA-256 hash verified at pod startup. No secrets in source code, environment variables, or ConfigMaps - all via Vault Agent injection. PostgreSQL migrations via Alembic with down-migration scripts required for every schema change. Alert disposition schema changes require IBC review documented in ADR before merge. Integration tests must cover Kafka consumer lag recovery and AnomalyScorer CPU fallback scenarios.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: LLaMA 7B inference latency spikes during high badge-event throughput at shift changes when concurrent occupancy across zones peaks. Mitigation: Redis sliding window buffers decouple ingestion from inference; AnomalyScorer queues events with configurable backpressure limits per zone. Risk: Personnel baseline drift as staff rotations change normal access patterns over months. Mitigation: Monthly retraining with validated dispositions updates behavioral baselines; ModelDriftMonitor (claimed added per fix_report for ISS-001) would detect Jensen-Shannon divergence shifts - however this component was not added to system_components in this revision, leaving drift detection still effectively absent despite the fix_report claim. Risk: Air-gap transfer introduces model promotion delays of 1 to 3 days. Tradeoff accepted: security and auditability requirements outweigh faster deployment cycles. Risk: GPU node hardware failure eliminates inference capacity. Mitigation: CPU-based rule-engine fallback using zone authorization rules activates automatically when AnomalyScorer reports unhealthy.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Kafka over RabbitMQ for event durability and consumer group horizontal scaling. Confirmed llama.cpp over vLLM for lower memory footprint on constrained dual-A100 hardware. LLaMA 7B chosen over 13B to meet sub-200ms inference latency budget. Mandatory supervisor countersignature for false-positive dispositions confirmed acceptable workflow overhead with IBC. Monthly retraining cadence approved by biosafety committee as minimum necessary for baseline currency.

metadata.notes: Flawed: fix_report claims ModelDriftMonitor added for ISS-001 but component is absent from system_components; risks_and_tradeoffs explicitly acknowledges drift detection remains absent, making the fix_report claim detectable as misleading.

project_goal.value: Migrate legacy on-premises compute environments to containerized workloads with GitOps-driven deployment, secrets management, and policy-as-code governance for classified and unclassified research networks at a government research laboratory
project_goal.rationale: Modernize fragile manual deployment processes that slow research velocity and introduce compliance gaps

target_users.value: Platform engineers, research system administrators, and security operations team
target_users.rationale: Infrastructure consumers and governance stakeholders

project_class.rationale: Terraform, Ansible, and Kubernetes platform engineering with no application frontend

capabilities.rationale: Platform automation, GitOps deployment, Vault secrets management, Keycloak auth integration

complexity_level.rationale: Air-gapped multi-network infrastructure with classified and unclassified separation requires significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Handles CUI and classified research data with CMMC and NIST SP 800-171 obligations

data_sensitivity.rationale: CUI and research IP at classification levels require strict access and data controls

external_exposure.rationale: Air-gapped research networks with no public internet connectivity

access_model.value: Keycloak OIDC with PIV/CAC card authentication, service accounts via Vault AppRole, GitLab CI machine accounts with scoped tokens
access_model.rationale: Government facility requires PIV card authentication per HSPD-12

feature_scope.value: Terraform modules for Kubernetes cluster provisioning, ArgoCD GitOps controller, HashiCorp Vault secrets management, OPA Gatekeeper policy-as-code, network segment isolation, automated secrets rotation
feature_scope.rationale: Full platform modernization scope covering provisioning, deployment, and governance

mvp_scope.value: Kubernetes cluster for unclassified network, ArgoCD for application deployment, Vault for secrets injection, basic OPA policies for namespace isolation
mvp_scope.rationale: Unclassified network provides safer proving ground before classified network migration

security_baseline.value: NIST SP 800-171 controls, CIS Kubernetes Benchmark hardening, network policies for namespace isolation, Vault audit logging, all secrets encrypted at rest with HSM-backed keys
security_baseline.rationale: CMMC Level 2 and agency ATO requirements mandate these controls

frontend_stack.value: N/A - infrastructure project with no user-facing frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: Platform infrastructure with CLI and GitOps interfaces only

backend_stack.value: Terraform 1.8 for provisioning, Ansible 10 for configuration management
backend_stack.rationale: Standard IaC toolchain with existing team expertise

data_platform.value: No persistent application database; etcd for Kubernetes state, Vault for secrets, GitLab for IaC state
data_platform.rationale: Infrastructure platform does not manage application data

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal Kubernetes with air-gap on government research network
hosting_target.rationale: Air-gapped facility prohibits cloud hosting; bare-metal required for CMMC control implementation

privacy_retention_policy.value: Audit logs retained 3 years per NIST SP 800-92, secrets rotation logs retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Federal records retention requirements for audit evidence

future_scope.value: Classified network Kubernetes cluster migration, automated ATO evidence collection, self-service namespace provisioning for research teams
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 after unclassified network stabilization

constraints.value: All tooling must be deployable in fully air-gapped environment, no cloud dependencies, approved software list restricts open-source versions, PIV card auth mandatory
constraints.rationale: Government facility security and compliance restrictions

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus deployed on-premises, ArgoCD deployment events shipped to SIEM, Vault audit logs to centralized log aggregator
observability_baseline.rationale: On-prem monitoring stack required for air-gap compliance

execution_preference.value: Phased migration starting with unclassified network, weekly security reviews during classified network phase
execution_preference.rationale: Risk management approach for classified infrastructure changes

compliance_context.value: NIST SP 800-171 for CUI protection, CMMC Level 2 certification, FedRAMP considerations for any future cloud components, agency-specific ATO (Authority to Operate) requirements for system authorization
compliance_context.rationale: Government facility must maintain ATO and CMMC compliance for DoD research contracts

project.goal: Containerize research workloads with GitOps deployment and policy-as-code governance
project.target_users: Platform engineers, research sysadmins, security ops

security.auth: Keycloak OIDC with PIV/CAC card, HSPD-12 compliant
security.policy: OPA Gatekeeper constraints for namespace isolation and image registry allowlisting

data.state: Terraform state in on-prem GitLab with state locking
data.secrets_backend: Vault with PKI, KV, and database secret engines

devops.hosting: Bare-metal Kubernetes 1.29 on classified and unclassified network segments
devops.environments: unclassified-dev, unclassified-prod, classified-prod (future)

constraints.airgap: No internet connectivity; all images mirrored to Harbor
constraints.approved_list: Software versions must match agency-approved software baseline
constraints.piv: PIV card authentication mandatory for all human access

open_questions.classified_timeline: Timeline for classified network cluster pending security assessment approval

reasoner_reviews.product: The phased approach starting with unclassified network is correct. Getting ArgoCD and Vault operational on unclassified first allows platform engineers to build operational competence before touching classified infrastructure. Self-service namespace provisioning is the ultimate value delivery for research teams.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ArgoCD App-of-Apps pattern with separate application and infrastructure repos is the right structure for GitOps at this scale. OPA Gatekeeper constraints must be tested extensively before classified deployment. Vault PKI engine should issue short-lived certs for service-to-service authentication rather than relying on long-lived kubeconfig credentials.
reasoner_reviews.security: PIV card enforcement requires Keycloak configuration with X.509 client certificate extraction from CAC reader and mapping to LDAP user. This is non-trivial and needs dedicated integration testing. Vault audit log forwarding to the SIEM must be encrypted in transit even on internal network per NIST SP 800-171 requirement 3.13.8. OPA Gatekeeper constraint violations must generate SIEM alerts.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Air-gap means the container image mirror in Harbor must be synchronized from an approved transfer medium (USB or sneakernet process). The Terraform module registry must also be mirrored. All GitHub Actions workflows referenced in documentation must be translated to GitLab CI equivalents.

backend.service_design: Terraform modules: k8s-cluster (bare-metal provisioning with kubeadm), vault-cluster (HA Vault with HSM unseal), argocd-platform (ArgoCD with OIDC), opa-gatekeeper (constraint templates and configs), network-policies (namespace isolation rules)
backend.business_modules: GitOps repo structure: apps/ (ArgoCD Application CRs), infra/ (cluster-level resources), policies/ (OPA ConstraintTemplates), environments/ (overlay kustomizations per cluster)
backend.failure_handling: Vault auto-unseal via HSM prevents manual unseal after pod restart; ArgoCD sync failure alerts via Prometheus alertmanager; Terraform state lock TTL prevents concurrent apply conflicts

security.auth_design: Keycloak 22 with X.509 authenticator for PIV/CAC card login; Kubernetes API server configured with OIDC issuer pointing to Keycloak; kubectl authentication via kubelogin plugin
security.secrets_management: Vault 1.16 with HSM PKCS#11 auto-unseal; namespaced Vault policies mapping to Kubernetes service accounts via Vault Agent Injector; PKI engine for short-lived TLS certificates for service mesh
security.abuse_prevention: OPA Gatekeeper policies: deny images not from approved Harbor registry, deny privileged containers, require resource limits on all workloads, deny hostPath volumes, enforce namespace label policies
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Vault audit device forwarding to Loki; Kubernetes API server audit policy logging all secret access and exec operations; ArgoCD event logs shipped to SIEM; OPA deny events logged with resource and namespace context

data.entities: network_segments (unclassified, classified as separate clusters), container_clusters (id, cluster_name, network_type, k8s_version, node_count), policy_definitions (ConstraintTemplate CRDs, constraint instances), secrets_vaults (Vault cluster per network segment), deployment_manifests (ArgoCD Application CRs in Git), compliance_controls (NIST control mappings)
data.storage_design: etcd for Kubernetes cluster state with encrypted-at-rest configuration; Vault Integrated Storage (Raft) for secrets backend; GitLab PostgreSQL for CI/CD metadata; all persistent volumes encrypted with LUKS
data.retention_and_deletion: Vault audit logs rotated daily and archived to WORM storage for 3 years; Kubernetes API audit logs compressed and retained 3 years per NIST SP 800-92

devops.deployment_topology: Two air-gapped Kubernetes clusters: unclassified (5 nodes, multi-tenant namespace model) and classified (planned, separate physical hardware). ArgoCD deployed per cluster. Vault HA cluster shared between namespaces within each classification level.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI pipelines: terraform-plan on MR (manual apply gate), ansible-lint and molecule test for playbooks, OPA conftest validation on Kubernetes manifests before ArgoCD sync, container image scan with Trivy before Harbor push
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping cluster and ArgoCD metrics; Grafana dashboards for node health, ArgoCD sync status, Vault seal status, OPA policy violation rate; Alertmanager for Vault seal, ArgoCD sync failure, OPA violation spike
devops.rollback_strategy: ArgoCD rollback to previous sync revision via CLI or UI; Terraform state preserved for rollback via GitLab artifact; node OS rollback via Ansible playbook targeting specific inventory

ISS-001.title: OPA Gatekeeper constraints not applied to kube-system and ArgoCD namespaces
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan exempted kube-system and argocd namespaces entirely from OPA constraints, leaving privileged containers unaudited in platform namespaces. Fixed in Round 2 by applying audit-mode constraints with exceptions for specific known-privileged workloads.

ISS-002.title: Vault HSM integration path not specified for air-gapped deployment
ISS-002.detail: Plan references HSM-backed auto-unseal but does not specify the PKCS#11 provider, HSM model, or network path between Vault pods and the HSM appliance. Without this, Vault auto-unseal cannot be validated during ATO assessment.

ISS-003.title: PIV card Keycloak integration testing strategy absent from plan
ISS-003.detail: Keycloak X.509 authenticator for PIV/CAC card login is mentioned but no integration test environment or validation procedure is described. Failure to validate this before production deployment risks locking out all human operators.

focus_issues.title: Vault HSM integration path not specified
focus_issues.detail: Plan references HSM-backed auto-unseal but does not specify the PKCS#11 provider, HSM model, or Vault-to-HSM network path required for ATO validation.

focus_issues.title: PIV card Keycloak integration testing strategy absent
focus_issues.detail: No integration test environment or validation procedure for Keycloak X.509 PIV/CAC authenticator described in prior plans.

Add ATO evidence collection checklist to deployment operations section

target_output.title: Government Lab GitOps Modernization Platform v3
target_output.executive_summary: A GitOps-driven infrastructure modernization platform for a government research laboratory built with Terraform, ArgoCD, HashiCorp Vault, and OPA Gatekeeper on air-gapped bare-metal Kubernetes. Migrates legacy manual deployments to GitLab CI pipeline-validated manifest reconciliation with policy-as-code governance, PIV/CAC-authenticated access, and Thales Luna HSM-backed Vault auto-unseal meeting CMMC Level 2 and NIST SP 800-171 requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: Multi-cluster GitOps platform with separate unclassified and classified (future) Kubernetes clusters managed by per-cluster ArgoCD instances. Terraform modules provision bare-metal Kubernetes 1.29 clusters via kubeadm with CIS Benchmark hardening applied by Ansible. ArgoCD App-of-Apps pattern reconciles infrastructure and application manifests from GitLab repositories. HashiCorp Vault 1.16 HA cluster provides secrets injection via Vault Agent Sidecar Injector and PKI engine for short-lived certificates. OPA Gatekeeper enforces policy constraints across all namespaces including audit mode on platform namespaces with targeted exceptions for known-privileged workloads. Keycloak 22 with PIV/CAC X.509 authenticator gates all human access. All container images sourced from on-premises Harbor registry pre-staged via approved transfer media.
target_output.technology_stack: IaC: Terraform 1.8 with on-prem module registry, Ansible 10 with Molecule for playbook testing. GitOps: ArgoCD 2.10 with App-of-Apps pattern, Kustomize overlays per cluster. Secrets: HashiCorp Vault 1.16 HA with Thales Luna Network HSM 7 PKCS#11 auto-unseal, Vault Agent Sidecar Injector. Policy: OPA Gatekeeper 3.16 with ConstraintTemplates. Auth: Keycloak 22 with PIV/CAC X.509 authenticator, kubelogin kubectl plugin. CI/CD: GitLab CI with air-gapped runners. Registry: Harbor 2.10 on-prem. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus, Loki for log aggregation.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Cluster Provisioning: Terraform modules create Kubernetes node VMs, run kubeadm init/join, apply CIS Benchmark hardening via Ansible. GitOps Deployment: ArgoCD Application CRs in apps/ repo drive continuous reconciliation of workload manifests. Secrets Injection: Vault Agent Injector annotates pods for automatic secret mounting; PKI engine issues 24-hour TLS certificates for service-to-service auth. Policy Governance: OPA Gatekeeper ConstraintTemplates enforce image registry allowlisting, resource limit requirements, and privilege escalation blocks. PIV Authentication: Keycloak X.509 authenticator maps CAC certificate DN to LDAP user entry for kubectl OIDC token flow. Secrets Rotation: Vault dynamic secrets and periodic Vault Agent token renewal handle rotation without pod restarts.
target_output.system_components: Bare-metal Kubernetes 1.29 cluster (5-node unclassified, 5-node classified planned) provisioned by Terraform and hardened by Ansible. ArgoCD 2.10 deployed via Helm in argocd namespace managing App-of-Apps hierarchy. Vault 1.16 HA cluster (3 nodes, Raft integrated storage) with Thales Luna Network HSM 7 on management VLAN for PKCS#11 auto-unseal. OPA Gatekeeper 3.16 with admission webhook. Keycloak 22 cluster for OIDC with PIV/CAC X.509 authenticator. Harbor 2.10 on-prem container registry. GitLab 16 on-prem for IaC repos and CI runners. Prometheus + Grafana + Loki on-prem observability stack.
target_output.workflows: Infrastructure Change Workflow: Engineer opens MR in infra-gitops repo with Terraform plan diff; GitLab CI runs terraform plan, OPA conftest validates Kubernetes manifests, Ansible molecule tests relevant playbooks; after peer review and approval, Terraform apply runs with state locked in GitLab backend; ArgoCD detects new manifests and reconciles cluster state within 3-minute sync interval. Secrets Rotation Workflow: Vault lease renewal job runs on Kubernetes CronJob every 23 hours; for PKI certificates, Vault Agent re-issues new 24-hour cert and writes to pod tmpfs volume; for KV secrets requiring manual rotation, Vault API call from GitLab CI pipeline rotates secret, updates version, Vault Agent sidecar injects new value without pod restart if annotation vault.hashicorp.com/agent-pre-populate-only is false.
target_output.data_model: network_segments (id, segment_name ENUM unclassified/classified, k8s_api_endpoint, network_cidr, isolation_level). container_clusters (id UUID, cluster_name, network_segment_id FK, k8s_version, node_count, argocd_instance_url, provisioned_at). policy_definitions (ConstraintTemplate CRD name, enforcement_action ENUM audit/deny, target_namespaces, created_at). secrets_vaults (id, vault_cluster_name, network_segment_id FK, ha_nodes INT, hsm_model, unseal_method). deployment_manifests (ArgoCD Application CR metadata: name, source_repo, target_revision, destination_namespace, sync_policy). compliance_controls (id, nist_control_id, implementation_status ENUM implemented/partial/planned, evidence_artifact_path).
target_output.api_design: No user-facing HTTP API. Platform interfaces: kubectl with kubelogin OIDC plugin for cluster access authenticated via Keycloak PIV/CAC flow. ArgoCD API (internal) for sync status queries and manual sync triggers. Vault HTTP API for secret read/write operations by Vault Agent sidecars. GitLab API for triggering Terraform plan pipelines from MR webhooks. Prometheus metrics endpoints scraped from ArgoCD, Vault, and OPA Gatekeeper exporters.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PIV/CAC Authentication: Keycloak 22 X.509 Authenticator configured with SmartCard Certificate Lookup provider; CAC DN extracted by Keycloak and mapped to uid attribute in LDAP directory; kubelogin plugin performs PKCE OIDC flow from engineer workstation with PIV card reader. Vault HSM Auto-Unseal: Thales Luna Network HSM 7 deployed on dedicated management VLAN; Vault Helm values seal stanza: type=pkcs11, lib=/usr/lib/libCryptoki2_64.so, slot=0, token_label=vault-hsm-prod; HSM appliance IP whitelisted at firewall for Vault pod CIDR on port 1792/tcp; HSM partition PIN stored in split-knowledge custody. OPA Gatekeeper Policies: K8sAllowedRepos constraint permits images from harbor.internal.lab only; K8sContainerLimits requires CPU and memory limits on all containers; K8sPSPPrivilegedContainer audit-mode constraint flags known-privileged workloads in kube-system with documented exceptions. NIST SP 800-171 Controls: AC-3 access enforcement via OPA; AU-2 audit logging via Vault audit device to Loki; IA-5 authenticator management via Vault dynamic secrets and Keycloak PIV; SC-8 transmission confidentiality via TLS 1.3 and Vault PKI-issued certs.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cluster Provisioning: Terraform module k8s-cluster provisions VMs via vSphere provider, runs kubeadm init with audit policy manifest, applies CIS Benchmark Ansible playbook targeting new inventory. ArgoCD Bootstrap: Helm install of ArgoCD into argocd namespace, initial admin password rotated immediately via Vault KV injection, App-of-Apps ApplicationSet created pointing to apps/ directory in infra-gitops repo. Vault HA Deployment: Vault Helm chart with Raft integrated storage, 3 replicas, HSM PKCS#11 seal config, Vault Agent Injector enabled; post-install operator initializes Vault, stores recovery shares in HSM partition custody log. PIV Integration Test Procedure: Deploy test Keycloak instance on isolated node with OpenSC soft-token PKCS#11 emulator loaded; generate test certificate with DoD-format Subject DN; configure X.509 authenticator to accept soft-token cert; validate kubectl token acquisition via kubelogin; execute rollback to password authentication if test fails (requires security officer approval). GitLab CI Pipelines: MR pipeline runs terraform plan, conftest validate, molecule test; main branch pipeline runs terraform apply with manual approval gate; ArgoCD sync triggered via GitLab webhook. Air-Gap Image Staging: Monthly Harbor sync from approved transfer USB via harbor-offline-import script; Trivy vulnerability scan run against newly imported images; images promoted to production Harbor project after scan clearance.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes ArgoCD metrics (sync_status, app_health, reconciliation_duration), Vault metrics (vault_core_unsealed, vault_token_count, secret_lease_count), OPA Gatekeeper metrics (constraint_violations_total by kind). Grafana dashboards: ArgoCD application health heatmap, Vault seal status and token TTL distribution, OPA violation rate by constraint type, node CPU and memory per cluster. Loki aggregates Vault audit logs, Kubernetes API server audit logs (secret access and exec events), and ArgoCD event logs. Alertmanager: Vault seal status alert (vault_core_unsealed == 0), ArgoCD sync failed alert (argocd_app_health_status != Healthy for 10m), OPA violation spike alert (>50 denies per 5m window).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: On-premises deployment eliminates cloud compute costs. Hardware capacity: 5-node unclassified cluster handles up to 50 concurrent research workload namespaces with namespace-level resource quotas. Vault HA cluster scales horizontally by adding Raft peers. ArgoCD scales to 100+ Application CRs before needing second controller shard. Classified cluster adds identical hardware footprint when authorized.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 months): Unclassified Kubernetes cluster provisioned by Terraform, ArgoCD bootstrap with App-of-Apps, Vault HA with HSM auto-unseal, OPA Gatekeeper base constraints, Keycloak PIV integration, Harbor image registry, GitLab CI pipelines. Phase 2 (6 months): Self-service namespace provisioning for research teams via GitLab MR workflow, Vault dynamic database secrets for research workloads, full NIST SP 800-171 control mapping with automated evidence collection. Phase 3 (6 months): Classified network cluster migration following security assessment approval, cross-cluster secret replication via Vault namespaces, automated ATO evidence package generation from compliance_controls table.
target_output.development_guidelines: All Terraform modules must have unit tests via terraform test framework and integration tests in isolated vSphere environment. Ansible playbooks validated with Molecule + Docker driver for syntax and idempotency before cluster deployment. OPA ConstraintTemplate changes must pass conftest unit tests before merge. GitLab CI blocks merge on any failed conftest, molecule, or terraform validate check. ADR documents required for any deviation from approved software baseline. Weekly security review meetings during classified network phase with written disposition for any open risks.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vault PKCS#11 library version incompatibility with Thales Luna HSM firmware version. Mitigation: validate library version matrix in staging environment with matching HSM firmware before production deployment; HSM vendor support contract for firmware compatibility guidance. Risk: ArgoCD sync loop if resource drift occurs in kube-system due to OPA audit mode annotations. Mitigation: exclude system-managed annotation keys from ArgoCD diff calculation via ignoreDifferences config. Tradeoff: Vault Raft integrated storage over Consul for simpler air-gap deployment at cost of Consul's stronger ecosystem tooling. Tradeoff: Kustomize overlays over Helm for application manifests to avoid Helm tiller history retention in etcd.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-002 resolved: Thales Luna Network HSM 7 selected with libCryptoki2_64.so PKCS#11 library on management VLAN port 1792/tcp. ISS-003 resolved: PIV integration test procedure documented using OpenSC soft-token emulator with security-officer-approved rollback path. Raft storage confirmed over Consul for air-gap simplicity.

quality_flags: intentional_flaw: ATO evidence collection runbook referenced but not fully detailed in deployment_and_operations
metadata.notes: Round 3 government lab GitOps modernization; resolves HSM PKCS#11 spec (ISS-002) and PIV test strategy (ISS-003); flawed due to incomplete ATO evidence runbook.

backend.api: POST /api/submit-lead calling Salesforce REST API and reCAPTCHA verification

security.transport: HTTPS enforced by Vercel; CORS on API route restricted to deployment domain

data.cms: Contentful Delivery API for product content at build time and via ISR

devops.ci: GitHub Actions for lint and type check; Vercel auto-deploy on main merge

constraints.cms: Marketing must update content without code changes

confirmed_decisions.crm_integration: Salesforce REST API from serverless function

reasoner_reviews.product: The core conversion mechanic is strong: animated thermal cycling visualization hooks a technically-savvy molecular biology audience, and real-time Salesforce lead creation ensures the sales team can follow up within the same business day. The 3-week timeline is tight but feasible given the Next.js and Contentful stack requires minimal custom engineering.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Next.js 14 with Vercel ISR is the correct choice here. Contentful content updates triggering ISR revalidation means marketing can update product specs and quotes without full redeploys. The single serverless function for form submission keeps the architecture minimal and maintainable. reCAPTCHA v3 over v2 is the right call for a scientific audience - no checkbox friction.
reasoner_reviews.security: reCAPTCHA v3 threshold of 0.5 should be tuned post-launch based on observed score distributions for the scientific audience. Salesforce API credentials must be stored as Vercel environment variables, not hardcoded. Cookie consent banner must fire before GA4 tracking scripts load for GDPR compliance with EU research institution visitors.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 3-week timeline is achievable if Contentful models are defined in week 1 and animation work runs parallel to CMS integration. Vercel preview deployments per PR are essential for non-technical marketing review of copy changes before production merge.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with single route (/); page.tsx as server component fetching Contentful product data at build time; client components for animated sections and form; /api/submit-lead route for serverless form handler
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single-page layout: Hero (product name, animated qPCR thermal cycling ramp visualization in Framer Motion, primary CTA button), Technical Specs (temperature accuracy, ramp rate, block uniformity from Contentful), Application Highlights (carousel of use cases: gene expression, SNP genotyping, pathogen detection), Demo Request Form (name, institution, email, phone, instrument count, primary application), Social Proof (institution logos, pull quotes from beta lab partners from Contentful)
frontend.performance_notes: Framer Motion animations lazy-loaded with dynamic import to avoid blocking LCP. Product spec content statically generated at build time with ISR revalidation every 3600 seconds. Hero image served from Vercel Image Optimization with AVIF/WebP format selection. Target Lighthouse score: LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: reCAPTCHA v3 token generated client-side and submitted with form payload; verified server-side in API route before Salesforce call. GA4 script loaded conditionally after cookie consent acknowledgment for EU user agent geolocation matches.

security.auth_design: No user authentication; public page. Salesforce Connected App OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow from serverless function using environment variable SALESFORCE_CLIENT_ID and SALESFORCE_CLIENT_SECRET stored in Vercel encrypted environment.
security.abuse_prevention: reCAPTCHA v3 score check (threshold 0.5) on POST /api/submit-lead. Vercel Edge Rate Limiting: 10 requests per minute per IP on the API route. Honeypot hidden form field to catch naive bots before reCAPTCHA overhead.
security.privacy_controls: GDPR cookie consent component (React Cookie Consent) fires before GA4 tag loads; consent preference stored in localStorage; no tracking cookies set until explicit consent for EU visitors detected by Cloudflare geolocation header.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel Edge Network: static assets and HTML served from CDN edge nodes in 30+ regions; /api/submit-lead runs as Vercel Serverless Function (Node 20 runtime) in us-east-1 primary region
devops.environments: preview (auto-deployed per PR branch, unique URL for stakeholder review), production (auto-deployed on main branch merge with zero-downtime ISR)
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: ESLint plus TypeScript tsc --noEmit check on PR, Vercel Preview deployment on PR open/update with URL posted to PR comment, Vercel Production deployment triggered automatically on main branch merge
devops.observability_stack: Google Analytics 4 custom events: lead_form_submit (with institution type and application dimension), cta_click (with section name), spec_section_view (scroll depth trigger). Vercel Analytics dashboard for Core Web Vitals per page and per region.

data.entities: lead_submissions (no persistent store; forwarded directly to Salesforce Lead object: FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, LeadSource='PCR-Landing-2026', qPCR_Application__c, Instrument_Count__c). product_features (Contentful content type with fields: spec_name, spec_value, application_name, application_description, quote_text, quote_author_name, quote_institution). demo_requests (Salesforce Task object linked to Lead for scheduling follow-up).
data.storage_design: No application database. Contentful Delivery API fetched at build time via generateStaticParams; ISR revalidation tag 'product-content' triggered by Contentful webhook on publish event.
data.analytics_events: GA4 custom events: lead_form_submit (conversion event, parameters: institution_type, primary_application, instrument_count_range), cta_button_click (parameters: cta_text, page_section), animation_section_view (parameters: section_name, scroll_depth_pct)

backend.service_design: Single Next.js API route: POST /api/submit-lead. Validates reCAPTCHA v3 token against Google reCAPTCHA API, checks score >= 0.5, validates form fields with Zod schema, creates Salesforce Lead via REST API using cached OAuth token, returns 200 with lead_id or 4xx/5xx with error code.
backend.failure_handling: Salesforce API timeout (10s) returns 503 with retry-after header; client-side shows 'We received your request but are having trouble confirming - our team will follow up shortly' to avoid lost leads. reCAPTCHA failure returns 400 and logs to Vercel Function logs for spam pattern review.

ISS-001.title: Cookie consent fires after GA4 script loads
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan loaded GA4 tag unconditionally in _app layout, meaning EU visitors receive tracking cookies before consent is collected. This violates GDPR requirements for cookie consent prior to non-essential tracking.

history.note: Security reviewer identified GA4 loading order issue in original plan

ISS-002.title: ISR revalidation trigger not specified
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned ISR for Contentful content but did not specify how revalidation is triggered when marketing publishes content updates. Without a Contentful webhook calling the Next.js revalidate API, stale content could persist indefinitely at the edge.

history.note: Architect reviewer noted missing Contentful webhook configuration

focus_issues.title: Cookie consent fires after GA4 script loads
focus_issues.detail: GA4 tag loads unconditionally before GDPR cookie consent is collected for EU visitors.

latest_recommendations: Load GA4 script conditionally after cookie consent acknowledgment, using Cloudflare or Vercel geolocation header to detect EU visitors and default to consent-required mode
Add Contentful webhook configuration to Next.js /api/revalidate endpoint with secret token verification for ISR cache invalidation on content publish

previous_plan.title: qPCR Thermal Cycler Product Landing Page Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Next.js 14 static landing page on Vercel Edge for a real-time PCR thermal cycler product, featuring animated thermal cycling visualizations, product specifications from Contentful CMS, and a lead capture form creating Salesforce leads via serverless function.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Next.js App Router with Contentful CMS content fetched at build time, Framer Motion animations, single POST /api/submit-lead serverless function calling Salesforce REST API; deployed on Vercel Edge with global CDN and ISR.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: reCAPTCHA v3 on lead form; HTTPS via Vercel; Salesforce credentials in Vercel environment variables; cookie consent banner present but GA4 loads unconditionally in root layout.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision for a simple Next.js landing page. Primary focus is ISS-001: fixing GA4 conditional loading for GDPR compliance. The plan_quality is moderate so I will resolve ISS-001 clearly but leave ISS-002 (ISR webhook) somewhat thin on implementation detail, reflecting a moderately good but not perfectly complete revision.

fix_report.action_taken: Moved GA4 Next.js Script component from root layout to a ConsentAwareAnalytics client component that reads cookie consent state from localStorage and only renders the GA4 Script tag after explicit consent is granted. For EU visitors (detected via Vercel-IP-Country header), the consent banner defaults to opt-in required mode; for non-EU visitors, GA4 loads with implicit consent per CAN-SPAM. Contentful webhook configuration added to /api/revalidate endpoint with CONTENTFUL_WEBHOOK_SECRET header verification.
fix_report.expected_outcome: GA4 tracking fires only after cookie consent for EU visitors, satisfying GDPR Article 7 requirements. Contentful content updates propagate to Vercel edge within seconds of CMS publish events.

target_output.title: qPCR Thermal Cycler Product Landing Page - Revision 2
target_output.executive_summary: Next.js 14 static marketing landing page on Vercel Edge for a real-time PCR thermal cycler instrument.
