fix_report.action_taken: Updated pkg/sacct TRES parser to split gres/ resource keys on colon and aggregate all gres/gpu:* variant values (gres/gpu:a100, gres/gpu:v100, gres/gpu:h100) into a single alloc_gpus total per job record; added --gpu-types flag to analyze subcommand for filtering reports to specific GPU model strings; added test fixtures with multi-GPU-type sacct records for A100 and V100 TRES strings
fix_report.expected_outcome: GPU allocation figures will be accurate for research centers with heterogeneous GPU clusters, enabling correct cost attribution for GPU-intensive HPC users

target_output.title: HPC Cost Attribution CLI v2
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 Cobra CLI tool for research computing centers that parses SLURM sacct accounting exports, loads job records into an in-process DuckDB database, and generates per-PI and per-project cost attribution reports with CPU and memory efficiency metrics, idle node identification, and GPU waste estimation. Outputs JSON, CSV, and HTML reports with optional Slack and email delivery. Distributed as a statically-linked binary for frictionless HPC login node deployment.
target_output.architecture_overview: Statically-linked Go binary with Cobra-structured subcommands: analyze, validate, and schema. The analyze subcommand drives the full pipeline: pkg/sacct parser reads SLURM sacct TSV exports, decomposes TRES resource strings (cpu=N,mem=Ng,gres/gpu:TYPE=N) into typed struct fields, and passes parsed job_records to pkg/analytics which opens a DuckDB in-process database, materializes records into a DuckDB table, and executes parameterized SQL queries for efficiency metrics, cost attribution, and idle node detection. pkg/report serializes query results to JSON via encoding/json, CSV via encoding/csv, or HTML via html/template with automatic job-name escaping. Optional pkg/deliver posts reports to Slack webhooks or SMTP recipients. No network access required for core functionality; all computation is local on the HPC login node.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Go 1.22. CLI: Cobra v1.8 with persistent flags for --input-dir, --output-dir, --format, --period, --account-filter, --gpu-types, --verbose, --dry-run. Analytics: DuckDB via go-duckdb CGO bindings with static linking for HPC compatibility. Parsing: Go standard library encoding/csv for sacct TSV; custom TRES parser in pkg/sacct. Reporting: encoding/json, encoding/csv, html/template (with auto-escaping). Delivery: net/http for Slack webhooks, net/smtp for email. Testing: go test with table-driven tests and testdata/ sacct fixture files. CI: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint, gosec, staticcheck.
target_output.functional_feature_map: analyze command: parses specified sacct TSV export file(s), runs DuckDB efficiency and cost attribution queries, outputs reports in --format to --output-dir. --period YYYY-MM flag filters job records to a specific billing month. --account-filter flag limits report to specific project account(s). --gpu-types flag filters GPU efficiency breakdown to named GPU models (a100, v100, h100). validate command: checks sacct file column layout and TRES field parsability, reports malformed rows with line numbers. schema command: prints expected sacct column headers for sacct --format string configuration. --dry-run flag on analyze prints DuckDB query plan without executing queries or writing output files.
target_output.system_components: Cobra CLI entry point (main.go) wiring subcommand handlers. pkg/sacct: TSV reader with SLURM sacct header auto-detection, TRES decomposer handling cpu/mem/gres/gpu:TYPE colon-suffix GPU variants, JobRecord struct with alloc_cpus, req_cpus, alloc_mem_gb, req_mem_gb, alloc_gpus map[string]int by GPU type. pkg/analytics: DuckDB session lifecycle manager, query template registry for efficiency_metrics, cost_attribution, idle_nodes, overalloc_memory SQL files, parameterized execution with period and account filters. pkg/report: ReportSerializer interface with JSONSerializer, CSVSerializer, HTMLSerializer implementations using html/template for XSS-safe HTML output. pkg/deliver: SlackDelivery via incoming webhook, SMTPDelivery via net/smtp, webhook URL and SMTP credentials from environment variables only.
target_output.workflows: Monthly Cost Attribution Workflow: Admin runs hpcreport analyze --input-dir /shared/slurm/accounting --period 2026-02 --format csv,html --output-dir /reports/2026-02. Tool reads sacct TSV files from input directory; TRES parser splits cpu=32,mem=128G,gres/gpu:a100=2 into alloc_cpus=32, alloc_mem_gb=128, alloc_gpus[a100]=2; DuckDB materializes 500k job records into in-process table; efficiency_metrics query computes SELECT account, user, SUM(alloc_cpus*elapsed_secs)/3600 as alloc_cpu_hours, SUM(req_cpus*elapsed_secs)/3600 as used_cpu_hours, ROUND(100.0*SUM(req_cpus)/NULLIF(SUM(alloc_cpus),0),1) as cpu_efficiency_pct, SUM(alloc_mem_gb*elapsed_secs)/3600 as alloc_mem_gb_hours FROM job_records GROUP BY account, user ORDER BY alloc_cpu_hours DESC; cost_attribution query joins with project_accounts cost_rate to produce estimated_waste_usd per PI; HTML report rendered via html/template with auto-escaped job names; report posted to configured Slack channel via pkg/deliver. GPU Waste Detection Workflow: Admin runs hpcreport analyze --gpu-types a100,h100 --period 2026-02 to focus on GPU allocations; TRES parser maps gres/gpu:a100 and gres/gpu:h100 to alloc_gpus subtotals; DuckDB GPU query computes alloc_gpu_hours vs actual_gpu_hours per account using elapsed_secs and gpu_alloc_count; low-utilization GPU jobs flagged with estimated_gpu_waste_usd for PI advisory report.
target_output.data_model: JobRecord Go struct: job_id STRING, job_name STRING, account STRING, user STRING, alloc_cpus INT, req_cpus INT, alloc_mem_gb FLOAT64, req_mem_gb FLOAT64, alloc_gpus map[string]int (keyed by GPU type e.g. 'a100','v100','h100'), elapsed_secs INT64, state STRING, submit_time TIME, start_time TIME, end_time TIME. DuckDB job_records table: materialized from parsed JobRecord slice with TRES GPU map unpivoted to columns alloc_gpus_a100, alloc_gpus_v100, alloc_gpus_h100 via dynamic column generation based on --gpu-types flag. project_accounts optional CSV sidecar: account_name, pi_name, department, cpu_cost_per_hour, gpu_cost_per_hour loaded if --account-rates-file flag provided. efficiency_metrics result struct: account, user, alloc_cpu_hours, used_cpu_hours, cpu_efficiency_pct, alloc_mem_gb_hours, mem_efficiency_pct, job_count, failed_job_count, waste_cost_estimate_usd. cost_attribution_reports: written to output directory as {period}-{format}.{ext} files.
target_output.api_design: CLI command interface: hpcreport analyze [--input-dir PATH] [--output-dir PATH] [--format json|csv|html] [--period YYYY-MM|start:end] [--account-filter ACCT,...] [--gpu-types TYPE,...] [--account-rates-file PATH] [--slack-channel CHANNEL] [--verbose] [--dry-run]. hpcreport validate --input-dir PATH prints per-file column validation and TRES parse errors with line numbers. hpcreport schema --scheduler slurm prints recommended sacct --format string for generating compatible exports. All flags follow GNU long-option conventions; short aliases: -i (--input-dir), -o (--output-dir), -f (--format), -v (--verbose).
target_output.security_and_compliance: Slack webhook URL read from HPCREPORT_SLACK_WEBHOOK environment variable; SMTP credentials from HPCREPORT_SMTP_PASSWORD; neither accepted as CLI flags to prevent shell history exposure. HTML report generation uses html/template for automatic escaping of all user-controlled string fields including job_name, account, and user to prevent HTML injection from maliciously crafted SLURM job names. Tool reads from existing SLURM accounting files at OS permission level of invoking user; no privilege escalation. No network connections for core analyze and validate subcommands. sacct file path validated to prevent directory traversal in output file naming.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions release.yml: builds linux/amd64 binary with CGO_ENABLED=1 in ubuntu-22.04 runner using static linking flags CGO_LDFLAGS='-static-libstdc++ -static-libgcc'; builds darwin/arm64 for macOS local use; uploads both binaries and SHA256 checksums to GitHub Release on tag push. HPC deployment: admin downloads binary to /usr/local/bin/hpcreport and sets executable permissions; no shared library dependencies. Homebrew tap formula for macOS users: brew install hpccompute/tap/hpcreport. RPM spec file for RHEL 8 package management. Binary versioning: --version flag prints git tag, build timestamp, and Go version for support diagnostics.
target_output.observability: Structured log output to stderr: JSON lines with fields {timestamp, level, command, step, message} when --verbose flag set. Progress indication: number of sacct records parsed, DuckDB query duration in ms, report size in KB. --dry-run flag prints DuckDB EXPLAIN output for each analytical query to validate query plan before full execution. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 invalid flags, 2 sacct parse errors, 3 DuckDB query failure, 4 report write failure; allows scripting in cron jobs with exit code alerting.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost; binary runs on existing HPC login node. DuckDB processes 1M sacct job records in under 10 seconds on a typical login node. Memory footprint: DuckDB uses approximately 2GB RAM for 10M record datasets; --memory-limit flag configures DuckDB memory cap for shared login nodes. Compilation: single GitHub Actions build job produces release artifacts; no ongoing compute costs.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (3 weeks): SLURM sacct TSV parser with TRES decomposer including GPU variant support, DuckDB efficiency_metrics and cost_attribution queries, JSON and CSV output, analyze and validate subcommands, GitHub Actions CI with golangci-lint and go test, binary release pipeline. Phase 2 (3 weeks): HTML report output with html/template and chart.js embedded sparklines for efficiency trend visualization, Slack and email delivery via pkg/deliver, --account-rates-file for dollar waste estimates, schema subcommand. Phase 3 (2 weeks): PBS/Torque sacct-equivalent log parser, monthly trend comparison across multiple report periods, Homebrew tap formula and RPM spec file.
target_output.development_guidelines: Table-driven unit tests in pkg/sacct with testdata/ fixture files covering standard TRES, multi-GPU-type TRES (gres/gpu:a100=2,gres/gpu:v100=1), zero-resource jobs, cancelled and timeout states. Integration tests in pkg/analytics load full synthetic sacct TSV fixtures into DuckDB and validate query result correctness. golangci-lint with errcheck (no ignored errors), staticcheck, gosec (no hardcoded credentials check). All error returns include context via fmt.Errorf('sacct parse row %d: %w', row, err) wrapping. Benchmark tests for TRES parser and DuckDB load path in pkg/analytics to catch performance regressions. NEWS.md changelog entry required for every user-visible change.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: go-duckdb CGO static linking fails on RHEL 8 due to libstdc++ version mismatch. Mitigation: CI release build validated against ubuntu-22.04 container with RHEL 8-compatible glibc; test binary on target RHEL 8 node before each release; fallback to glibc-compatible build flags documented in CONTRIBUTING.md. Risk: SLURM sacct output format varies across SLURM versions; undocumented columns cause parse failures. Mitigation: validate subcommand performs column layout check before analyze; parser uses column-name-indexed reading not positional so additional columns are ignored. Tradeoff: DuckDB over SQLite for columnar aggregation performance on multi-million-row sacct files at cost of CGO complexity and larger binary size. Tradeoff: Go over Python for single static binary distribution without Python runtime dependency management on HPC nodes.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: ISS-001 resolved: TRES parser updated to handle gres/gpu:TYPE colon-suffix GPU variants with aggregation by GPU type. ISS-002 resolved in round 1: html/template with auto-escaping replaces fmt.Sprintf HTML construction. DuckDB selected over SQLite for columnar scan performance. Go selected over Python for static binary HPC deployment requirement.

metadata.notes: Round 2 HPC SLURM cost attribution CLI; resolves GPU TRES parser gap (ISS-001); strong quality with DuckDB query examples and full Cobra command signatures.

project_goal.value: Continuously audit instrument calibration records, software validation documentation, and employee training certifications in GxP-regulated pharma development labs to flag overdue items before regulatory inspections
project_goal.rationale: Proactive compliance monitoring reduces inspection findings and regulatory risk

target_users.value: Compliance coordinators, department heads, and QA managers at pharmaceutical development laboratories
target_users.rationale: Primary users who act on compliance alerts and generate reports

project_class.rationale: Headless automated auditing with no browser UI required

capabilities.rationale: Polling integrations, scheduled jobs, data persistence, and reporting are the core needs

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-system integration with compliance rule engine but no real-time streaming

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect compliance status reporting could cause regulatory violations and production shutdowns

data_sensitivity.rationale: Calibration and validation records are GxP-controlled documents with restricted access

external_exposure.rationale: Tool operates entirely within corporate network without external access

access_model.value: Azure AD service principals for system integrations; role-based access for compliance coordinator and department head views via report download endpoint
access_model.rationale: Service accounts for automated polling, RBAC for human-facing report access

feature_scope.value: Instrument calibration expiry tracking, software validation status monitoring, training certification tracking, overdue item alerting, weekly digest reports, escalation workflows to department heads
feature_scope.rationale: Complete compliance coverage across three GxP domains

mvp_scope.value: Calibration expiry monitoring and weekly email digests with department head escalation for critical overdue items
mvp_scope.rationale: Highest inspection risk area delivered first

security_baseline.value: Azure AD service principals with minimum-privilege scopes, all inter-service communication over TLS 1.3, audit log of all compliance data reads and state changes enforced via append-only PostgreSQL trigger
security_baseline.rationale: GxP systems require traceable access and tamper-evident records under 21 CFR Part 11

frontend_stack.value: None - headless automation tool with no browser UI; output via email digests and status API
frontend_stack.rationale: No user interface required for automated compliance polling and alerting

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Celery 5.3 task queue and Redis broker
backend_stack.rationale: Celery provides mature scheduled job and retry semantics; Python integrates well with QMS APIs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for compliance state and rule definitions; Azure Blob Storage immutable tier for report archives
data_platform.rationale: Relational data suits compliance rules and audit trail; Blob immutable for tamper-evident report storage

hosting_target.value: Azure Container Apps on corporate Azure subscription
hosting_target.rationale: Managed container runtime within existing corporate Azure footprint

privacy_retention_policy.value: Compliance records retained 15 years per GMP documentation requirements; audit logs retained 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: FDA 21 CFR Part 211 mandates GMP record retention periods

future_scope.value: Risk-based audit scheduling, real-time dashboard via separate web app, integration with batch record systems
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 enhancements after MVP validation

constraints.value: Must integrate with Trackwise QMS, SAP HR for training records, and internal calibration database via REST APIs; all data must remain on corporate Azure
constraints.rationale: Data sovereignty requirements and existing system landscape

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor for container health, structured JSON logging to Log Analytics, Celery task failure alerts via email
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard; Celery failures directly impact compliance monitoring

execution_preference.value: Phased delivery with MVP in 10 weeks, validated and deployed to production before full rollout
execution_preference.rationale: GxP systems require validation before production use

compliance_context.value: FDA 21 CFR Part 211 (GMP manufacturing), 21 CFR Part 11 (electronic records and audit trails), EU GMP Annex 11 (computerized systems), ICH Q10 (pharmaceutical quality system)
compliance_context.rationale: Tool itself is a GxP computerized system requiring validation and compliant record-keeping

project.goal: Automate GxP compliance monitoring across calibration, validation, and training domains
project.target_users: Compliance coordinators, department heads, QA managers
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks, full deployment in 16 weeks

backend.api: Internal REST API for compliance status queries

security.auth: Azure AD service principals
security.audit: Append-only compliance_events table enforced via PostgreSQL trigger

data.archive: Azure Blob Storage immutable tier for PDF reports

devops.environments: dev, validation, production

constraints.integrations: Trackwise QMS, SAP HR, calibration DB via REST

reasoner_reviews.product: Compliance monitoring scope is well-prioritized with calibration tracking as the MVP anchor. The escalation workflow from coordinator to department head mirrors how GxP organizations actually manage overdue items. The 10-week MVP target is achievable given the defined integration scope with Trackwise and calibration DB.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Celery on Azure Container Apps is appropriate for the scheduled polling and digest generation pattern. The three-domain compliance rule engine should be modeled as a pluggable rule registry so new rule types can be added without core code changes. PostgreSQL for state and a separate Blob archive for immutable PDFs is the correct separation of concerns.
reasoner_reviews.security: All service principal credentials must be stored in Azure Key Vault and rotated on a schedule. The audit log of compliance record reads is essential since GxP requires traceability of who accessed controlled documents. The tool itself requires IQ/OQ/PQ validation as a GxP computerized system before production use.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Trackwise QMS and SAP HR integration via REST is feasible but requires IT coordination for API credentials and schema documentation. Integration with calibration DB needs confirmation on whether it exposes REST or flat file exports.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The round 1 plan did not address the IQ/OQ/PQ validation protocol requirement for the tool itself as a GxP computerized system. This is a blocking gap. Additionally, the severity scoring logic for overdue items was undefined, which would lead to inconsistent escalation behavior.

backend.service_design: Three Celery beat workers: CalibrationPoller (daily at 06:00), ValidationDocPoller (weekly Monday 07:00), TrainingCertPoller (daily at 06:30). ComplianceRuleEngine evaluates overdue rules per domain. DigestComposer aggregates findings and triggers NotificationDispatcher.
backend.business_modules: compliance_rules (rule definitions per domain with advance_warning_days and critical_threshold_days), overdue_items (current state per domain), audit_events (immutable append-only log), integration_adapters (Trackwise, SAP HR, CalibDB connectors), notification_templates
backend.background_jobs: Celery beat schedule: CalibrationPoller 06:00 daily, TrainingCertPoller 06:30 daily, ValidationDocPoller 07:00 Monday, DigestComposer 08:00 Monday, EscalationChecker 09:00 daily for critical items
backend.failure_handling: Celery retry with exponential backoff max 3 retries for integration failures; dead-letter queue for persistently failing polls; alert on more than 2 consecutive failures for any adapter

security.auth_design: Azure AD service principals per integration target with minimum-required API scopes; human API access via Azure AD group membership checked at report download endpoint
security.secrets_management: All service principal credentials and connection strings in Azure Key Vault; Container Apps pulls secrets at startup via managed identity
security.audit_and_logging_controls: compliance_events table with immutable rows enforced via PostgreSQL trigger blocking UPDATE and DELETE; all reads of instrument or training records logged with actor, timestamp, and resource ID
security.security_risks: Service principal credential theft could expose QMS data; mitigated by short-lived tokens and Key Vault rotation policy

data.entities: instruments (id, asset_number, description, location, calibration_frequency_days, last_calibrated_at, next_due_at, status), calibration_records (id, instrument_id, performed_by, performed_at, certificate_ref, result ENUM pass/fail/conditional), validation_documents (id, system_name, doc_type ENUM IQ/OQ/PQ/periodic_review, version, effective_date, next_review_due, status), training_certifications (id, employee_id, training_code, completed_at, expiry_at, competency_level), overdue_items (id, domain ENUM calibration/validation/training, reference_id, due_date, days_overdue, severity ENUM critical/high/medium, assigned_department), compliance_rules (id, domain, rule_name, advance_warning_days, critical_threshold_days, enabled)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security per department; Azure Blob Storage immutable tier for generated PDF digest reports
data.retention_and_deletion: compliance_events and calibration_records: 15 years per 21 CFR Part 211; automated archival jobs move records older than 5 years to Blob cold tier

devops.deployment_topology: Azure Container Apps environment with three containers: celery-worker (2 replicas), celery-beat (1 replica singleton), postgres sidecar health monitor. Azure Cache for Redis Standard C1 as Celery broker.
devops.environments: dev (single replica, non-production integrations), validation (production-mirror for IQ/OQ/PQ test execution), production (2 worker replicas, zone-redundant Postgres)
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps pipeline: ruff lint, pytest 85% coverage gate, Docker build, push Azure Container Registry, az containerapp update to dev, manual approval gate for validation and production promotion
devops.rollback_strategy: Container Apps revision-based rollback; previous image retained in ACR for 30 days; PostgreSQL point-in-time recovery window 7 days

ISS-001.detail: The plan had no section covering IQ/OQ/PQ validation of the compliance monitoring tool as a GxP computerized system. Deploying an unvalidated tool to monitor GxP activities would itself be a regulatory violation under 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GMP Annex 11.

target_output.title: GxP Compliance Automation Tool Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A headless Python 3.12/Celery automation tool deployed on Azure Container Apps that monitors three GxP compliance domains - instrument calibration, software validation documentation, and employee training certifications - across pharmaceutical development laboratories. Polls Trackwise QMS, SAP HR, and the calibration database daily via REST adapters, applies a configurable compliance rule engine with explicit severity scoring, and delivers weekly digest emails with department-head escalation for critical overdue items. The tool itself is validated as a GxP computerized system per 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GMP Annex 11 before production deployment.
target_output.architecture_overview: Celery beat scheduler (singleton container) drives three domain pollers: CalibrationPoller pulling instrument and certificate records from the internal calibration database REST API, ValidationDocPoller querying Trackwise QMS for IQ/OQ/PQ and periodic review status, and TrainingCertPoller fetching employee certification records from SAP HR OData. Each poller writes current state into PostgreSQL where the ComplianceRuleEngine evaluates overdue status using domain-specific threshold rules stored in compliance_rules. DigestComposer aggregates overdue_items into structured HTML reports delivered via Azure Communication Services email. An append-only compliance_events table captures every integration read and state change for 21 CFR Part 11 traceability. All service credentials stored in Azure Key Vault accessed via Container Apps managed identity.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, Celery 5.3 with Redis broker, SQLAlchemy 2.0 ORM, Jinja2 for email templates. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security per department. Archive: Azure Blob Storage immutable tier for PDF digest reports. Secrets: Azure Key Vault with managed identity. Hosting: Azure Container Apps (celery-worker x2, celery-beat x1). CI/CD: Azure DevOps. Monitoring: Azure Monitor and Log Analytics. Email: Azure Communication Services. Integrations: Trackwise QMS REST, SAP HR OData, internal calibration DB REST.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Calibration Expiry Monitor: daily poll of instrument calibration records with advance warning at configurable day thresholds and critical escalation when overdue exceeds threshold or inspection within 14 days. Validation Document Tracker: weekly poll of Trackwise for IQ/OQ/PQ validation status and periodic review due dates by system name. Training Certification Monitor: daily poll of SAP HR for employee certification expiry by training code and competency level. Compliance Rule Engine: evaluates severity scoring formula against current overdue state with deterministic critical/high/medium assignment. Weekly Digest Composer: aggregates all domains into department-grouped HTML emails. Escalation Dispatcher: sends department-head alerts for critical items within 30 minutes of detection.
target_output.system_components: CalibrationPoller (Celery task, 06:00 daily): GETs /api/v1/instruments and /api/v1/calibration-records from calibration DB REST API, writes to instruments and calibration_records tables. ValidationDocPoller (Celery task, 07:00 Monday): queries Trackwise QMS REST for validation documents by system, updates validation_documents table. TrainingCertPoller (Celery task, 06:30 daily): queries SAP HR OData /EmployeeTraining endpoint, updates training_certifications. ComplianceRuleEngine (invoked post-poll): applies severity formula - severity=critical if days_overdue > compliance_rules.critical_threshold_days OR inspection_proximity_days < 14; severity=high if days_overdue > 0 and not critical; severity=medium if days_until_due <= advance_warning_days. DigestComposer (Celery task, 08:00 Monday): queries overdue_items grouped by department and domain, renders Jinja2 HTML templates, posts to Azure Communication Services Send Email API. EscalationChecker (09:00 daily): queries critical overdue_items added in last 24 hours, dispatches immediate department-head alert emails.
target_output.workflows: Calibration Expiry Detection: CalibrationPoller retrieves next_due_at from calibration DB for all active instruments; ComplianceRuleEngine computes days_until_due and days_overdue, writes overdue_items row with severity=critical if overdue more than critical_threshold_days or lab inspection scheduled within 14 days, severity=high if overdue 1 to critical_threshold_days days, severity=medium if due within advance_warning_days; compliance_events row logged for each instrument read. Weekly Digest Dispatch: DigestComposer at 08:00 Monday aggregates all open overdue_items by department; renders HTML digest listing each item with domain, reference ID, due date, days overdue, severity badge, and responsible owner; email posted to Azure Communication Services; PDF copy stored to Azure Blob immutable container with 15-year retention tag. Critical Escalation: EscalationChecker daily detects new critical overdue_items; generates targeted alert to department head and QA manager with item detail and regulatory citation such as '21 CFR Part 211.68 - instrument calibration'; alert dispatched within 30 minutes of detection.
target_output.data_model: instruments (id UUID PK, asset_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, description, location_code, department_id FK, calibration_frequency_days INT, last_calibrated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, next_due_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM active/retired). calibration_records (id UUID PK, instrument_id FK, performed_by VARCHAR, performed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, certificate_ref VARCHAR, result ENUM pass/fail/conditional, source_system VARCHAR). validation_documents (id UUID PK, system_name VARCHAR, doc_type ENUM IQ/OQ/PQ/periodic_review, version VARCHAR, effective_date DATE, next_review_due DATE, owner_department_id FK, status ENUM current/overdue/superseded). training_certifications (id UUID PK, employee_id VARCHAR, employee_name, training_code VARCHAR, completed_at DATE, expiry_at DATE, competency_level ENUM basic/advanced/qualified). overdue_items (id UUID PK, domain ENUM calibration/validation/training, reference_id UUID, due_date DATE, days_overdue INT, days_until_due INT, severity ENUM critical/high/medium, assigned_department_id FK, first_detected_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). compliance_rules (id UUID PK, domain, rule_name, advance_warning_days INT, critical_threshold_days INT, enabled BOOL). compliance_events (id UUID PK, event_type VARCHAR, domain, actor VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ) - append-only enforced via PostgreSQL trigger blocking UPDATE and DELETE.
target_output.api_design: Internal status API (SQLAlchemy direct queries). GET /api/v1/status returns overall compliance posture counts by domain and severity. GET /api/v1/overdue?domain=calibration&severity=critical returns paginated overdue items for department views. GET /api/v1/instruments/:id/history returns calibration record timeline. POST /api/v1/rules (admin) creates or updates compliance rule thresholds with advance_warning_days and critical_threshold_days. GET /api/v1/digests returns list of generated digest report metadata with Blob SAS URLs. All endpoints require Azure AD bearer token with compliance-reader or compliance-admin scope.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Access Control: Azure AD service principals per external system with minimum-required API scopes; human API access via Azure AD group-based role assignment checked at each endpoint. Secrets: all connection strings and client secrets in Azure Key Vault; Container Apps managed identity retrieves secrets at startup. Audit Trail: compliance_events table append-only enforced via PostgreSQL trigger blocking UPDATE and DELETE; all polled record reads and state changes logged with actor, timestamp, and resource ID to satisfy 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements. Computerized System Validation: The tool is validated as a GxP computerized system per 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GMP Annex 11 before production deployment. Validation protocol covers Installation Qualification confirming infrastructure matches design specification, Operational Qualification with pytest test scripts verifying each poller fetches and stores records correctly and severity formula produces expected outputs against synthetic test fixtures, and Performance Qualification running in validation environment with production-representative data volumes. Validation documentation stored in Trackwise with change control. Periodic review every 2 years or on significant code change. TLS 1.3 enforced on all inter-service and integration calls.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Container topology: Azure Container Apps environment with three container revisions - celery-beat (singleton, 0.5 vCPU, 1Gi), celery-worker (2 replicas scaling 2-4 on queue depth via KEDA, 1 vCPU, 2Gi), postgres health monitor sidecar. Azure Cache for Redis Standard C1 as Celery broker and result backend. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server Standard_D2s_v3 zone-redundant for compliance state. Azure Blob Storage immutable policy container for digest PDF archives. Environments: dev (single worker replica, non-production integrations with read-only cloned schemas), validation (production-mirror for IQ/OQ/PQ execution, isolated from prod data), production (2 worker replicas, zone-redundant Postgres, production integration credentials). CI/CD: Azure DevOps pipeline - ruff lint, pytest 85% coverage gate, Docker build, push to Azure Container Registry, az containerapp update to dev automatically, manual approval gate for validation promotion, separate manual gate for production. Rollback: Container Apps revision rollback via az containerapp revision set-mode pointing to prior revision retained 30 days.
target_output.observability: Azure Monitor container insights for CPU, memory, and restart counts on all Container Apps replicas. Log Analytics workspace with structured JSON log ingestion from Python logging handlers with correlation IDs per poller run. Custom metrics via Azure Application Insights: poller_run_duration_seconds per domain, overdue_items_count by domain and severity as gauge scraped after each poll cycle, digest_emails_sent_total, escalation_alerts_sent_total, integration_failures_total by adapter name. Alerts: integration_failures_total more than 2 in 1 hour triggers Azure Monitor action group to on-call DevOps; celery-beat container restart triggers immediate page; PostgreSQL connection pool above 90% triggers alert.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $180/month: Azure Container Apps compute $60 for 3 containers with low CPU headroom, Azure Cache for Redis Standard C1 $55, Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Standard_D2s_v3 $50, Azure Blob Storage $5 for digest archives, Azure Communication Services email $10 for weekly digests and escalations. Celery-worker scales 2-4 replicas based on queue depth via KEDA scaler. No horizontal DB sharding needed at projected compliance data volumes for a single laboratory facility.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): CalibrationPoller with Trackwise calibration DB integration, ComplianceRuleEngine with calibration domain rules and explicit severity formula, DigestComposer for weekly calibration-focused email digest, EscalationChecker for critical overdue items, PostgreSQL schema migrations, IQ/OQ/PQ validation protocol execution in validation environment, production deployment with validated status documentation in Trackwise. Phase 2 (6 weeks): ValidationDocPoller for Trackwise validation document tracking, TrainingCertPoller for SAP HR training certification integration, multi-domain digest combining all three compliance domains, compliance_rules admin API. Phase 3 (8 weeks): risk-based scheduling increasing poll frequency for high-risk instruments approaching inspection dates, inspection proximity detection for 14-day critical threshold, PDF digest archival to Azure Blob immutable tier, API surface for future dashboard integration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with strict type annotations enforced by mypy. Ruff for linting and formatting in CI. Each Celery task unit-tested with mocked integration adapters using pytest-mock; 85% coverage gate enforced. Integration adapter tests use VCR.py cassettes recorded against non-production Trackwise and SAP HR environments. Validation test scripts written as pytest parameterized tests against synthetic compliance data fixtures and committed to the validation protocol repository. All compliance rule changes require code review plus test coverage for the new rule formula. Change control ticket required before any production deployment post-validation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Trackwise QMS REST API unavailability causes silent compliance data staleness; mitigation: poller records last_successful_poll_at per domain and raises alert if more than 24 hours since last successful run, and digest email includes data-freshness warning. Risk: SAP HR API schema changes break training cert parsing; mitigation: schema version pinning in adapter with automated contract tests against HR API sandbox. Risk: IQ/OQ/PQ validation timeline extends MVP delivery if test script failures require bug fixes and re-execution; mitigation: allocate 3 weeks in Phase 1 for validation assuming one re-execution cycle. Tradeoff: Celery beat singleton risks single-point-of-failure for scheduling; accepted over complex distributed locking because Container Apps restart SLA is sufficient for non-real-time compliance polling.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Calibration DB API protocol confirmed as REST not flat file export after IT coordination. Trackwise version confirmed as 12.x with REST API module licensed. SAP HR OData endpoint access granted to service principal after security review. Validation environment provisioned as production-mirror with non-production integration credentials. Digest email distribution lists owned by compliance coordinators and maintained in Azure AD groups.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision resolving critical GxP computerized system validation gap and severity logic. Pharmaceutical compliance automation with Celery/PostgreSQL/Azure Container Apps.

profile.domain: Research Institution Chemical Inventory and Regulatory Compliance

project_goal.value: Web application for research institutions to manage chemical inventory, track hazardous substance usage, and generate regulatory compliance reports for OSHA, EPA, and local fire authority requirements across multiple building locations
project_goal.rationale: Core mission is replacing paper-based chemical logs with a digital system that automates Tier II and HazCom reporting

target_users.value: Lab staff logging chemical receipts and usage, EHS (Environmental Health and Safety) officers generating compliance reports, and department safety coordinators managing storage location limits
target_users.rationale: Primary users span bench-level chemical handlers and compliance administrators

project_class.rationale: Multi-page web application with backend API and admin panel; not a single landing page or pure API service

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Rich data-entry UI, Django admin for EHS officers, scheduled regulatory report generation, and usage analytics required

complexity_level.rationale: Regulatory report generation and storage limit enforcement add domain complexity beyond standard inventory CRUD

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect chemical storage limit tracking could result in regulatory violations or safety incidents; EHS compliance data must be accurate and audit-ready

data_sensitivity.rationale: Chemical inventory data is internal institutional operational data; not personal health or financial data but requires access control given regulatory obligations

external_exposure.rationale: System accessed only by institutional users on campus network or VPN; no external partner access

access_model.value: RBAC with lab_staff (log usage), safety_coordinator (manage location limits, view reports), ehs_officer (full access, report submission, admin panel), institution_admin (user management) roles; Azure AD SSO
access_model.rationale: Regulatory compliance requires role-separated access with EHS officers having report submission authority

feature_scope.value: Chemical receipt logging with barcode scanning, usage event recording, storage location management with hazard class quantity limits, SDS (Safety Data Sheet) attachment and search, disposal event recording, automated Tier II report generation, HazCom chemical list export, storage limit breach alerts, compliance calendar with reporting deadlines
feature_scope.rationale: Full EPCRA Tier II, OSHA HazCom, and local fire code compliance workflow

mvp_scope.value: Chemical inventory management with receipt and usage logging, storage location limit tracking with alerts, SDS attachment, and manual Tier II report generation for EHS review
mvp_scope.rationale: Core compliance workflow for annual Tier II reporting cycle pilot

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, Azure AD SAML SSO, CSRF protection on all form submissions, audit log for all inventory modifications
security_baseline.rationale: Internal institutional security baseline for an EHS regulatory system

frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for fast initial page loads on chemical inventory views; team familiarity with React ecosystem

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Django 5 and Django REST Framework
backend_stack.rationale: Django admin provides rapid EHS admin panel; DRF for API consumed by Next.js frontend; Django ORM for complex inventory queries

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7 for Celery task queue
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for chemical inventory and regulatory report associations; Redis for background report generation jobs

hosting_target.value: Azure App Service with Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
hosting_target.rationale: Institution uses Azure as preferred cloud; Azure AD integration is native with App Service Managed Identity

privacy_retention_policy.value: Chemical inventory records retained 10 years per RCRA and EPA record-keeping requirements; disposal records retained 3 years per RCRA; audit logs retained 7 years per institutional policy
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: EPA EPCRA and RCRA mandate specific record retention periods for hazardous chemical data

future_scope.value: Barcode printer integration for chemical container labels, automated submission of Tier II reports to state SERC portal via API, waste pickup request workflow, integration with institutional procurement system for automatic receipt creation from purchase orders
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP automation roadmap to reduce EHS staff manual effort

constraints.value: Must integrate with institutional Azure AD for SSO; must generate Tier II reports in official EPA Tier2 Submit software import format (XML schema); must be accessible on campus network without VPN for lab staff using lab workstations
constraints.rationale: Azure AD is institutional standard; Tier II report format is mandated by EPA Tier2 Submit tool

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor Application Insights for APM and structured logging; Azure Monitor alerts for storage limit breach events and failed Celery report generation tasks
observability_baseline.rationale: Institution is standardized on Azure Monitor; Application Insights native to App Service

execution_preference.value: 2-week sprints with EHS officer review of report output after each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Regulatory accuracy requires domain expert validation of generated report content throughout development

compliance_context.value: OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (29 CFR 1910.1200, HazCom 2012), EPA EPCRA Section 312 Tier II reporting, local fire code chemical storage quantity limits (NFPA 30 and IFC), RCRA hazardous waste tracking requirements
compliance_context.rationale: Research institution handles regulated hazardous chemicals and has annual EPCRA Tier II reporting obligation

project.goal: Digital chemical inventory with automated EPCRA Tier II and HazCom compliance reporting for multi-building research campus
project.target_users: Lab staff, safety coordinators, EHS officers
project.timeline: MVP before next annual Tier II reporting deadline (March 1) in 16 weeks

frontend.state: SWR for data fetching; React Hook Form for inventory entry forms
frontend.barcode: react-barcode-reader for USB barcode scanner input on chemical receipt logging screens

backend.framework: Django 5 with DRF for API; Django admin for EHS panel
backend.tasks: Celery 5 with Redis broker for async Tier II report generation

security.auth: Azure AD SAML via django-saml2-auth; Managed Identity for Azure services
security.csrf: Django CSRF middleware on all form submissions

data.cache: Redis Cache for Azure (Celery broker and task result backend)
data.files: Azure Blob Storage for SDS PDF attachments

devops.hosting: Azure App Service (Django) + Azure Static Web Apps (Next.js) or unified on App Service
devops.environments: staging, production

constraints.report_format: EPA Tier2 Submit XML import format
constraints.network: Accessible on campus LAN without VPN

open_questions.sds_source: Should SDS PDFs be fetched from a third-party SDS library API or uploaded manually?

reasoner_reviews.product: The March 1 Tier II reporting deadline creates a hard external forcing function for the 16-week MVP timeline. Tier II report generation must be validated against the EPA Tier2 Submit XML schema before lab staff rely on it for regulatory submission. EHS officers need the ability to review and manually edit generated reports before export, as automated quantity aggregation may require domain judgment calls on maximum daily amount calculations.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Django with Django admin is the right choice here - the EHS admin panel requirements map directly to Django admin customizations without custom UI development. The Tier II XML report generation via Celery is appropriate since reports aggregate chemical quantities across thousands of records and can take several minutes for large institutions. PostgreSQL with Django ORM is well-suited for the complex joins required to aggregate inventory quantities by chemical and storage location for compliance thresholds.
reasoner_reviews.security: Azure AD SAML SSO is mandatory per institutional requirement and is well-supported by django-saml2-auth. The audit log requirement for all inventory modifications is critical for regulatory defensibility - any chemical quantity discrepancy during an EPA inspection must be traceable to a specific user action. CSRF protection on all Django form submissions is the baseline; Next.js API routes also need CSRF token passing from Django backend.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The EPA Tier2 Submit XML schema is a well-defined, stable format that maps to EPCRA Section 312 reporting fields. The aggregation logic for maximum daily amount (MDA) is the most complex calculation - requires finding the peak single-day quantity present at each storage location for each hazardous chemical over the reporting year. This must be precisely correct for regulatory submission.

backend.service_design: Django application with domain apps: inventory (chemicals, receipts, usage, disposals), locations (buildings, rooms, storage locations, hazard limits), compliance (Tier II reports, HazCom lists), sdslibrary (SDS PDF management), notifications (storage limit alerts), users (RBAC, Azure AD sync)
backend.api_patterns: Django REST Framework at /api/v1 with DefaultRouter; versioned ViewSets for chemicals, receipts, usage_events, disposal_events, storage_locations, tier2_reports. Django admin at /admin for EHS officer full-control panel with custom admin actions for report approval and submission logging.
backend.background_jobs: Celery tasks: generate_tier2_report (async, queries inventory aggregations, renders EPA XML, stores in Azure Blob); check_storage_limits (scheduled hourly, compares current quantities per location per hazard class against configured limits, creates StorageAlert records for breaches); daily_usage_summary (nightly aggregation for analytics dashboard)
backend.failure_handling: Celery task retry on PostgreSQL connection failure; Tier II report generation failures send email notification to EHS officer with error detail and partial progress; storage limit check failures logged as Azure Monitor critical alert

frontend.app_structure: Next.js App Router with server components for chemical inventory list and report views (SSR for fast initial load); client components for barcode scanner input and real-time storage limit gauge widgets
frontend.pages_and_flows: Chemical Inventory (/inventory): searchable paginated list of chemicals with current quantities per location, SDS link, hazard class badges. Receipt Logging (/receipts/new): barcode scan input for container ID, chemical lookup, quantity entry, storage location assignment. Usage Recording (/usage/new): chemical selection, quantity used, date, experiment reference. Storage Location Dashboard (/locations): per-location quantity vs limit gauges by hazard class with breach highlight. Tier II Report (/reports/tier2): select reporting year, trigger generation, download XML or view HTML preview. EHS Admin Panel (/admin): Django admin for full system management.
frontend.state_management: SWR for server data fetching with 30-second revalidation on inventory lists; React Hook Form for receipt and usage entry forms; no global state manager

security.auth_design: django-saml2-auth for Azure AD SAML 2.0 SSO; Django session authentication after SAML assertion; session timeout 8 hours matching university single sign-on policy
security.authorization_model: Django permission groups: lab_staff (add receipts and usage events, view own lab chemicals), safety_coordinator (add/edit chemicals and storage locations, view all location reports), ehs_officer (full CRUD, report generation and submission logging, Django admin access), institution_admin (user management, group assignments)
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Django signal-based audit middleware recording every CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE on Chemical, Receipt, UsageEvent, DisposalEvent models with user_id, timestamp, changed_fields JSON, and IP address into AuditLogEntry table; Application Insights structured log shipping

data.entities: chemicals (id UUID PK, cas_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, chemical_name VARCHAR, hazard_classes TEXT[], physical_state ENUM solid/liquid/gas, ehs_listed BOOLEAN, tier2_reportable BOOLEAN, sds_blob_path VARCHAR, created_at). storage_locations (id UUID PK, building VARCHAR, room VARCHAR, location_code VARCHAR, hazard_class_limits JSONB - keys are hazard class codes, values are quantity limits in kg). chemical_receipts (id UUID PK, chemical_id FK, storage_location_id FK, received_date DATE, quantity_received FLOAT, unit VARCHAR, container_barcode VARCHAR, received_by FK, po_number VARCHAR). usage_events (id UUID PK, chemical_id FK, storage_location_id FK, usage_date DATE, quantity_used FLOAT, unit VARCHAR, recorded_by FK, experiment_reference VARCHAR). disposal_events (id UUID PK, chemical_id FK, storage_location_id FK, disposal_date DATE, quantity_disposed FLOAT, unit VARCHAR, disposal_method VARCHAR, manifest_number VARCHAR, recorded_by FK). tier2_reports (id UUID PK, reporting_year INT, facility_id FK, status ENUM draft/pending_review/submitted, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, xml_blob_path VARCHAR, generated_by FK). storage_limit_alerts (id UUID PK, storage_location_id FK, chemical_id FK, hazard_class VARCHAR, current_quantity FLOAT, limit_quantity FLOAT, alerted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acknowledged_by FK NULLABLE, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Database Flexible Server with automated 35-day backup retention; Azure Blob Storage for SDS PDF files and generated Tier II XML reports; Redis Azure Cache for Celery broker and task result backend
data.analytics_events: chemical_inventory_by_hazard_class (current quantities aggregated by hazard class per building for compliance dashboard), tier2_max_daily_amounts (peak single-day quantity per chemical per location for Tier II MDA calculation), disposal_volume_by_method (monthly waste stream summary by disposal method for RCRA tracking)

devops.deployment_topology: Azure App Service Plan (B2 or P1v3) running Django application; Azure Static Web Apps for Next.js frontend or co-hosted on App Service with whitenoise; Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server; Azure Cache for Redis; Azure Blob Storage for SDS and report files
devops.environments: staging (Azure App Service Deployment Slot, same App Service Plan, shared PostgreSQL with staging database, Azure AD SAML configured for test users), production (full App Service with production database, live Azure AD)
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: Python lint (flake8, mypy), pytest Django tests (80% coverage gate with factory_boy fixtures for chemical inventory data), Next.js TypeScript type check and ESLint, Docker build, Azure App Service deployment via az webapp deploy; database migrations run as pre-deployment task via Django manage.py migrate
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor Application Insights SDK in Django (opencensus-ext-azure) and Next.js (applicationinsights); custom events: tier2_report_generated (reporting_year, chemical_count, duration_seconds), storage_limit_breach (location_code, hazard_class, current_qty, limit_qty); Azure Monitor alerts for Celery task failure rate > 5% and storage limit breach events

target_output.thinking_summary: First-pass architecture for a moderate-complexity regulatory compliance web app. Key decisions: Django admin as the EHS officer control panel to avoid building custom admin UI, Celery for async Tier II XML report generation since the maximum daily amount aggregation across thousands of inventory records can be slow, PostgreSQL window functions for MDA calculation, and Azure AD SAML as institutional SSO. Plan quality is moderate per manifest - the architecture is solid but the observability and cost sections will be somewhat thinner than a strong plan.
target_output.title: Research Institution Chemical Inventory and EPCRA Compliance Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A Django 5 fullstack web application for managing hazardous chemical inventory and generating OSHA HazCom and EPA EPCRA Tier II regulatory compliance reports across multi-building research campuses. Lab staff log chemical receipts and usage via barcode-scanned container IDs; Celery batch jobs aggregate maximum daily amounts for Tier II XML generation in EPA Tier2 Submit import format. Storage location quantity limits by hazard class are monitored with automated breach alerts. Deployed on Azure App Service with Azure AD SAML SSO and PostgreSQL Flexible Server.
target_output.architecture_overview: Django 5 application with Django REST Framework API consumed by a Next.js 14 SSR frontend. The EHS officer admin panel is built directly on the Django admin framework with custom model admins and actions for report approval. Celery workers backed by Azure Cache for Redis handle asynchronous Tier II XML report generation and scheduled storage limit checks. PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database Flexible Server stores all chemical inventory, usage, and disposal records. Azure Blob Storage holds SDS PDF attachments and generated report XML files. Azure AD SAML via django-saml2-auth provides institutional SSO. Application Insights provides APM and custom compliance event tracking.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, SWR, React Hook Form 7, react-barcode-reader for USB scanner input. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5.0, Django REST Framework 3.15, Celery 5.3, django-saml2-auth for Azure AD SAML. ORM and DB: Django ORM with PostgreSQL 16, psycopg3, Alembic (or Django migrations). Storage: Azure Blob Storage via azure-storage-blob for SDS files and report XML. Task Queue: Celery 5 with Azure Cache for Redis broker. Observability: Application Insights via opencensus-ext-azure. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Hosting: Azure App Service.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Chemical Inventory Management: register chemicals by CAS number with hazard class tags, physical state, and Tier II reportable flag. Chemical Receipt Logging: barcode scan container ID, record received quantity and storage location assignment; triggers storage limit re-check. Usage Event Recording: log quantity-used, date, and experiment reference per chemical per location. Disposal Event Recording: log disposal quantity, method (down-drain, incineration, pickup), and RCRA manifest number. Storage Location Limit Management: configure per-location maximum quantities per NFPA hazard class (Flammable, Oxidizer, Corrosive, Toxic); automated hourly limit checks with dashboard gauge widgets and email alerts on breach. SDS Management: upload and attach Safety Data Sheet PDFs to chemical records; searchable by chemical name and CAS number. Tier II Report Generation: EHS officer selects reporting year; Celery task aggregates maximum daily amounts per chemical per storage location, formats EPA Tier2 Submit XML, stores in Azure Blob; HTML preview and XML download available. HazCom Chemical List Export: generates OSHA-formatted chemical inventory list in Excel for posting requirements.
target_output.system_components: Next.js 14 frontend (Azure Static Web Apps or App Service whitenoise). Django 5 application server (Azure App Service, 2-4 instances with autoscale). Django admin panel (/admin) for EHS officer full-control interface with custom ChemicalAdmin, StorageLocationAdmin, Tier2ReportAdmin. Django REST Framework API (/api/v1) for frontend data fetching. Celery worker process (Azure App Service Worker Role or separate App Service instance) for report generation and storage limit monitoring. PostgreSQL 16 Azure Database Flexible Server (General Purpose tier with 32GB RAM for complex aggregation queries). Azure Cache for Redis (Celery broker and task result backend). Azure Blob Storage containers: sds-documents (SDS PDFs), tier2-reports (generated XML files, 10-year lifecycle policy). Application Insights workspace for APM and compliance event logging.
target_output.workflows: Chemical Receipt Logging Workflow: lab staff navigates to /receipts/new and scans container barcode with USB scanner; barcode is matched to existing chemical record or prompts CAS number lookup to create new chemical; staff enters received quantity, unit, and selects storage location from dropdown; Django REST Framework POST /api/v1/receipts/ creates ChemicalReceipt record; post-save signal triggers async Celery task to re-evaluate storage limit totals for the affected location and hazard classes; if any limit is exceeded, StorageLimitAlert record is created and email sent to assigned safety_coordinator. Tier II Report Generation Workflow: EHS officer opens /reports/tier2, selects reporting year (default: prior calendar year), clicks Generate Report; POST /api/v1/tier2-reports/ creates Tier2Report record with status=draft; Celery task generate_tier2_report is dispatched; task queries usage_events and chemical_receipts to calculate maximum daily amount for each hazardous chemical at each storage location using PostgreSQL window function MAX(daily_balance) OVER (PARTITION BY chemical_id, storage_location_id ORDER BY event_date ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING); formats EPA Tier2 Submit XML with facility contact information, chemical entries, and storage location coordinates; uploads XML to Azure Blob tier2-reports/{year}/{report_id}.xml; updates Tier2Report status to pending_review; EHS officer reviews HTML preview rendered from the XML, edits any fields requiring manual judgment, approves via PATCH /api/v1/tier2-reports/{id}/approve; downloads final XML for upload to EPA Tier2 Submit portal.
target_output.data_model: chemicals (id UUID PK, cas_number VARCHAR(10) UNIQUE, chemical_name VARCHAR, trade_names TEXT[], hazard_classes TEXT[] - NFPA/GHS class codes, physical_state ENUM solid/liquid/gas/mixture, ehs_listed BOOLEAN, tier2_reportable BOOLEAN, sds_blob_path VARCHAR NULLABLE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). storage_locations (id UUID PK, building_name VARCHAR, room_number VARCHAR, location_code VARCHAR UNIQUE, primary_contact_user_id FK, hazard_class_limits JSONB e.g. {'Flammable':50.0, 'Oxidizer':25.0} in kg, active BOOLEAN). chemical_receipts (id UUID PK, chemical_id FK, storage_location_id FK, received_date DATE, quantity_kg FLOAT NOT NULL, container_barcode VARCHAR, po_number VARCHAR, received_by FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). usage_events (id UUID PK, chemical_id FK, storage_location_id FK, usage_date DATE, quantity_used_kg FLOAT, experiment_reference VARCHAR, recorded_by FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). disposal_events (id UUID PK, chemical_id FK, storage_location_id FK, disposal_date DATE, quantity_disposed_kg FLOAT, disposal_method ENUM down_drain/incineration/hazmat_pickup/recycling, manifest_number VARCHAR NULLABLE, recorded_by FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). tier2_reports (id UUID PK, reporting_year INT, facility_name VARCHAR, status ENUM draft/pending_review/approved/submitted, generated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULLABLE, xml_blob_path VARCHAR, generated_by FK). storage_limit_alerts (id UUID PK, storage_location_id FK, hazard_class VARCHAR, current_quantity_kg FLOAT, limit_quantity_kg FLOAT, alerted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acknowledged_by FK NULLABLE). audit_log_entries (id UUID PK, user_id FK, action ENUM create/update/delete, model_name VARCHAR, object_id UUID, changed_fields JSONB, ip_address INET, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: DRF REST API at /api/v1. GET /chemicals?search=&hazard_class= (paginated list with current aggregate quantities). POST /chemicals (create chemical record). GET /chemicals/{id}/sds-url (Azure Blob signed URL for SDS PDF). POST /receipts (log chemical receipt; triggers storage limit check Celery task). POST /usage-events (log usage; triggers storage limit re-evaluation). POST /disposal-events (log disposal with manifest number). GET /storage-locations?building= (list with current quantity vs limit summary). GET /storage-locations/{id}/inventory (current chemical quantities at location by hazard class). POST /tier2-reports (create and dispatch Celery generation task; returns report_id). GET /tier2-reports/{id}/status (generation status: draft/generating/pending_review). GET /tier2-reports/{id}/preview (HTML render of Tier II data for EHS review). GET /tier2-reports/{id}/download (signed Azure Blob URL to XML file). PATCH /tier2-reports/{id}/approve (set status=approved). GET /storage-limit-alerts?acknowledged=false (open breach alerts). POST /storage-limit-alerts/{id}/acknowledge.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Azure AD SAML 2.0 SSO via django-saml2-auth; session timeout 8 hours; RBAC via Django permission groups with group assignments managed in Django admin by institution_admin role. CSRF protection: Django CsrfViewMiddleware on all Django views; DRF API uses SessionAuthentication with CSRF enforcement for Next.js client requests. Audit logging: Django post-save and post-delete signals write AuditLogEntry records for all Chemical, Receipt, UsageEvent, DisposalEvent, and Tier2Report model changes with user_id, changed_fields JSON, and IP; audit entries never deleted per 7-year retention policy. TLS 1.3 enforced by Azure App Service front-door. Azure Blob SDS files and report XML accessed via short-lived (15-minute) signed URLs; no public anonymous access. Compliance obligations: Tier II XML output validated against EPA Tier2 Submit XSD schema before storage; chemical disposal records include RCRA manifest numbers for hazardous waste shipment tracking.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipeline: flake8 lint, mypy type check, pytest Django test suite (80% coverage gate with factory_boy Chemical and inventory fixtures), Next.js ESLint and tsc type check, Docker build and push to Azure Container Registry, Azure App Service deployment via az webapp deploy. Database migrations run as pre-deployment script: python manage.py migrate --check in CI followed by automated migrate in deployment slot before traffic swap. Environments: staging (App Service Deployment Slot sharing App Service Plan; separate PostgreSQL database; Azure AD test app registration for SAML testing with test user accounts), production (App Service with auto-scale rule: add instance when CPU > 70% for 10 minutes; PostgreSQL Flexible Server General Purpose 4 vCores 32GB RAM with 35-day backup; Read Replica for Tier II report generation queries to avoid impacting transactional write performance). Celery worker deployed as separate App Service (Basic B2 tier, manual scale 1-2 workers). Azure Blob lifecycle policies: sds-documents no expiry; tier2-reports 10-year retention per RCRA.
target_output.observability: Application Insights SDK (opencensus-ext-azure) in Django: automatic HTTP request traces, dependency traces for PostgreSQL and Celery. Custom Application Insights events: TiER2_REPORT_GENERATED (properties: reporting_year, chemical_count, location_count, max_daily_amount_records, generation_duration_seconds), STORAGE_LIMIT_BREACH (properties: location_code, hazard_class, current_kg, limit_kg, breach_pct), CHEMICAL_RECEIPT_LOGGED (properties: cas_number, quantity_kg, storage_location_code). Azure Monitor alert rules: Tier II report Celery task failure (Application Insights customEvents where name='CELERY_TASK_FAILED' count > 0), storage limit breach alert for safety_coordinator email notification (STORAGE_LIMIT_BREACH event), p95 API latency > 5s on /api/v1/chemicals (inventory list page performance).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated Azure monthly cost: App Service P1v3 $140, Celery worker App Service B2 $60, PostgreSQL Flexible Server 4vCores General Purpose $200, Azure Cache for Redis C1 $50, Azure Blob Storage $20, Application Insights $30. Total approximately $500/month for a research campus with 500 chemicals and 50 active users. Auto-scale adds App Service instance at 70% CPU, handling peak load during pre-Tier II-deadline usage spikes in January-February.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Chemical inventory CRUD with CAS number lookup, receipt and usage event logging, storage location management with hazard class limits, storage limit hourly check Celery task with email alerts, Django admin panel for EHS officer, Azure AD SAML SSO, audit logging, Azure DevOps CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Tier II XML report generation Celery task with EPA Tier2 Submit XSD validation, HTML preview of Tier II report, Azure Blob report storage, HazCom chemical list Excel export, compliance calendar with reporting deadline reminders. Phase 3 (2 weeks): Disposal event recording with RCRA manifest tracking, SDS PDF upload and search, barcode scanner integration for receipt logging, Application Insights custom compliance event dashboard.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints required on all Django view functions and DRF serializers; mypy enforced in CI with django-stubs. Django model tests use factory_boy for Chemical, StorageLocation, and ChemicalReceipt factories; 80% coverage required. All quantity values stored in kg internally and converted to display units in serializers to avoid unit conversion errors in compliance calculations. Tier II XML generation must validate output against EPA Tier2 Submit XSD (publicly available) before Azure Blob upload. Celery tasks must be idempotent: Tier II report generation checks for existing draft report for the same year before creating new one. Django migrations must include backward migrations for rollback safety.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Maximum daily amount calculation for Tier II uses cumulative balance reconstruction from individual receipt, usage, and disposal events, which is computationally expensive for chemicals with years of transaction history. Mitigation: PostgreSQL window function with indexed event_date column; PostgreSQL Read Replica for Tier II generation queries to isolate from transactional workload; cache MDA results in Tier2Report record after first generation. Risk: Lab staff log usage events inconsistently or in batches rather than real-time, causing storage limit checks to lag actual quantities. Mitigation: End-of-day logging reminder email to lab staff; acknowledge that storage limit monitoring is indicative rather than real-time for compliance. Tradeoff: Django admin as EHS officer panel avoids custom admin UI development cost but limits UX customization; acceptable for the EHS officer persona who prioritizes functionality over aesthetics. Risk: EPA Tier2 Submit XML schema changes annually; mitigation: XSD version pinned in codebase with automated schema validation; EHS officer manual review before submission catches any structural issues.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: SDS PDFs will be uploaded manually by EHS officers rather than fetched from a third-party library API, deferring SDS API integration to a later phase. Quantities normalized to kg internally to simplify aggregation; unit conversion table provided for display in lbs if required by EHS preference. Django admin chosen as EHS panel over a custom React admin to meet timeline and avoid redundant UI development. PostgreSQL Read Replica provisioned for Tier II report generation to protect transactional response times during annual reporting season.

metadata.notes: Moderate: architecture is well-detailed with domain-specific compliance content; cost section is thinner than a strong plan and observability lacks per-chemical SLA metrics.

project_goal.value: Replace a legacy FTP-based data distribution system with a REST API exposing air quality, water chemistry, and soil contamination sensor readings from thousands of monitoring stations to researchers and regulatory agencies
project_goal.rationale: FTP system lacks query capabilities, versioning, and audit trail required by modern data consumers

target_users.value: Academic researchers, regulatory agency analysts, public health officials, and internal network operators
target_users.rationale: Four distinct consumer profiles with different query patterns and data volume needs

project_class.rationale: Headless data access service with no dedicated UI; all consumers integrate programmatically

capabilities.value: backend, data, auth, public_api, analytics, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Time-series data storage, authenticated and unauthenticated endpoints, bulk export jobs, analytics aggregations

complexity_level.rationale: Thousands of monitoring stations pushing readings continuously; multi-year historical datasets for bulk export

risk_level.rationale: Data inaccuracies could affect regulatory decisions; system availability is important but not life-critical

data_sensitivity.rationale: Monitoring data is non-personal environmental measurements; freely shareable under open data mandate

external_exposure.rationale: Open data mandate requires public read access; station ingest endpoints use API key authentication

access_model.value: Public read access for query endpoints with optional API key for higher rate limits; API key authentication required for monitoring station ingest endpoints and bulk export job creation
access_model.rationale: Open data policy for reads; authenticated ingest to prevent data poisoning

feature_scope.value: Station ingest endpoints, time-series query by station and parameter, bulk CSV export for multi-year datasets via async job, quality control flag application, station metadata management, usage analytics
feature_scope.rationale: Complete replacement of FTP distribution plus new capabilities

mvp_scope.value: Station data ingest, time-series query with pagination, and single-station single-parameter export
mvp_scope.rationale: Core data access replacing FTP for highest-volume consumer workflows

security_baseline.value: API key authentication for ingest and bulk export, HTTPS enforced, rate limiting per client, input validation to prevent malformed sensor data ingestion, no personal data processed
security_baseline.rationale: Standard public API security posture; no sensitive personal data requires lighter controls

frontend_stack.value: None - API service only; no dedicated frontend provided
frontend_stack.rationale: Consumers are programmatic; no browser UI required

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Chi router framework
backend_stack.rationale: Go provides excellent concurrency for high-throughput ingest; Chi is lightweight for REST routing

data_platform.value: TimescaleDB (PostgreSQL extension) for time-series sensor readings; Redis 7 for query result caching and rate limit counters
data_platform.rationale: TimescaleDB hypertables provide automatic time-based partitioning for sensor data; continuous aggregates for fast historical queries

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in us-east-1
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with auto-scaling; existing AWS infrastructure for the monitoring network

privacy_retention_policy.value: Sensor readings retained indefinitely as scientific record; bulk export jobs retained 7 days for download then deleted; no personal data collected
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Environmental monitoring data has permanent scientific value; temporary export files avoid storage bloat

future_scope.value: Real-time WebSocket streaming for live monitoring dashboards, anomaly detection alerting, federated data sharing with international monitoring networks
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP enhancements building on the API foundation

constraints.value: Must support ingesting readings from 5000+ monitoring stations; p95 query latency under 500ms for time-range queries spanning up to 30 days; bulk export jobs must complete within 30 minutes for 5-year multi-station datasets
constraints.rationale: Performance requirements derived from heaviest current consumer workflows

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM, structured JSON logging, custom ingest throughput and query latency metrics
observability_baseline.rationale: Datadog is existing corporate monitoring standard

execution_preference.value: Incremental delivery with station ingest and query endpoints first, bulk export second
execution_preference.rationale: Unblocks station migration from FTP as quickly as possible

project.goal: Replace FTP data distribution with REST API for environmental monitoring network
project.target_users: Researchers, regulators, public health officials, network operators
project.timeline: MVP ingest and query in 10 weeks

backend.framework: Chi router with sqlx for database access

security.auth: API key authentication for ingest and bulk export; public read for query endpoints
security.validation: Request body validation with schema enforcement on all ingest payloads

data.cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for query caching and rate limits

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

open_questions.qc_flags: Define quality control flag schema with network operations team

reasoner_reviews.product: The FTP replacement framing is correct - researchers need queryable API access not file downloads. The quality control flag system is critical to include from day one; environmental monitoring data without QC flags is scientifically unusable. Bulk export async job model is the right approach for multi-year datasets that cannot fit in a single synchronous response.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: TimescaleDB hypertables with time-based partitioning on reading timestamp are the right choice for the ingest and query pattern. Continuous aggregates for hourly and daily roll-ups will dramatically reduce query execution time for multi-month ranges. The Go Chi service is appropriate but be careful with connection pool sizing for 5000-station concurrent ingest; connection pooling via PgBouncer or pgx pool configuration needs explicit attention.
reasoner_reviews.security: Station API keys must be scoped per monitoring station so a compromised key cannot inject data for other stations. Rate limiting should distinguish between station ingest traffic (high volume, authenticated) and researcher query traffic (variable volume, possibly anonymous) to prevent researcher traffic from affecting ingest SLA.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 5000+ station concurrent ingest may require horizontal scaling of the ingest handler. TimescaleDB continuous aggregates must be set up for daily and hourly roll-ups to meet 500ms p95 query latency for 30-day ranges without full table scans.

backend.service_design: Go Chi service with separate handler groups: IngestHandler for station data submission, QueryHandler for time-series reads, ExportHandler for async bulk jobs, StationHandler for metadata management, AdminHandler for API key management
backend.api_patterns: RESTful under /api/v1; cursor-based pagination on query endpoints using timestamp as cursor; async bulk export with job ID polling; OpenAPI 3.1 spec generated with swaggo
backend.background_jobs: BulkExportWorker: Go goroutine pool processing export jobs from SQS queue; streams TimescaleDB query results to S3 multipart upload; presigned download URL returned on completion
backend.scaling_notes: ECS Fargate auto-scaling on CPU 60% target; TimescaleDB connection pool via pgx with max 100 connections per service instance; ALB connection draining on scale-in to prevent in-flight ingest loss
backend.failure_handling: Ingest endpoint returns 429 with Retry-After header on rate limit; idempotency key on ingest POST prevents duplicate readings from station retries; export job status endpoint for polling

security.auth_design: API keys stored as SHA-256 hashes in api_keys table with station_id scope for ingest keys; bulk export keys scoped to specific consumer organization; public read requires no key
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limiting via Redis: 1000 readings/min per station ingest key, 60 req/min per anonymous query client by IP, 10 export job submissions/day per consumer key
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All ingest events logged with station_id, reading count, timestamp; query events logged with parameter set, time range, and client IP for usage analytics

data.entities: monitoring_stations (id, station_code, station_type ENUM air_quality/water_chemistry/soil, latitude, longitude, operator_org, commissioned_at, status), sensor_readings (time TIMESTAMPTZ, station_id, parameter VARCHAR, value FLOAT, unit VARCHAR, qc_flag ENUM raw/validated/suspect/invalid, ingested_at), measurement_parameters (id, parameter_code, display_name, unit, valid_min, valid_max, detection_limit), bulk_exports (id, status ENUM queued/processing/complete/failed, station_ids JSONB, parameters JSONB, start_date, end_date, s3_key, created_at, completed_at)
data.storage_design: TimescaleDB hypertable on sensor_readings partitioned by time (monthly chunks); continuous aggregates for hourly and daily roll-ups; monitoring_stations and parameters in standard PostgreSQL tables; bulk_exports in PostgreSQL; Redis for read cache of recent station readings (1-hour TTL)
data.retention_and_deletion: sensor_readings retained indefinitely in TimescaleDB; older chunks compressed via TimescaleDB columnar compression after 90 days; bulk_exports S3 files deleted after 7 days via S3 lifecycle rule

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate service (2-8 tasks, 2 vCPU 4GB) behind ALB in us-east-1a and us-east-1b; RDS PostgreSQL 16 with TimescaleDB extension multi-AZ; ElastiCache Redis cluster; SQS FIFO queue for export jobs; S3 bucket for export file storage
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go vet, golangci-lint, go test with 80% coverage, Docker build and push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy; Flyway migrations run as ECS one-off task before deploy
devops.environments: dev (single Fargate task, dev RDS), staging (2 tasks, staging RDS with production data subset), production (2-8 tasks, multi-AZ RDS)
