target_output.thinking_summary: High-scale environmental monitoring API replacing FTP. Key decisions: TimescaleDB hypertables with time partitioning and continuous aggregates for 500ms query SLA on 30-day ranges; Go Chi for high-throughput concurrent ingest from 5000+ stations; async bulk export to S3 via SQS worker for large historical datasets; station-scoped API keys to prevent cross-station data poisoning. No personal data simplifies security posture to rate limiting and API key authentication.
target_output.title: National Environmental Monitoring Network Data API
target_output.executive_summary: A high-scale Go 1.22/Chi REST API on AWS ECS Fargate that replaces FTP-based data distribution for a national environmental monitoring network of 5000+ stations. Exposes air quality, water chemistry, and soil contamination sensor readings via time-series query endpoints with public read access and API-key-authenticated ingest. TimescaleDB with hypertables and continuous aggregates powers sub-500ms queries across 30-day ranges. Bulk exports of multi-year multi-station datasets are processed asynchronously via SQS worker goroutines streaming results to S3 with presigned download URLs. Quality control flags are applied to all readings at ingest time with range checks and temporal consistency validation.
target_output.architecture_overview: Go Chi service deployed across 2-8 ECS Fargate tasks behind an ALB in two availability zones. Ingest path: monitoring stations POST batches of sensor readings via API-key-authenticated endpoints; the IngestHandler validates readings against measurement parameter range checks, applies QC flags, and bulk-inserts into TimescaleDB hypertable partitioned by time. Query path: public read endpoints return paginated time-series data for a station and parameter combination; Redis caches recent readings (1-hour TTL) to reduce DB load from repeated queries. Bulk export path: consumer POSTs export job request to SQS FIFO queue; BulkExportWorker goroutine pool streams TimescaleDB query results through S3 multipart upload; presigned download URL delivered via job status endpoint when complete. Redis also manages rate limit counters per station key and per anonymous IP.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Go 1.22. Router: Chi v5 with middleware for request ID, structured logging, and rate limiting. DB driver: pgx v5 with connection pool max 100 per service instance. Database: TimescaleDB on RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ db.r7g.xlarge. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster mode for rate limits and query cache. Queue: SQS FIFO for bulk export jobs. Storage: S3 for export files with lifecycle delete after 7 days. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, ECR, CloudWatch Logs to Datadog. CI: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint and go test.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Station Data Ingest: POST /api/v1/stations/:station_id/readings accepting batched readings with parameter, value, unit, and observation timestamp; applies QC range validation against measurement_parameters.valid_min/max; inserts into TimescaleDB hypertable. Time-Series Query: GET /api/v1/stations/:station_id/readings?parameter=PM2.5&start=&end= returns paginated readings with QC flags using cursor-based pagination on reading timestamp. Station Metadata: GET/PUT /api/v1/stations for station registration and metadata management. Bulk Export: POST /api/v1/exports creates async job for multi-station multi-year dataset; GET /api/v1/exports/:job_id polls status and retrieves presigned S3 download URL. QC Flag Update: PATCH /api/v1/stations/:station_id/readings/:time/qc-flag allows network operators to correct suspect flags.
target_output.system_components: IngestHandler: validates request body schema, checks station_id matches API key scope, validates each reading value against parameter valid_min/valid_max range, assigns qc_flag=raw for in-range or qc_flag=suspect for out-of-range readings, batch-inserts via pgx CopyFrom for throughput. TimescaleDB Hypertable: sensor_readings partitioned into monthly time chunks with columnar compression after 90 days; continuous aggregates hourly_readings_agg and daily_readings_agg materialized on schedule for fast range query performance. QueryHandler: queries hypertable or continuous aggregates depending on time range (hourly agg for >7 days, daily agg for >90 days); returns cursor-paginated response with next_cursor as encoded timestamp. BulkExportWorker: SQS message consumer running as goroutine pool of 10; streams TimescaleDB COPY output to S3 multipart upload; updates bulk_exports status and s3_key on completion. RateLimitMiddleware: reads and increments Redis counter keys keyed by API key ID or client IP; returns 429 with Retry-After on breach.
target_output.workflows: Monitoring Station Ingest: station firmware POSTs JSON batch to POST /api/v1/stations/STN-0042/readings with API key header; IngestHandler checks key hash against api_keys table, verifies station_id scope matches key, validates each reading against parameter range table, assigns qc_flags, executes pgx CopyFrom batch insert into sensor_readings hypertable; returns 201 with inserted count; Redis rate limit counter incremented; duplicate readings rejected via unique index on (station_id, parameter, time). Bulk Historical Export: researcher POSTs to POST /api/v1/exports with station_ids array, parameters array, start_date, end_date; server validates parameters exist in measurement_parameters table, checks export quota in Redis, inserts bulk_exports row with status=queued, publishes to SQS FIFO queue; researcher polls GET /api/v1/exports/JOB-123 for status; BulkExportWorker picks up message, streams SELECT station_id, parameter, time, value, qc_flag FROM sensor_readings WHERE station_id = ANY($1) AND time BETWEEN $2 AND $3 to S3 multipart CSV upload; on completion updates bulk_exports with s3_key and status=complete; researcher receives presigned URL valid for 24 hours.
target_output.data_model: monitoring_stations (id UUID PK, station_code VARCHAR UNIQUE, station_type ENUM air_quality/water_chemistry/soil, latitude FLOAT, longitude FLOAT, operator_org VARCHAR, commissioned_at DATE, status ENUM active/inactive/decommissioned). sensor_readings (time TIMESTAMPTZ, station_id UUID, parameter VARCHAR, value FLOAT, unit VARCHAR, qc_flag ENUM raw/validated/suspect/invalid, ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ, PRIMARY KEY (time, station_id, parameter)) - TimescaleDB hypertable partitioned by time monthly. measurement_parameters (id UUID PK, parameter_code VARCHAR UNIQUE, display_name VARCHAR, unit VARCHAR, valid_min FLOAT, valid_max FLOAT, detection_limit FLOAT, medium ENUM air/water/soil). bulk_exports (id UUID PK, api_key_id UUID FK, station_ids JSONB, parameters JSONB, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, status ENUM queued/processing/complete/failed, s3_key VARCHAR, row_count BIGINT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). api_keys (id UUID PK, key_hash CHAR(64), station_id UUID FK nullable, org_name VARCHAR, scopes JSONB, last_used_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/stations/:station_id/readings (API key required, station scope) - batch ingest up to 500 readings per request. GET /api/v1/stations/:station_id/readings?parameter=PM2.5&start=2026-01-01T00:00Z&end=2026-01-31T23:59Z&limit=1000&cursor= (public) - paginated time-series query. GET /api/v1/stations/:station_id/latest (public) - most recent readings per parameter. GET /api/v1/stations (public) - station catalogue with optional bbox and type filters. POST /api/v1/exports (API key required) - create async bulk export job. GET /api/v1/exports/:job_id (API key required) - poll export status and retrieve download URL. PATCH /api/v1/readings/qc (operator scope API key) - batch update QC flags. GET /api/v1/parameters - list supported measurement parameters with valid ranges.
target_output.security_and_compliance: API Keys: stored as SHA-256 hashes in api_keys table; station ingest keys carry station_id scope preventing cross-station data injection; org keys for bulk export carry org_name for audit logging. Public read endpoints require no authentication. Rate Limiting: Redis counters per key or IP; station ingest keys 1000 readings/min; anonymous query clients 60 req/min by IP; bulk export 10 jobs/day per org key. Input Validation: all ingest payloads validated against measurement_parameters.valid_min/max; parameter codes validated against reference table; timestamp in ISO 8601 required; unrecognized fields rejected. HTTPS enforced by ALB with HTTPS-only listener; HTTP redirected. No personal data processed so no GDPR or HIPAA obligations. Open data mandate satisfied by public read access without authentication.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate service with 2 minimum tasks and 8 maximum tasks, auto-scaling on ALB RequestCountPerTarget 1000 req/min. Each task: 2 vCPU, 4GB memory, 30-second health check interval on GET /health. ALB in us-east-1a and us-east-1b for availability. RDS PostgreSQL 16 with TimescaleDB extension Multi-AZ db.r7g.xlarge (16 vCPU, 32GB) for query throughput; read replica for bulk export queries. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster mode (2 shards, 1 replica per shard) for rate limits and query cache. SQS FIFO queue with 24-hour message visibility timeout for export jobs. S3 bucket for export files with 7-day lifecycle deletion. GitHub Actions pipeline: go vet, golangci-lint, go test 80% coverage gate, Docker build and push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy with minimum healthy percent 50. Flyway schema migrations run as ECS scheduled task before deploy. Rollback: ECS set desired count to 0 on current task definition and increment prior revision.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM agent as ECS sidecar injecting traces into all Go handlers. Custom Datadog metrics: ingest_readings_per_second by station type (gauge), query_duration_milliseconds histogram per endpoint, export_job_queue_depth (SQS approximate messages), qc_flag_distribution by flag type (counter). Structured JSON logs to CloudWatch Logs with Datadog forwarder: correlation ID per request, station_id and parameter on ingest logs, job_id on export logs. Monitors: p95 ingest latency >200ms triggers warning, >500ms triggers critical; export job queue depth >50 triggers page; RDS CPU >70% for 10 minutes triggers investigation alert; TimescaleDB compression lag >24 hours triggers alert.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $2100/month at 5000-station production load: ECS Fargate 4 average tasks $480, RDS Multi-AZ db.r7g.xlarge $1100, RDS read replica $550, ElastiCache Redis cluster $200, SQS and S3 export storage $50, ALB and data transfer $120, ECR and CloudWatch $50, Datadog $150 incremental. TimescaleDB columnar compression reduces storage by 90% for chunks older than 90 days. Read replica dedicated for bulk export queries removes impact on transactional read path. Fargate auto-scales 2-8 tasks on ALB request count.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): station registration and metadata API, sensor reading ingest endpoint with QC range validation, time-series query endpoint with cursor pagination, TimescaleDB hypertable setup with monthly partitioning, Redis rate limiting, API key management, ECS Fargate deployment with multi-AZ RDS, Datadog APM integration. Phase 2 (6 weeks): bulk export async job system with SQS and S3, BulkExportWorker goroutine pool, presigned download URL delivery, TimescaleDB continuous aggregates for hourly and daily roll-ups improving long-range query performance. Phase 3 (8 weeks): QC flag workflow for operator corrections, read replica routing for bulk exports, TimescaleDB columnar compression automation, usage analytics dashboard data endpoints.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go 1.22 with golangci-lint enforcing all standard linters. Explicit error handling with no blank identifiers on error returns. Table-driven unit tests for IngestHandler QC validation logic and QueryHandler pagination logic. Integration tests against real TimescaleDB dev instance using testcontainers-go. pgx CopyFrom required for all bulk inserts; never use individual INSERT statements for reading batches. All Redis keys must include expiry on SET. OpenAPI spec (swaggo-generated) committed to repository and published to API portal on merge to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TimescaleDB continuous aggregate refresh lag causes stale data in long-range queries during high ingest periods; mitigation: monitor aggregate refresh completion time and alert if lagging more than 1 hour, fall back to base hypertable scan for affected time ranges. Risk: 5000-station concurrent ingest causes database connection pool exhaustion; mitigation: pgx pool max 100 connections per Fargate task, horizontal Fargate scaling on ALB request count, PgBouncer pooler considered if connections exceed DB limits. Tradeoff: TimescaleDB over pure InfluxDB chosen for SQL compatibility with existing analytics tooling and bulk export SQL streaming; at cost of more complex operational setup than managed InfluxDB. Tradeoff: Go Chi over FastAPI chosen for better concurrency under high-throughput ingest load at cost of larger codebase.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: QC flag schema confirmed as ENUM raw/validated/suspect/invalid with range-check logic against measurement_parameters at ingest. Open data mandate confirmed as requiring public read access for query endpoints without API key. TimescaleDB extension approved for use on RDS PostgreSQL 16 by DBA team. Export file format confirmed as CSV with header row per station parameter combination.

metadata.notes: High-scale environmental monitoring API replacing FTP. Go Chi on ECS Fargate with TimescaleDB and Redis. Flawed: risk_level=medium but no compliance_context field despite public-internet EPA/Clean Air Act context; intentionally omitted to create flawed training example.

project_goal.value: A cost-tracking and budget management fullstack application for research principal investigators to monitor grant expenditures across multiple funding sources, track personnel effort allocations, and forecast remaining runway against approved budgets
project_goal.rationale: PIs currently manage grant finances in spreadsheets leading to effort certification errors and budget overruns

target_users.value: Principal investigators, lab managers, research financial administrators, and sponsored programs office staff
target_users.rationale: Four roles with different views: PIs need budget overview, admins need transaction detail, SPO needs compliance reports

project_class.rationale: Requires both a financial dashboard frontend and a backend API with university financial system integration

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Dashboard UI, budget computation backend, financial data persistence, institutional SSO, analytics charts, university system integration

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-grant budget tracking with effort certification workflows adds logic complexity; no real-time requirements

risk_level.rationale: Incorrect effort certification reports could trigger audit findings under federal grant regulations

data_sensitivity.rationale: Personnel salary and effort allocation data is sensitive financial and HR information requiring access control

external_exposure.rationale: Only authenticated university community members access grant financial data

access_model.value: Google OAuth via university institutional SSO; RBAC with PI, lab_manager, financial_admin, and spo_staff roles controlling data visibility per grant
access_model.rationale: Institutional SSO for identity; grant-level RBAC ensures PIs only see their own grants

feature_scope.value: Multi-grant budget dashboard, expenditure tracking by category, personnel effort allocation by grant percentage, monthly effort certification workflow, budget variance alerts, financial system data sync, runway forecasting
feature_scope.rationale: Complete feature set addressing current spreadsheet pain points

mvp_scope.value: Budget dashboard with expenditure summary per grant, financial system sync, and budget category variance alerts
mvp_scope.rationale: Replaces most critical spreadsheet workflow and demonstrates value quickly

security_baseline.value: Google OAuth JWT validation, grant-level row security in PostgreSQL, HTTPS enforced, audit log of financial data access
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data requires access controls and auditability

frontend_stack.rationale: SSR for initial page load performance; React ecosystem for chart libraries; institutional familiarity with Next.js

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async performance; Python ecosystem for financial computation; team expertise

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for all grant financial data
data_platform.rationale: Relational data suits budget categories, allocation percentages, and audit trails; no caching needed at current scale

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for frontend and backend
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless containers match sporadic usage pattern of academic financial workflows

privacy_retention_policy.value: Grant financial records retained 7 years per OMB Uniform Guidance; effort certifications retained 7 years; salary data masked in display for non-admin roles
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Federal grant regulations mandate 7-year financial record retention

future_scope.value: No-code budget projection scenario modeling, integration with NIH Commons for grant profile data, mobile-responsive PI dashboard
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP enhancements once core workflow is validated

constraints.value: Must integrate with university Oracle Financials system via OAuth-protected REST API for expenditure sync; no salary amounts displayed to non-admin roles; budget under $300/month on GCP
constraints.rationale: Existing financial system landscape; privacy policy for salary data; cloud cost constraint

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run metrics, structured JSON logging to Cloud Logging
observability_baseline.rationale: Native GCP observability stack

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; backend API and financial system integration first to unblock frontend development
execution_preference.rationale: Integration-first approach reduces risk of dependency on Oracle Financials API

compliance_context.value: OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200 for federal grant financial management; effort reporting compliance requirements; institutional cost accounting standards; DHHS effort certification requirements for NIH-funded grants
compliance_context.rationale: Application handles federal grant financial data requiring specific effort reporting compliance

project.goal: Multi-grant budget tracking and effort certification for research PIs
project.target_users: PIs, lab managers, financial admins, sponsored programs office

security.auth: Google OAuth via university SSO

devops.environments: dev, staging, production

constraints.salary_masking: Non-admin roles cannot see salary amounts

open_questions.oracle_api: Oracle Financials API schema and auth method needs confirmation

reasoner_reviews.product: The effort certification workflow is the highest-compliance-risk feature and should be designed carefully. OMB Uniform Guidance requires certifications to be completed by the employee or responsible official with supervisory knowledge - the system must support both self-certification and supervisor sign-off. The salary-masking constraint for non-admin roles must be enforced at the database level not just the UI.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: FastAPI with SQLAlchemy on Cloud Run is appropriate for the access pattern. The Oracle Financials integration is the highest technical risk - get the API credentials and schema documentation early in Sprint 1. Row-level security in PostgreSQL is the correct approach for grant-level data isolation; implement via PostgreSQL RLS policies on grants and expenditure tables rather than application-layer filtering.
reasoner_reviews.security: PostgreSQL row-level security must be used for grant data isolation - application-layer filtering is insufficient for sensitive financial data. Salary amounts must be encrypted at rest or stored as masked values; prefer encryption with decryption only on explicit admin fetch. All financial data access events should be written to a dedicated audit log table.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: $300/month on GCP is tight for Cloud Run with Cloud SQL. Cloud SQL db-f1-micro is insufficient; use db-g1-small shared-core. Serverless Cloud Run min-instances=0 will help with cost on the sporadic usage pattern.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with domain modules: grants (CRUD and metadata), expenditures (sync from Oracle Financials and manual entry), effort_allocations (personnel effort assignments per grant), certifications (effort certification workflow), forecasts (runway computation)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful under /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.0 spec; grant-scoped URL nesting for expenditures and allocations; async OAuth refresh for Oracle Financials token management
backend.background_jobs: Oracle Financials Sync: nightly Cloud Run job pulling new expenditure records from Oracle REST API for all active grants; updates expenditure_records and recomputes budget category totals. Monthly Certification Trigger: sends certification request emails to personnel at month-end via SendGrid.
backend.failure_handling: Oracle Financials API timeout retried with exponential backoff; sync failures logged to sync_errors table with detailed message for manual review; certification workflow state machine prevents invalid transitions

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with route groups: (dashboard) for PI views, (admin) for financial_admin views, (spo) for sponsored programs staff views
frontend.pages_and_flows: Grant Portfolio Dashboard: card-per-grant showing total budget, spent, and remaining with burn rate trend. Grant Detail: budget category breakdown, expenditure list, personnel allocations chart. Effort Certification: table of lab members with monthly allocation percentages and certification status badges. Financial Admin: full expenditure detail with salary amounts for admin role.
frontend.state_management: React Query for server state with grant-level cache keys; no global state needed beyond auth context
frontend.performance_notes: Next.js SSR for initial grant portfolio load to avoid loading state on first visit; client-side React Query refetch for stale data

security.auth_design: NextAuth.js with Google OAuth provider; session stored as JWT; FastAPI backend verifies JWT on each request
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL row-level security policies: PI sees grants where user_id = auth.uid(); financial_admin sees all grants in their department; spo_staff reads all grants. Salary masking: expenditure_records stores raw salary_amount_cents; FastAPI returns null for salary fields when role != financial_admin
security.audit_and_logging_controls: financial_access_log table recording user_id, grant_id, action type, and timestamp for all expenditure and certification reads and writes

data.entities: grants (id, award_number, title, pi_user_id, funding_agency, total_budget_cents, start_date, end_date, status), budget_categories (id, grant_id, category_name ENUM personnel/fringe/travel/supplies/indirect, approved_amount_cents, spent_amount_cents), expenditure_records (id, grant_id, budget_category_id, transaction_date, description, amount_cents, salary_amount_cents nullable, oracle_transaction_id, source ENUM oracle_sync/manual), personnel_allocations (id, grant_id, user_id, role_title, effort_percent INT, period_start DATE, period_end DATE), effort_certifications (id, allocation_id, certification_period CHAR(7), certified_by UUID, certified_at TIMESTAMPTZ, status ENUM pending/certified/supervisor_approved), forecast_models (id, grant_id, computed_at, monthly_burn_rate_cents, months_remaining FLOAT)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL db-g1-small with RLS policies on grants and expenditure_records tables; no caching layer needed at projected user count
data.retention_and_deletion: Grants and expenditures retained 7 years post-grant-end per OMB Uniform Guidance; effort_certifications retained 7 years; archive job moves closed grants to cold storage after 7 years

devops.deployment_topology: Two Cloud Run services: next-frontend (Next.js 14, min 0, max 5) and fastapi-backend (Python 3.12, min 0, max 5); Cloud SQL PostgreSQL db-g1-small shared-core; Cloud Run jobs for nightly Oracle sync and monthly certification trigger
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: frontend (eslint, tsc, vitest), backend (ruff, mypy, pytest 80% coverage), Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, deploy both Cloud Run services
devops.environments: dev (auto-deploy on dev branch), staging (manual promote for PI acceptance testing), production (manual promote)

target_output.thinking_summary: Research grant budget management fullstack app with financial data sensitivity. Key decisions: PostgreSQL row-level security for grant data isolation (not application-layer filtering), salary amount encryption with role-based decryption only for financial_admin, Oracle Financials integration as nightly sync job to avoid synchronous dependency, Next.js SSR for initial dashboard load performance. OMB Uniform Guidance compliance requires 7-year retention and effort certification with supervisor sign-off capability.
target_output.title: Research Grant Budget Management Platform
target_output.executive_summary: A Next.js 14 and FastAPI fullstack application on GCP Cloud Run for principal investigators to monitor grant expenditures across multiple federal and non-federal funding sources, track personnel effort allocations, and complete monthly effort certifications in compliance with OMB Uniform Guidance 2 CFR 200. Integrates with the university Oracle Financials system via nightly sync for automatic expenditure ingestion. PostgreSQL row-level security enforces grant-level data isolation; salary amounts are visible only to financial_admin role. Runway forecasting computes months remaining based on current monthly burn rate per grant.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two Cloud Run services: a Next.js 14 frontend with App Router serving server-rendered grant portfolio dashboards and an authenticated FastAPI backend providing the grant financial API. Google OAuth via university institutional SSO authenticates users; NextAuth.js manages sessions as JWTs verified by FastAPI on each request. PostgreSQL row-level security policies on grants and expenditure_records enforce that PIs only access their own grants and financial_admin roles see department-wide data. Nightly Cloud Run job syncs expenditure records from Oracle Financials REST API into expenditure_records table, updating budget_categories.spent_amount_cents and triggering variance alert evaluation. Monthly Cloud Run job dispatches effort certification requests to personnel via SendGrid when period-end is detected.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query for server state, Recharts for budget trend and effort allocation visualizations, NextAuth.js for Google OAuth session management. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 with asyncpg driver, Alembic for migrations, Pydantic v2 for request validation. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 db-g1-small shared-core with row-level security. Email: SendGrid for effort certification notifications. Infrastructure: Cloud Run (frontend and backend services, plus two Cloud Run jobs), Artifact Registry, Cloud Logging.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Grant Portfolio Dashboard: server-rendered list of PI grants with budget-spent-remaining summary cards and 3-month burn rate trend sparklines. Grant Budget Detail: budget category breakdown (personnel, fringe, travel, supplies, indirect) with approved vs spent bar charts and category-level variance alerts. Expenditure Transaction Log: paginated expenditure records with Oracle transaction ID, date, description, and amount; salary amounts masked for non-admin roles. Personnel Effort Allocation: lab member allocation percentages per grant with total effort sanity check (sum must not exceed 100%). Monthly Effort Certification: certification checklist by lab member and grant showing pending/certified/supervisor_approved status; certification action with required supervisory context for non-self-certifications. Budget Runway Forecast: computed months remaining at current burn rate with configurable scenario adjustment.
target_output.system_components: Next.js App Router groups: (dashboard) for PI and lab manager routes with Next.js SSR on initial portfolio load; (admin) for financial_admin routes with full salary visibility; (spo) for sponsored programs staff read-only views. FastAPI grant module: CRUD for grants with RLS-enforced SQLAlchemy queries. FastAPI expenditure module: paginated expenditure queries with salary field nulled on role != financial_admin; manual expenditure creation endpoint. FastAPI certification module: state machine for pending/certified/supervisor_approved transitions with timestamp and actor recording. OracleFinancialsSync (Cloud Run job, nightly): authenticates to Oracle Financials OAuth endpoint, fetches new transactions since last sync cursor, upserts expenditure_records, updates budget_categories.spent_amount_cents, evaluates budget_variance_alerts. PostgreSQL RLS policies: grants table policy WHERE pi_user_id = current_setting('app.user_id')::uuid OR department_id = current_setting('app.department_id')::uuid; expenditure_records policy inherits from grant policy.
target_output.workflows: Expenditure Sync Workflow: nightly Cloud Run job authenticates to Oracle Financials via OAuth 2.0 client credentials; GETs /api/financials/transactions?modified_since={last_sync_cursor}; for each transaction finds matching grant by award_number; classifies to budget_category by account code mapping; upserts expenditure_records with oracle_transaction_id deduplication; recomputes budget_categories.spent_amount_cents; evaluates each budget category for overspend variance alert, inserting variance_alerts rows where spent > approved * 0.9; updates sync_cursors table with latest successful timestamp. Monthly Effort Certification Workflow: Cloud Run job triggers on first business day of each month; queries personnel_allocations for active allocations in prior month; inserts effort_certifications rows with status=pending for each allocation; dispatches SendGrid email to each lab member with certification link listing their grant allocations and expected effort percentages; PI receives summary of pending certifications dashboard widget; lab members click link, review allocations, and certify; PIs review and supervisor-approve for personnel under their supervision; spo_staff can view all certifications and flag missing ones for follow-up.
target_output.data_model: grants (id UUID PK, award_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, title VARCHAR, pi_user_id UUID FK, department_id UUID FK, funding_agency VARCHAR, total_budget_cents BIGINT, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, status ENUM active/no_cost_extension/closed, oracle_project_id VARCHAR). budget_categories (id UUID PK, grant_id UUID FK, category_name ENUM personnel/fringe/travel/supplies/indirect/other_direct, approved_amount_cents BIGINT, spent_amount_cents BIGINT). expenditure_records (id UUID PK, grant_id UUID FK, budget_category_id UUID FK, transaction_date DATE, description VARCHAR, amount_cents BIGINT, salary_amount_cents BIGINT nullable encrypted, oracle_transaction_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, source ENUM oracle_sync/manual). personnel_allocations (id UUID PK, grant_id UUID FK, user_id UUID FK, role_title VARCHAR, effort_percent INT CHECK between 1 and 100, period_start DATE, period_end DATE). effort_certifications (id UUID PK, allocation_id UUID FK, certification_period CHAR(7), certified_by UUID FK, certified_at TIMESTAMPTZ, supervisor_approved_by UUID FK nullable, supervisor_approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable, status ENUM pending/certified/supervisor_approved). forecast_models (id UUID PK, grant_id UUID FK, computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, monthly_burn_rate_cents BIGINT, months_remaining FLOAT). variance_alerts (id UUID PK, budget_category_id UUID FK, alert_type ENUM approaching_limit/over_limit, threshold_percent INT, triggered_at TIMESTAMPTZ, acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ nullable). users (id UUID PK, google_sub VARCHAR UNIQUE, email VARCHAR, display_name VARCHAR, role ENUM pi/lab_manager/financial_admin/spo_staff, department_id UUID FK).
target_output.api_design: GET /api/v1/grants returns PI-scoped grant list with budget summary. GET /api/v1/grants/:id returns grant detail with budget categories and current spend. GET /api/v1/grants/:id/expenditures returns paginated expenditures (salary fields nulled for non-admin). POST /api/v1/grants/:id/expenditures creates manual expenditure entry. GET /api/v1/grants/:id/allocations returns personnel effort allocations. PUT /api/v1/grants/:id/allocations/:alloc_id updates effort percentage. GET /api/v1/certifications?period=2026-03 returns certification status per allocation for authenticated user. POST /api/v1/certifications/:id/certify records self-certification. POST /api/v1/certifications/:id/supervisor-approve records supervisor approval. GET /api/v1/grants/:id/forecast returns runway computation. GET /api/v1/alerts returns unacknowledged variance alerts for PI.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Authentication: NextAuth.js Google OAuth with university hd domain restriction; JWT forwarded to FastAPI in Authorization header; FastAPI verifies signature and extracts user_id and role claim. Row-Level Security: PostgreSQL RLS policies set on grants and expenditure_records with app.user_id and app.department_id session variables set by FastAPI before each query execution; prevents cross-PI data leakage even if application-layer query is incorrect. Salary Data: salary_amount_cents stored encrypted using PostgreSQL pgcrypto symmetric encryption; decrypted only by financial_admin queries; all other roles receive NULL. Audit Logging: financial_access_log table records user_id, grant_id, action ENUM (view_expenditures, create_expenditure, certify_effort, approve_certification), and timestamp for all financial operations. HTTPS enforced by Cloud Run load balancer. OMB Compliance: effort certifications include certified_by, certified_at, supervisor_approved_by, and supervisor_approved_at for regulatory audit requirements.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Two Cloud Run services deployed in parallel: next-frontend (min 0, max 5 instances, 512MB 1 vCPU) and fastapi-backend (min 0, max 5 instances, 512MB 1 vCPU). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 db-g1-small shared-core with automated backups and 7-day point-in-time recovery. Cloud Run jobs: oracle-financials-sync (Cloud Scheduler trigger 02:00 UTC nightly) and certification-trigger (Cloud Scheduler first business day of month 08:00 UTC). GitHub Actions CI: frontend (eslint, tsc, vitest 80% coverage), backend (ruff, mypy, pytest 80% coverage), Docker build and push to Artifact Registry, gcloud run deploy for both services. Alembic migrations run via Cloud Run job before deploy. Environments: dev (auto-deploy on dev branch push), staging (manual approve for PI acceptance testing), production (manual approve). Rollback: gcloud run services update-traffic to prior revision.
target_output.observability: Cloud Monitoring: Cloud Run request count, p50/p99 latency, error rate, and instance count for both services. Cloud Logging: structured JSON logs with user_id and grant_id on all API calls for debug tracing. Custom log-based metrics: oracle_sync_new_transactions_count (counter per nightly run), certification_completion_rate (gauge per period computed day after deadline), variance_alerts_triggered (counter). Alerts: FastAPI error rate above 2% in 5 minutes triggers Slack webhook; Oracle sync job failure triggers email to IT liaison; Cloud SQL CPU above 80% for 10 minutes triggers investigation alert.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $180/month: Cloud Run frontend and backend approximately $60 combined for sporadic academic usage pattern, Cloud SQL db-g1-small approximately $75, Cloud Scheduler and Cloud Run jobs approximately $15, Artifact Registry approximately $10, Cloud Logging approximately $10, SendGrid Essentials approximately $10 for monthly certification emails. Within $300/month budget with headroom. Cloud Run auto-scales on request volume; at projected user count (200 PIs, 50 admins) no database sharding needed.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Oracle Financials sync job, grant portfolio dashboard with expenditure summary, budget category variance alerts, Google OAuth with RLS, FastAPI backend with OpenAPI spec, Cloud Run deployment with three environments, 80% test coverage. Phase 2 (6 weeks): effort allocation management UI, monthly effort certification workflow with SendGrid email dispatch, supervisor approval flow, certification report export for SPO. Phase 3 (6 weeks): budget runway forecast computation and visualization, scenario modeling for budget projections, salary data encryption implementation for financial_admin, audit log dashboard for SPO compliance reporting.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode on Next.js frontend. Python 3.12 with mypy strict type checking on FastAPI backend. PostgreSQL RLS policies tested in integration tests by asserting cross-user data isolation using pytest fixtures with two different user sessions. Salary amount fields never logged or serialized when role is non-admin; enforced by Pydantic response model conditional field exclusion. Oracle Financials sync uses upsert with oracle_transaction_id as idempotency key; never duplicates on re-run. Alembic migrations committed to repository; DDL changes require review by lead engineer. Effort percent validation: sum of active allocations for a user cannot exceed 100%; enforced in application logic with check constraint on individual allocation.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Oracle Financials REST API schema or auth method changes break nightly sync silently; mitigation: Pydantic schema validation on Oracle API response with sync job failure alert and last_successful_sync_at monitoring. Risk: PostgreSQL RLS misconfiguration allows cross-PI data leakage for edge case roles; mitigation: integration tests with explicit cross-user isolation assertions on all sensitive endpoints. Tradeoff: nightly sync over real-time Oracle webhook chosen for simplicity and Oracle API availability constraints; PIs see expenditure data with up to 24-hour lag which is acceptable for grant management workflows. Tradeoff: salary encryption added in Phase 3 over Day 1 to unblock MVP delivery; Phase 1 salary fields are stored as plaintext with strict RLS access control as interim measure with clear migration path.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Oracle Financials uses OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow for server-to-server authentication confirmed by IT. Budget category to Oracle account code mapping table confirmed as available from financial systems team. Effort certification requirement: both self-certification and supervisor approval required for personnel paid from federal grants per DHHS guidelines confirmed by compliance team. Google OAuth institutional SSO confirmed to support hd domain restriction parameter.

metadata.notes: Research grant budget management with OMB Uniform Guidance compliance. Next.js 14 and FastAPI on GCP Cloud Run with PostgreSQL RLS for financial data isolation.

project_goal.value: A static documentation and knowledge-base website for an open-source scientific workflow management framework, providing searchable API reference docs, tutorial guides, example workflow galleries, and community contribution guides for computational scientists
project_goal.rationale: Documentation site is the primary growth driver for framework adoption; existing docs are scattered in markdown files without search or versioning

target_users.value: Computational scientists and bioinformaticians using the framework, potential adopters evaluating it, and community contributors improving the framework
target_users.rationale: Three distinct user journeys: users finding specific API docs, evaluators assessing capabilities, contributors finding contribution guidelines

project_class.rationale: Documentation sites are purely static; no server-side state, no authentication, no database

capabilities.rationale: Static content rendering and anonymous page view analytics are the only runtime requirements

complexity_level.rationale: No authentication, database, or server-side logic; complexity is in build pipeline for API doc generation and versioning

risk_level.rationale: Public documentation site with no user data, no authentication, and no third-party payment processing

data_sensitivity.rationale: No personal or sensitive data; only public documentation content

external_exposure.rationale: Public-facing documentation site with no access restrictions

access_model.value: Fully public with no authentication; no user data collected; Cloudflare Analytics for privacy-preserving page view tracking
access_model.rationale: Open source documentation should be accessible without registration

feature_scope.value: Versioned API reference documentation auto-generated from source code docstrings, searchable tutorial guides, example workflow gallery with embedded code, community contribution guide, version switcher for multiple release branches
feature_scope.rationale: All features needed to support the three user journeys

mvp_scope.value: API reference docs auto-generated from source, basic tutorial section, and version switcher for two latest releases
mvp_scope.rationale: API reference is the highest-value content for existing users; version switcher is critical for users on older releases

security_baseline.value: HTTPS enforced via Cloudflare Pages, CSP headers blocking inline scripts, no server-side secrets, no user input forms
security_baseline.rationale: Standard static site security posture with no attack surface beyond CDN delivery

frontend_stack.rationale: Docusaurus is purpose-built for open source project documentation with native versioning, MDX support, Algolia DocSearch integration, and React plugin ecosystem

backend_stack.value: None - static site with CI-driven API doc generation from Python source docstrings
backend_stack.rationale: No runtime backend required; API docs generated at CI time from framework source

data_platform.value: None - all content is markdown files and auto-generated MDX in the repository
data_platform.rationale: Static content eliminates runtime data dependency

hosting_target.value: Cloudflare Pages with global CDN
hosting_target.rationale: Free tier for open source projects; global edge delivery; zero configuration HTTPS

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected; Cloudflare Analytics collects anonymized aggregate page views only; no cookies set
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Privacy-first analytics avoids any GDPR cookie consent obligation

future_scope.value: Algolia DocSearch integration for full-text search, interactive workflow playground embedded in tutorials, localization for Chinese and German scientific communities
future_scope.rationale: Post-launch documentation quality improvements

constraints.value: Build must complete within 4 minutes; API reference must auto-generate from Python docstrings without manual editing; versioned docs must support at least 3 simultaneous release versions
constraints.rationale: CI speed and documentation automation are core requirements

observability_baseline.value: Cloudflare Analytics for page views and country distribution; GitHub Actions build status badges; Lighthouse CI for performance regression detection
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight observability appropriate for open source documentation

execution_preference.value: Continuous deployment on main branch merge; tagged releases trigger versioned doc build and publication
execution_preference.rationale: Documentation must stay in sync with framework releases

project.goal: Static documentation site for scientific workflow framework with API doc generation and versioning
project.target_users: Computational scientists, evaluators, contributors

frontend.styling: Default Docusaurus theme customized with framework branding colors
frontend.search: Local search (lunr) for MVP, Algolia DocSearch in future

security.headers: CSP and security headers via Cloudflare Pages _headers file
security.analytics: Cloudflare Analytics (no cookies, no consent required)

data.source: MDX files in repository plus auto-generated API reference MDX from Python docstrings

devops.environments: Cloudflare Pages branch previews per PR, production on main

confirmed_decisions.api_doc_tool: Sphinx with MyST-Parser for MDX output

reasoner_reviews.product: Docusaurus 3 is the correct choice for this project - its native versioning system handles multiple release branches, MDX support enables interactive examples, and the React plugin ecosystem allows customization. The auto-generation pipeline from Python docstrings is the highest-complexity build step and needs validation early.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The sphinx-to-MDX pipeline for API reference generation needs careful design. Sphinx autodoc generates RST which requires pandoc conversion to MDX, or alternatively use sphinx-docusaurus plugin directly. The versioning model should follow Docusaurus conventions: versioned docs in versioned_docs/version-2.x/ directories created via docusaurus docs:version CLI command on each tagged release.
reasoner_reviews.security: No server-side secrets or user data means the attack surface is essentially zero beyond CDN delivery. CSP headers should disallow all script sources except self and any CDN domains used for fonts. No forms or user input means no XSS injection risk beyond content authoring.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 4-minute build constraint is achievable with Sphinx autodoc generation taking roughly 90 seconds for the framework source, followed by Docusaurus build. Caching the Sphinx output between builds when source files are unchanged will keep incremental builds under 2 minutes.

frontend.app_structure: Docusaurus 3 project with docs/ for tutorial guides, api-reference/ for auto-generated MDX (gitignored, generated in CI), blog/ for release announcements, src/pages/ for homepage and example gallery
frontend.pages_and_flows: Homepage: hero section with quick-start code snippet, framework capability highlights, community stats. Tutorial section: Getting Started, Core Concepts, Advanced Patterns guide sequence. API Reference: auto-generated per module with cross-links between related functions and classes. Example Workflow Gallery: MDX pages with embedded runnable code snippets and download links. Contribution Guide: fork-to-PR workflow with code style and test requirements.
frontend.performance_notes: Docusaurus production build generates optimized static HTML with code splitting per page; lazy-load images in example gallery; preconnect hints for CDN font domains
frontend.frontend_risks: Auto-generated API reference MDX may have formatting inconsistencies if docstring coverage is incomplete; mitigation: CI step validating docstring coverage minimum 80% before site build

security.auth_design: No authentication required - fully public
security.abuse_prevention: Cloudflare WAF basic rules protecting Cloudflare Pages origin; no forms means no spam attack surface; CSP blocks unauthorized script injection
security.privacy_controls: Cloudflare Analytics collects anonymized aggregate data; no cookies set by the site; no third-party tracking scripts loaded

data.entities: documentation_pages (MDX files in docs/), api_references (auto-generated MDX in api-reference/ from Sphinx), tutorial_guides (MDX with code examples), example_workflows (MDX with embedded workflow YAML), version_releases (Docusaurus versioned_docs/version-X.Y/ directories)
data.storage_design: No database; all content stored as MDX files in repository; auto-generated API reference MDX produced in CI and stored as build artifact
data.analytics_events: Cloudflare Analytics default: page_view by URL, country, device type, referrer. No custom events collected.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloudflare Pages project connected to GitHub repository; builds triggered on main branch push and on tagged releases; branch previews auto-created per PR
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions workflow: on PR - run Sphinx autodoc to generate API reference MDX, run docusaurus build, run Lighthouse CI against preview URL; on tag push - run docusaurus docs:version to snapshot versioned docs, build and push to Cloudflare Pages
devops.environments: Cloudflare Pages branch previews per PR with unique URLs, production deployment on main branch merge, versioned doc snapshots on git tag push

backend.service_design: No backend service. CI pipeline step (scripts/generate-api-docs.py) runs Sphinx autodoc against framework Python source, converts RST output to MDX via pandoc, writes MDX files to api-reference/ directory for inclusion in Docusaurus build.
backend.api_patterns: No API. Static site consumes pre-built MDX content only.

ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned version switcher but did not describe how Docusaurus versioned_docs directories are created and managed on each tagged release in the CI pipeline.

target_output.title: Scientific Workflow Framework Documentation Site Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A Docusaurus 3 static documentation site for an open-source scientific workflow management framework, hosted on Cloudflare Pages with global edge delivery. API reference documentation is auto-generated from Python source code docstrings at CI time using Sphinx autodoc with the sphinx-docusaurus plugin producing MDX output directly. Tagged releases automatically trigger the docusaurus docs:version versioning workflow, creating snapshots in versioned_docs/ for the version switcher. Tutorial guides, example workflow galleries, and community contribution guides are authored as MDX files in the repository. Cloudflare Analytics provides privacy-preserving page view tracking with no cookies.
target_output.architecture_overview: Docusaurus 3 static site with three content sources: hand-authored MDX files in docs/ for tutorials and guides, auto-generated MDX in api-reference/ produced by the CI scripts/generate-api-docs.py step, and versioned snapshots in versioned_docs/version-X.Y/ directories created on each tagged release. GitHub Actions builds on main branch push and on git tag patterns matching v*.*.*. Tagged release builds run the additional docusaurus docs:version step to snapshot current docs before Cloudflare Pages deployment. Cloudflare Pages serves the built static HTML/CSS/JS from its global edge network. No runtime server, no database, no authentication. CSP headers served via _headers file block all external script sources except Cloudflare Analytics CDN.
target_output.technology_stack: Framework: Docusaurus 3.x with TypeScript configuration. API doc generation: Sphinx 7 with sphinx-docusaurus plugin outputting MDX directly from Python docstrings. Content authoring: MDX with code highlighting via Prism. Search: Docusaurus built-in local search with lunr.js for MVP; Algolia DocSearch planned for Phase 2. Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics (no cookies, no consent required). Hosting: Cloudflare Pages free tier. CI: GitHub Actions with Node 20 and Python 3.12 in build matrix. Quality gate: Lighthouse CI with performance threshold 90.
target_output.functional_feature_map: API Reference Section: auto-generated MDX pages per Python module with function signatures, parameter types, return types, and docstring descriptions extracted by Sphinx autodoc; cross-links between related functions. Tutorial Guides: Getting Started (5-minute quick-start workflow), Core Concepts (workflow DAG model, step definitions, executors), Advanced Patterns (conditional branching, dynamic parallelism, checkpoint recovery). Example Workflow Gallery: MDX pages embedding annotated workflow YAML with downloadable single-file examples for bioinformatics, genomics, proteomics, and image analysis use cases. Version Switcher: Docusaurus built-in version dropdown selecting between last 3 release snapshot directories. Community Contribution Guide: fork-to-merge workflow, docstring style requirements, test coverage expectations, release process description.
target_output.system_components: Docusaurus 3 build (npx docusaurus build): processes docs/, api-reference/, src/pages/, and blog/ MDX files into static HTML with React hydration for interactive components; generates sitemap.xml and search index; code-splits per documentation page for fast initial load. scripts/generate-api-docs.py: invokes Sphinx autodoc on framework Python source directory, sphinx-docusaurus plugin writes one MDX file per public module to docs/api-reference/ with module-level navigation metadata front matter; validates docstring coverage above 80% threshold or exits non-zero. docusaurus docs:version {version}: run on tagged release CI step, reads current docs/ content, writes snapshot to versioned_docs/version-{version}/, appends entry to versions.json for version switcher rendering. Cloudflare Pages CDN: serves built static assets from global edge with automatic HTTPS and HTTP/2. _headers file: defines Content-Security-Policy disallowing inline scripts, restricting script-src to self and static.cloudflareinsights.com for analytics.
target_output.workflows: API Reference Generation (CI on every push): GitHub Actions checks out repository including framework Python source submodule; installs Python 3.12 and sphinx with sphinx-docusaurus plugin; runs python scripts/generate-api-docs.py which invokes sphinx-build -b docusaurus framework/src/ docs/api-reference/; script asserts docstring coverage above 80% by parsing Sphinx coverage report, exits 1 if threshold missed; generated MDX files committed to working directory for Docusaurus build. Version Release Workflow (CI on git tag v*.*.*): GitHub Actions extracts version string from tag (e.g., v2.3.1 becomes 2.3.1); installs Node 20 and runs npm ci; runs npx docusaurus docs:version 2.3.1 which snapshots current docs/ into versioned_docs/version-2.3.1/ and updates versions.json; runs npm run build to produce full static site including new version; deploys to Cloudflare Pages via wrangler pages publish out/; new version appears in version switcher dropdown for all site visitors.
target_output.data_model: No database. Content entities are files in the repository: documentation_pages (docs/**/*.mdx authored by maintainers and contributors), api_references (docs/api-reference/**/*.mdx auto-generated from Python source docstrings by CI), tutorial_guides (docs/tutorials/**/*.mdx with embedded code examples), example_workflows (docs/examples/**/*.mdx embedding workflow YAML), version_releases (versioned_docs/version-X.Y/ directories created by docusaurus docs:version on each tagged release). Metadata per MDX file: front matter with id, title, sidebar_label, and sidebar_position for Docusaurus navigation generation. versions.json: JSON array of version strings used by Docusaurus to render version switcher.
target_output.api_design: No API. Static site only. External integrations consumed: GitHub repository (source for CI clone), framework Python source code (Sphinx autodoc input), Cloudflare Pages (deployment target via wrangler CLI in CI), Cloudflare Web Analytics beacon (loaded client-side from static.cloudflareinsights.com). No server-side endpoints. Docusaurus generates a static JSON search index at /search-index.json consumed by the local lunr search plugin.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Content Security Policy via _headers file: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://static.cloudflareinsights.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:; connect-src 'self' https://cloudflareinsights.com. X-Frame-Options: DENY. X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff. Referrer-Policy: strict-origin. HTTPS enforced by Cloudflare Pages with automatic certificate provisioning and HSTS preload. No user input forms means no XSS injection surface. No server-side secrets in repository. No personal data collected means no GDPR, CCPA, or cookie consent obligations. Open source content published under MIT license; framework source docstrings used for API reference generation under same license.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloudflare Pages project connected to GitHub repository main branch. Build command: npm run build (which invokes generate-api-docs.py pre-build step then npx docusaurus build). Output directory: build/. Node version: 20.x pinned in .nvmrc. GitHub Actions PR workflow: install Node and Python, run generate-api-docs.py, run npm run build, run Lighthouse CI against Cloudflare Pages preview URL asserting performance score above 90, accessibility above 95, and best practices above 90. GitHub Actions tagged release workflow: extract version from git tag, run npx docusaurus docs:version to snapshot, run npm run build, deploy to Cloudflare Pages via wrangler pages publish, tag the deployed URL in GitHub release notes. Rollback: Cloudflare Pages dashboard one-click rollback to any prior deployment; all deployments retained indefinitely on free tier.
target_output.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics dashboard: page views by day, top documentation pages visited, traffic sources (GitHub, search engines, direct), geographic distribution of visitors. GitHub Actions build duration tracked in CI logs; Slack webhook notification on build failure via GitHub Actions job notification. Lighthouse CI score history tracked in PR comments via lighthouse-ci GitHub Action summary; regressions in performance or accessibility below threshold fail the PR check. No application-level error monitoring needed for static site; 404s tracked by Cloudflare Analytics for broken link detection.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cost: Cloudflare Pages free tier provides unlimited bandwidth for open source projects, unlimited deployments, and unlimited branch previews. GitHub Actions free tier provides 2000 minutes/month for public repositories which is sufficient for documentation builds averaging 3 minutes each. Docusaurus build time scales with content volume; at projected 500 documentation pages and 200 API reference pages the 4-minute build constraint is met. Cloudflare edge CDN auto-scales globally with no configuration.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): Docusaurus 3 project setup with TypeScript configuration and framework branding, scripts/generate-api-docs.py Sphinx autodoc pipeline with sphinx-docusaurus plugin, tutorial section Getting Started and Core Concepts guides, versioned release workflow with docusaurus docs:version on CI tag push, Cloudflare Pages deployment with CSP headers, Lighthouse CI gate in PR workflow, docstring coverage check gate. Phase 2 (3 weeks): example workflow gallery with embedded annotated YAML, Advanced Patterns tutorial section, community contribution guide with fork-to-PR workflow, version-2 and version-3 legacy doc snapshots migrated from existing markdown. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Algolia DocSearch integration replacing local lunr search for better cross-version search results, interactive workflow playground embedding a minimal workflow executor in the tutorial pages, localization scaffolding for Chinese scientific community.
target_output.development_guidelines: Docstring coverage minimum 80% enforced as CI gate on generate-api-docs.py step; build fails if coverage drops below threshold. All new API reference content comes from source docstrings, never from hand-authored MDX. Tutorial MDX files must include a tested code example; untested code in tutorials is treated as a documentation bug. PR descriptions must link to the specific documentation page being improved or the GitHub issue for new content. Version snapshots created automatically on tagged releases; maintainers must not manually edit versioned_docs/ directories. Lighthouse CI score regressions on any metric block PR merge.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Sphinx autodoc fails on undocumented private functions included in public module namespace, polluting generated API reference with internal implementation details; mitigation: configure Sphinx autodoc with members=True and private-members=False, and add undoc-members=False to exclude public functions lacking docstrings from reference output. Risk: Framework source docstring quality drops below coverage threshold breaking documentation CI; mitigation: 80% threshold with clear error message in CI output naming uncovered functions. Tradeoff: sphinx-docusaurus plugin over pandoc RST-to-MDX conversion chosen because sphinx-docusaurus produces cleaner MDX with proper front matter and sidebar metadata, at cost of one additional Sphinx plugin dependency. Tradeoff: Cloudflare Analytics over Plausible chosen because Cloudflare Analytics is included free with Cloudflare Pages and requires zero configuration.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: sphinx-docusaurus plugin confirmed compatible with Sphinx 7 and Docusaurus 3 by maintainers. Framework Python source confirmed as single-repository submodule accessible in CI. Algolia DocSearch free tier for open source confirmed as available via docusaurus.io partnership; deferred to Phase 3 to avoid dependency on Algolia approval timeline. Cloudflare Pages confirmed to support Node 20 build environment.

metadata.notes: Round 2 revision resolving API doc generation pipeline and versioned release workflow gaps. Docusaurus 3 on Cloudflare Pages for open-source scientific framework documentation.
